name: Registered Student Organizations
description:
The Center for Student Engagement applies a student centered approach for students to develop and clarify identity, to understand others, and to promote social change. Student Engagement provides avenues for student learning and global awareness to nurture the development of responsible, global citizens and inclusive leaders who are committed to building community in the intricate, multi-layered world. Georgia Tech students achieve success by engaging in meaningful spaces to build connections, apply purpose, and develop mutually-beneficial partnerships.

Student Engagement
404-894-3458
2211 Student Center Commons
350 Ferst Drive Atlanta, GA 30332-0285
Fall & Spring Hours: Mon -Thurs: 8:00 am - 6:00 pm; Fri: 8:00am-5:00pm
Summer Hours: Mon - Fri: 8:00 am – 5:00 pm
summary: CSE assists GT students identify, create, and grow engagement opportunities in our community. Our four areas are Civic Engagement, Fraternity and Sorority Life, Student Media, and Student Organizations.

name: Academic Enrichment Programs
description: A true education isn’t just about earning a degree – it’s about self-discovery and cultivating an intellectual identity. Academic Engagement Program fosters growth by giving students the opportunity to engage with one another and faculty in research and innovation programs, summer session initiatives, living-learning communities, and other endeavors in which they can share ideas, explore possibilities, and take initiative. We encourage you to take advantage of these opportunities to make the most of your academic experience at Tech.
summary: Academic Engagement Program fosters growth by giving students the opportunity to engage with one another and faculty in research and innovation programs, summer session initiatives, and living-learning communities.

name: Fraternity and Sorority Life
description:
The Office of Fraternity & Sorority Life will define the fraternal experience for the next generation.

  • Holistic Development: Our advising focuses on a holistic perspective which is concerned with the development of every person's intellectual, emotional, social, physical, artistic, creative and spiritual potentials.
  • Tradition: We believe in the values each organization's founders determined were the basis for their brotherhood and sisterhood. We challenge each member to hold themselves and their brothers and sisters to these values each day.
  • Collaboration: We believe in the importance of campus and community collaboration and work at establishing strong partnerships with all that have a stake in the success of fraternity and sorority life at Tech.
  • Equity: We approach our work from a fair and impartial standpoint and advocate for the same for every member of our community.
  • Empowerment: We believe the fraternity and sorority experience creates the opportunity for each member to become stronger and more confident in their dealings with everyday life.
summary: The Fraternity & Sorority Life department advocates for and facilitates the education and development of our community necessary to achieve a safe, innovative and positive fraternal experience.

name: Campus Departments
summary: Member organizations of the Campus Departments umbrella in Engage are all those offices, departments, and programs utilizing Engage to provide services and opportunities for students, faculty, staff, and community at GT.

name: Special Scholarships
description: Not just focused on awarding funds, the OSP provides developmental programming and mentoring in addition to financial merit scholarships. Through professional development activities including retreats, speakers, seminars, outdoor leadership expeditions, workshops and mentoring, scholars grow and learn in the context of a world class public and technological university.
summary: Scholar Programs (OSP) seeks to recruit the nation’s best student scholars to Georgia Tech to further enrich the composition of the student body, improve the campus, and impact their environment within a global context as students and as alumni.

name: Sandbox
description:
Welcome to the Sandbox! This is an Engage test portal. 

https://youtu.be/lUJCoGoyGaI
summary: Welcome to the Sandbox! This is an Engage test portal.

name: Buzz Studios Filmmaking Club
description:
Buzz Studios is a filmmaking club in which members work together to create short films written, produced, directed, and edited by students. We shoot around four projects a semester, including student shorts, commercial and club projects, and more! No prior experience is required, and anyone can join, pitch scripts, help out on set, and create films with us.

View the 2024 Sizzle Reel

Watch Our Films Here
summary: Buzz Studios is Georgia Tech's premier, award-winning, and longest-running filmmaking club helping to develop student shorts from script to screen since 2001.

name: Multicultural Panhellenic Council
description: The Multicultural Panhellenic Council is one of the four Greek Councils at Georgia Tech. Our diverse Greek sororities and fraternities foster and promote unity, campus awareness, and community service. Our member organizations are open to students from all backgrounds, religions, and creeds.
summary: The Multicultural Panhellenic Council is one of Georgia Tech's four Greek councils. Our sororities and fraternities foster and promote unity, campus awareness, and community service. Our member organizations are open to students from all backgrounds.

name: Student Center Programs Council
description: The Student Center Programs Council is a student-led programming community of over 90 members that brings more than 50 exciting programs to campus each year. These programs can be entertaining, thought-provoking, educational, energizing, or anything in between-- but most of all they are FUN! Don't miss our signature events throughout the year such as Georgia Tech Night at Six Flags, Sting Break, Midnight Breakfast, Homecoming Week, and more!
summary: The Student Center Programs Council is a student-led programming community that brings more than 50 exciting programs to campus each year. Some of our programs include Sting Break, GT Night at the Aquarium, Homecoming Week, and Midnight Breakfast.

organizationType.name: Division of Student Engagement and Well-being

name: Sai Young Adults
description:
SAI (Spirituality, Awareness, Interfaith) Young Adults at Georgia Tech (SYA@GT) provides a forum for spiritual discussion and a respect for all traditions. People from all faiths and all walks of life are welcome to attend, as we encourage a sense of "Unity in Diversity". Sai Baba devotees, and those interested in learning about Sai Baba are especially encouraged to attend. Membership is free and open to all who have an open mind and have an interest. Weekly meetings involve spiritual discussion (Satsang), meditation, and devotional singing (Bhajans, a.k.a. Kirtan). Examples of other activities include various service opportunities and semesterly retreats.

Meeting Time & Location:
Thursdays 7 PM - 8 PM, Skiles 257
summary: SAI (Spirituality, Awareness, Interfaith) Young Adults at Georgia Tech (SYA@GT) provides a forum for spiritual discussion and a respect for all traditions. People from all faiths and all walks of life are welcome to attend, as we encourage a sense of "...

name: Alpha Gamma Delta
description: Alpha Gamma Delta is a group of passionate and involved women who aim to positively contribute to Tech's community through philanthropy, leadership, inclusivity, and personal growth.
summary: Alpha Gamma Delta is a group of passionate and involved women who aim to positively contribute to Tech's community through philanthropy, leadership, inclusivity, and personal growth.

name: Alternative Service Breaks
description: Alternative Service Breaks hosts group-oriented community service trips during Institute Breaks (i.e. fall, winter, spring, and summer breaks) and virtual service-learning experiences during the semester. Trips include student trip leaders, staff advisors, and student participants and all trips are alcohol and drug free. The trip locations vary from domestic to international locations and bring a diverse group of Tech students together to address service issues including, but not limited to, affordable housing, early childhood education, hunger/food insecurity, disaster relief, and environmental sustainability. We are always looking for participants who want to be part of something bigger in this world and have a desire to create a smaller community within the "Tech bubble". ASB is a great place for participants to maintain a platform through which they can develop as leaders, learn about surrounding communities and social issues through education and reflection, and tackle issues through service.
summary: Our mission is to educate and empower Georgia Tech students and staff by providing quality service-learning trips that engage global communities in need

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description: Alpha Kappa Psi- Epsilon Sigma (AKΨ) is Georgia Tech's Premier Co-Ed Professional Fraternity. We work to bring together Tech's highest achieving, business-minded students to foster a community of growth and success. With over 80 members spanning all majors, all disciplines, and all cultures at Tech, Alpha Kappa Psi consistently ranks among the highest achieving organizations, in academics, job opportunities, and campus involvement. Boasting active student members who go above and beyond in academics, service and professional opportunities alike with job placements at companies like Goldman Sachs, Google, Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, Delta, Tesla, Coca-Cola, PwC, McKinsey & Company, Accenture, Deloitte, KPMG, Capital One, Alpha Kappa Psi serves as Atlanta's hearth of student success and professional leadership, fostering tomorrow's leaders with a niche, real-world professional edge.
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi (AKΨ) is Georgia Tech's Premier Co-Ed Professional Fraternity. With a group of 60+ student members, we work to bring together Tech's highest achieving, business-minded students to foster a community of growth and success.

name: Cycling
description: Georgia Tech Cycling is the premier road and mountain biking organization on campus. We compete in every cycling discipline including mtb, road, CX and track and are open to riders of all ability. Vist https://gtcycling.com/join/ for more info, reach out to gatechcycling@gmail.com with any questions.
summary: Georgia Tech Cycling is the premier road and mountain biking organization on campus. We compete in every cycling discipline including mtb, road, CX and track and are open to riders of all ability. Vist https://gtcycling.com/join/ for more info.

name: RoboJackets
description:
RoboJackets is a student-run organization dedicated to the promotion, education, and advancement of the field of robotics through competition and outreach initiatives. Our five competition teams combine the talents of students from a spectrum of majors to represent Georgia Tech at competitions around the world. 

JOIN US: Visit our website, https://robojackets.org/join/, to learn more about joining!
All communications happen on our SLACK.
summary: RoboJackets is a student-run organization dedicated to the promotion, education, and advancement of the field of robotics through competition and outreach initiatives. Our five competition teams combine the talents of students from a spectrum of majors.

name: Acoustical Society of America
description: The purpose of the Georgia Tech Chapter of the Acoustical Society of America is to increase and propagate the knowledge of acoustics and promote its practical applications. The ASA chapter at GT aims to host monthly seminars in the topic areas of acoustics and dynamics to assist students in academic seminar requirements.
summary: The purpose of the Georgia Tech Chapter of the Acoustical Society of America is to increase and diffuse the knowledge of acoustics and promote its practical applications.

name: Parents Board
description: The Georgia Tech Parents Board works to actively advance the mission of Parent & Family Programs, the Division of Student Life and the Institute. Board members attend two meetings per year (early fall and late spring) and serve as special advisors to John Stein, Vice President for Student Life and Dean of Students, to provide input on student services, events, programming, funding priorities. The Georgia Tech Parents Board acts as the voice of all Georgia Tech parents and families and serves as representatives at on-campus and regional events.
summary: The Georgia Tech Parents Board works to actively advance the mission of Parent & Family Programs, the Division of Student Life and the Institute. Board members attend two meetings per year (early fall and late spring) and serve as special advisors to J...

name: Muslim Students Association
description: We are dedicated to fostering friendship and goodwill between people of all faith. We strive to further the understanding and appreciation of Islam as a beautiful way of life among the GT and Atlanta community, as well as provide a community on campus for Muslim students, faculty, and staff.
summary: We are dedicated to fostering friendship and goodwill between people of all faith. We also strive to further the understanding and appreciation of Islam as a beautiful way of life among the GT and Atlanta community, as well as provide a community on ca...

name: Club Math
description:
Hey! Welcome to Club Math! We're a social club united by a common interest in math! Join us weekly to hang out, solve problems, work on homework, or get to know other math enthusiasts at Tech. We may also have special meetings like movie nights, craft days, or game nights!

This semester, Spring 2026, we meet Wednesdays 6:30-7:30 PM in Skiles 006. There is a GT Math Discord server; please find the invite link for that from the Georgia Tech Student Hub. Join our GroupMe at https://groupme.com/join_group/52872639/jSViCC4b for updated meeting information.

Hope to see you there!
summary: Hey! Welcome to Club Math! We're a social club united by a common interest in math! Come join us weekly to hang out, solve problems, work on homework, or just get to know other math enthusiasts at Tech.

name: Latter-day Saint Student Association
description: Our group represents members of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (Mormons) on campus. Our events include weekly game nights, scripture studies, and church. We are here to help any new students who are members of the Church or interested in learning more about our church, including carpools to church every Sunday.
summary: Our group represents members of and people interested in the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (Mormons) on campus. Our events include weekly game nights, scripture studies, and church. Any and all are welcome!

name: Alpha Chi Omega
description:
Alpha Chi Omega was founded in 1885 and Georgia Tech’s Epsilon Phi chapter was founded in 1975. Ever since, we have developed and maintained a tight-knit community of sisters who lift each other up in all aspects of life. From going on study adventures around our beloved city of Atlanta to planting trees for campus beautification projects, our members look for any chance to come together and
better our world.
summary: Alpha Chi Omega is a National Social Sorority founded in 1885. At Georgia Tech we pride ourselves on being a dynamic group of women – we have sisters from all over the country, involved in numerous organizations both on and off campus.

name: Club Tennis
description:
GT Club Tennis is a competitive team comprised of players with experience ranging from high school to NCAA Division I. The season is comprised of weekly practices and competitions at regional, sectional, and national tournaments organized by USTA. Our goal this upcoming year is to earn a bid to the Tennis on Campus Nationals in the spring, and to defend our title at Sectionals! Social and team bonding events such as team dinners, volunteering, and intramural sports are also an integral part of club tennis that creates a fun environment both on and off the court.  

Tryouts are held during the second week of every Fall and Spring semester. This Fall's tryouts will be held from August 25th-28th at Ken Byers Tennis Complex from 6-8pm! Please sign up using this form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfuZUMCle9C9S5bbCIamO-RUvEvbU4EFijt7UFIvCEm_UIz0g/viewform?usp=header

For more information about the club or tryouts, check out our website https://sites.google.com/view/gtclubtennis/home, our Instagram @gtclubtennis, or email techtennisclub@gmail.com!
summary: GT Club Tennis is a competitive team comprised of players with experience ranging from high school to NCAA Division I. The season consists of weekly practices and competitions at regional, sectional, and national tournaments organized by USTA.

name: T-Book
description:
The Georgia Tech T-book was originally published by the Georgia Tech YMCA with the intention of providing a guide to the new Tech students about traditions, campus life and history. This original T-book was published for more than 65 years and stopped in 1970 due to lack of funding and the lack of partnership between Georgia Tech and its YMCA.

The T-book was therefore not able to provide freshman with a helping hand until it was revived by the Student publications Board in 1997 as a website that provided an overview of Georgia Tech traditions and history. The website soon grew out of date, and in 2008 Ramblin’ Reck Club decided to bring the T-book back in its original form. Since that entering class, every entering freshman has been distributed a T-book at freshman convocation. In addition to convocation, Reck Club distributes T-books to freshman dorm PL’s as well as to GT 1000 TL’s in an attempt to allow every first year to have access to the handbook.

The T-book in its modern form has sections which provide an overview of the Ramblin’ Reck, GT history, traditions, Alumni, Fans and Spirit and Athletics. Finally the T-book has been designed to be a resource to entering RATS by printing the guidelines to filling out RAT caps, the fight songs and a copy of the old RAT rules for those who want to participate.
summary: The Georgia Tech T-Book was originally published by the Georgia Tech YMCA with the intention of providing a guide to the new Tech students about traditions, campus life and history. Today, the T-Book is a Student Publication handed out at convocation.

name: American Nuclear Society
description: The Georgia Tech student chapter of the American Nuclear Society supports the mission and vision of ANS and hosts a variety of activities, guest speakers, tour trips, and outreach events to increase general understanding of nuclear-related industries and concepts while also providing useful insight and experience to students both within and outside of our chapter.
summary: The Georgia Tech student chapter of the American Nuclear Society supports the mission and vision of ANS and hosts a variety of activities, guest speakers, tour trips, and outreach events to increase general understanding of nuclear-related industries.

name: The Diabetes Link
summary:
Serve, empower, and advocate for the diabetic community on and off campus as a chapter of the Diabetes Link, a non-profit organization whose mission is to improve the lives of young adults living with Type 1 Diabetes. 
**Formerly known as CDN@GT**

name: Men's Club Soccer
description: The Georgia Tech Men's Club Soccer team is a student organization whose focus is competitive soccer at the collegiate level. There is not currently an NCAA sanctioned varsity team here at Georgia Tech, but we hope that through our success we will one day become exactly that. If you are an experienced player from the youth leagues, high school, or just a talented individual this is the place to play as a representative of Georgia Tech against other colleges around the South East. The Georgia Tech Men's Club Soccer team is a member of the Southeastern Collegiate Soccer League (SCSL) and competes against other Division I schools such as: Georgia, Tennessee, Clemson, Georgia Southern, University of Central Florida, Alabama, and Florida State.
summary: The Georgia Tech Men's Club Soccer team is a student organization whose focus is competitive soccer at the collegiate level. There is not currently an NCAA sanctioned varsity team here at Georgia Tech, but we hope that through our success we will one d...

name: Sigma Beta Rho Fraternity Inc.
description: Sigma Beta Rho has grown considerably since its inception. It has expanded from its roots on the East Coast to more than forty recognized chapters and colonies that span the breadth of the nation. This growth stems from a reputation for excellent, values-based programming, diversity amongst our brotherhood, and alignment with the ideals of many students who strive for a competitive edge before entering a post-globalized working world. As an organization that prides itself on having individuals from every continent, ethnicity, and religion, we seek to foster enriching, cross-cultural discourse both amongst our members and within our campus communities. Through the efforts of our brothers, we have become the largest, most expansive fraternity of our kind in twenty years. With each passing day, we strive to take one more step towards realizing the nationwide network envisioned by our National Founding Fathers.
summary: Sigma Beta Rho has grown considerably since its inception. It has expanded from its roots on the East Coast to more than forty recognized chapters and colonies that span the breadth of the nation. This growth stems from a reputation for excellent, valu...

name: Student Construction Association
description: The purpose of SCA is to enhance the experience of students interested in the construction field by exposure to industry professionals, Georgia Tech faculty, technologies, and topics in practice, provide more avenues for students to enjoy construction outside of the classroom, provide a community for students interested in the construction industry, and provide resources and knowledge of upcoming networking and career fair services.
summary: Student Construction Association (SCA) brings students across the campus dedicated to the construction industry.

name: Parent Assistant Leaders at Georgia Tech (PALs)
description: Parent & Family Programs created a new student leadership position in 2014 called Parent Assistant Leaders (PALs). This leadership position assists current and prospective student's family members as they become part of the Georgia Tech community. PALs serve a key role in the facilitation of ongoing programmatic efforts by Parent & Family Programs and also serve as a resource to the Division of Student Life.
summary: Parent & Family Programs created a new student leadership position in 2014 called Parent Assistant Leaders (PALs).  This leadership position assists current and prospective parents and family members as they become part of the Georgia Tech Family.  PAL...

organizationType.name: Division of Student Engagement and Well-being

name: African American Student Union
description: The African American Student Union has five fundamental responsibilities: to provide a voice for Black students at the Georgia Institute of  Technology, to promote social and cultural awareness in the Georgia Tech community, to improve and maintain relationships between African-American students and students of other ethnic backgrounds, to provide a social and cultural outlet for its members, and to take an active part in the activities of the Institute. We go about attending to these responsibilities within the framework set fourth by our Eight Pillars of Unity: Self Cultural Awareness, Effective Leadership, Community Service, Scholarship, Coalition Building, Social Awareness, Political Awareness, and Economic Empowerment. When we say "Redefining your Black Experience", we intend to set the standard of AASU bringing together all Black students by focusing on two pillars in particular- Coalition Building and Social Awareness. We plan on spreading the joy of AASU by uniting, uplifting, and transforming not just the Georgia Tech community, but the Atlanta community as well.
summary: The African American Student Union is the oldest and largest Black student organization on campus. Founded in 1968 after the assassination of the late Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., AASU has worked to prove a social and cultural outlet for 54 years

name: DanceTech
description: DanceTech was founded in 2003 as Georgia Tech's leading multi-genre dance organization. DanceTech offers members the chance to perform on and off campus in events throughout the year. Members come from various dance backgrounds, but our performances are mainly in lyrical, contemporary, jazz, and ballet. We host in-person auditions at the beginning of every semester. For audition announcements and to see past performance videos, please check our Instagram @gtdancetech.
summary: DanceTech is an organization that allows dancers to continue to pursue their passion for dance in college. We perform in showcases every semester and practice weekly.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
description:
Delta Sigma Pi is a professional fraternity organized to foster the study of business in universities; to encourage scholarship, social activity and the association of students for their mutual advancement by research and practice; to promote closer affiliation between the commercial world and students of commerce, and to further a higher standard of commercial ethics and culture and the civic and commercial welfare of the community.

Delta Sigma Pi is exclusive to business, economics majors and industrial engineers.
summary: Delta Sigma Pi is a professional fraternity organized to foster the study of business in universities.

name: Omicron Delta Kappa Honor Society
description:
ODK Application for 2025-2026

 

Overview

ODK is a very prestigious organization that usually only has the top 3-5% of campus leaders involved as members of ODK across the country. We are Georgia Tech’s top premier leadership honor society, focused on developing well-rounded leaders who excel in scholarship, service, character, and collaboration. We believe that impactful leadership doesn’t stop at academics—it includes how students show up for their communities and grow into changemakers beyond college. ODK brings together top student leaders, faculty, and staff to share ideas, foster meaningful dialogue, and support one another’s development as contributors to a better, more connected society. 

 

Mission Statement

As Georgia Tech’s official Circle of Omicron Delta Kappa, we strive to recognize and elevate student leaders across all disciplines. Through collaboration, reflection, and campus engagement, we use our collective experience to act as a catalyst for growth, innovation, and service at Georgia Tech and beyond.

 

Primary Events & Initiatives

Internal

  • ODK Opinions

  • Deek Duos

  • ODK Socials

External

  • Talks@Tech (public event)

  • Ramble In (campus-wide, semiannual event)

  • Freshman Leadership Awards (for incoming students)

  • Annual ODK Leadership Conference (amazing for networking with top students & faculty from across the country)

 

ODK × Georgia Tech’s Motto of Progress & Service

ODK advances Progress by creating open dialogue between students and administration to address real campus challenges and propose impactful solutions.

We live out Service by supporting student-led initiatives, spotlighting leaders across campus, and welcoming incoming students with events like Ramble In. Through platforms like ODK Opinions, we bring together diverse voices to shape a better Georgia Tech experience for all.

 

Member Impact Anecdotes

“I first interacted with ODK by attending the ODK Leadership Conference as a first-year. The workshops and talks by industry professionals showed me how real-world leadership is built through connection and humility. Since then, ODK has helped me grow as a team player and communicator—skills I now use daily in my engineering internship.”

~ Emily Hale, AE, Class of 2020

 

“While in ODK, I gained a deeper understanding of how Tech’s administration approaches complex issues like mental health. Organizing Talks@Tech with Dean McLaughlin opened my eyes to the systemic challenges we face—and how students and administrators can work together to shift the culture. ODK helped me become a more empathetic and intentional leader.”

~ Michael Xiao, CS, Class of 2020
summary: Omicron Delta Kappa is a national leadership honor society recognizing top collegiate leaders in academics, service, and character. ODK empowers members through networking, personal growth, and leadership development across all disciplines.

name: Men's Club Lacrosse
description: Men's Lacrosse is a club sport at Georgia Tech. We are in Division I of the Men’s Collegiate Lacrosse Association (MCLA) and compete in the SouthEast Lacrosse Conference (SELC). We compete in our conference to achieve a spot in the national championships hosted by the MCLA. Our tournament is televised via the internet and cable. With this being said we run our program like an NCAA varsity sport.
summary: Men's Lacrosse is a club sport at Georgia Tech. We are in Division I of the Men’sCollegiate Lacrosse Association (MCLA) and compete in the SouthEast LacrosseConference (SELC). We compete in our conference to achieve a spot in the nationalchampionships ...

name: Economics Club
description:
Do you wonder why the stock market fluctuates right before the Fed makes the announcement on the interest rates? Or are you curious about the idea of self-regulation or socially responsible corporate strategies? Or do you wonder how multinational corporations navigate the waters of globalization? Whether you are an ECON major or someone who is has an innate interest in such things, come and join our club to meet and talk about all these complex and amazing phenomena.

Get to know your fellow jackets who breath all things market innovations, disruption, stock fluctuations, trade negotiations! Learn where you can apply the skills you hone in your ECON courses and how to find those job opportunities! You voice your interests and we’ll provide the experts! Best of all, socialize, network, and meet new people in your university community!

Check out our website and join us here in Engage!
summary: Economics is the driving force behind your daily decisions. If you’ve ever weighed the pros and cons of buying that new iPad, or wanted to know what the big deal about recycling is, you’ve come to the right place. Here you can talk about your opinions!

name: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc
description: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Incorporated was founded January 9, 1914 on the principles of Brotherhood, Scholarship, and Service with an underlying belief that a prospective member should be judged by his own merits rather than his family background or affluence. The founders wished and wanted their fraternity to exist as part of even a greater brotherhood which would be devoted to the "inclusive we" rather than the "exclusive we". The Nu Beta Chapter of Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc. was founded on May 11, 1981 on the campus of the Georgia Institute of Technology by 10 undergraduate students. Through continually embodying the fraternity's principles and initiatives such as Sigma Wellness, the American Cancer Society, and combating the AIDS/HIV Epidemic, the Nu Beta chapter has shown itself to be a vehicle for positive change not only at Georgia Tech, but also in the surrounding communities. Through the principles of Brotherhood, Scholarship, and Service, the chapter strongly focuses on leadership development, academic excellence, and community outreach. With the events and programs coordinated, Nu Beta enlightens, enriches, and enhances the lives of all individuals in the Georgia Tech community as well as the Metro Atlanta area. They pride themselves as being a fraternity of leaders, diversified in background, though unified in cause. Their main goal as a chapter is to spread the light that we gain as Sigmas to all of those around them.
summary: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Incorporated was founded January 9, 1914 on the principles of Brotherhood, Scholarship, and Service with an underlying belief that a prospective member should be judged by his own merits rather than his family background or a...

name: Buddy Jackets
description:
Mission: Buddy Jackets strives to foster a tradition of service for underprivileged children in the surrounding Atlanta community by promoting higher education through year-round mentorship.

 

Buddy Jackets is an organization at Georgia Tech that works with the John H. Harland Boys and Girls Club to strengthen community bonds and bring GT students and underprivileged children in the area together through year-round volunteering and events. Our fall event, "Tech the Halls," allows students to serve as "mentors" to the children and take them on a $100 shopping spree to Target along with participating in a variety of holiday-themed activities such as cookie decorating, karaoke, gift wrapping, etc. Our Spring Event, "Rec with Tech," will be a day of games, STEM activities, food, and more! Along with the major events, we have a variety of membership requirements that require mentors to volunteer several times throughout the year and attend general meetings, mentor socials, and proceeds nights.

 

Our requirements for the upcoming 2022-2023 school year can be seen below:

Participation in our organization is awarded by points. The number of points required for this semester are still being discussed. In order to allow our mentors to continue to have a strong positive impact on their community, we will be working on a pen-pal mentorship program for the Boys and Girls Club children. We will also still provide gifts for the children, whether we are able to deliver them in person or not. Other point opportunities will include monthly general meetings and mentor bonding events.

There will be dues, which is also a requirement for membership. The price is currently TBD due to the pandemic and budget cuts but typically they have been between $20-30 in the past. They cover various expenses, such as background checks, event day shirts, and food

 

If you are interested in applying to be a Buddy Jackets mentor for the 2022-2023 school year, our application is now open and you can apply here!

 

**This organization was formerly known as Tech the Halls
summary: Mission: Buddy Jackets strives to foster a tradition of service for underprivileged children in the surrounding Atlanta community by promoting higher education through year-round mentorship.

name: Alpha Chi Sigma
description: The Alpha Omega Chapter of Alpha Chi Sigma is Georgia Tech's professional co-ed chemistry fraternity. Our organization strives to build its members into professionals who will better their respective fields within the chemical sciences. We have a strong passion to give back to the community through our philanthropy and STEM outreach events, including tutoring, merit badge clinics, and fundraisers. Our Professional Events connects brothers with industry professionals to broaden their perspectives as scientists and engineers. Alpha Chi Sigma offers a unique way to meet with colleagues of similar academic backgrounds and to network with a larger community of both professors and alumni. Rush for our organization occurs each semester – https://gtaxsrush.carrd.co/, and check out our website, https://sites.google.com/view/axsgatech/home, or axs_gt on instagram to learn more!
summary: We are the Alpha Omega Chapter of Alpha Chi Sigma, Georgia Tech's professional co-ed chemistry fraternity. Our organization strives to build its members into better professionals who will augment their respective fields within the chemical sciences.

name: Musician's Network
description:
Musician's Network is the organization that has run Under the Couch, a student-run venue and lounge on campus since 1994 (currently UtC is displaced, but we are working on finding a new home!). We offer a number of services to musicians and music-lovers, including: practice room access in the Student Center, a professional recording studio, a great place to meet people with similar interests, a great place to start a band (or find that one, badly needed drummer).

We also provide equipment rentals, put on concerts, and have a free music festival every semester! We have meetings every Monday at 7pm at the Musician's Lounge outside the Practice Rooms on the 2nd floor of the Student Center. Becoming a member costs $30 a semester and comes with a free GTMN t-shirt. (Alternately, membership is free with the purchase of one $30 t-shirt). Membership gives you access to our music practice rooms, as well as discount admission to all of our shows! What kind of fantastic deal is that? For more information about any of this, come to our 7 o’clock meetings on Mondays and see what we’re all about! We also hold advertising and equipment meetings (TBD) so keep an eye out! Join our discord (where we have all club communications): https://discord.gg/3s28fBvxTg
summary: Musician's Network is the organization that runs Under the Couch (currently displaced on campus). We offer a number of services to musicians and music-lovers, such as concerts, practice spaces, lessons, equipment rentals, and semesterly music festivals.

name: TEAM Buzz
description: TEAM Buzz is a club that attempts to bring members of Georgia Tech into the greater Atlanta Community through service. TEAM Buzz strives to create a sense of community through service by bringing together many students--especially those involved in other student organizations. The club hosts a campus-wide service day every semester, where students come together to better the Atlanta community.
summary:
TEAM Buzz connects Georgia Tech to the local Atlanta community through community service. We encourage student organizations, peer groups, and individuals to join the rest of the GT community to serve together! 

Join as a member to stay updated!

name: Student Polymer Network
description:
Our mission is to enrich the experience of students interested in polymer sciences and engineering here at Georgia Tech. Because there is no centralized major dedicated to polymer science, the Student Polymer Network at Georgia Tech (SPN@GT) provides multiple opportunities to unify polymer-minded peers. These include professional development events, such as our annual Research Symposium and company info sessions, as well as social outings and numerous outreach events.

Follow us on Twitter @SPN_GT for updates or email us at spn@groups.gatech.edu!
summary:
The Student Polymer Network aims to enrich the experience of students interested in polymer sciences & engineering through social, outreach, and professional development events.

Join us at spn@groups.gatech.edu

name: Alpha Phi
description: Alpha Phi International Fraternity is more than just a Greek organization - it is a community of empowered women supporting one another for a lifetime. At Georgia Tech, our chapter focuses on sisterhood, academic excellence, philanthropy, social events, and leadership, which allows us to thrive on campus. Through an international sisterhood of more than 280,000 members, we offer women a culture of support, understanding, friendship and celebration.
summary: Alpha Phi at Georgia Tech is a lifelong community of empowered women built on sisterhood, leadership, academics, philanthropy, and connection. As part of an international network of 280,000 members, we foster a culture of support, friendship, and growth.

name: Bridge Club
description:
The Bridge Club at Georgia Tech welcomes all students, faculty, staff, and community members who are interested in the game of bridge. Bridge is a fun and strategic game for four players (two teams of two) using a standard deck of playing cards.

We are a teaching club and everyone is welcome, even if you’ve never played bridge before. Our meetings are casual and focus on playing the game socially. We also play in club games and tournaments in the Atlanta area, and compete in national tournaments against other college bridge clubs.
summary: The Georgia Tech Bridge Club teaches, plays, and promotes the card game of bridge.

name: Sailing Club
description:
Georgia Tech Sailing Club Est. 1961

All Skill levels welcome!

Join our Slack to join the club and answer any and all questions! We don't check/update Engage very often, so Slack is the best place to get fresh info.

Like and follow us for updates, or go to our website to learn more!

Our Website

Instagram

Facebook

Twitter
summary:
The Sailing Club is a club for anyone that wants to go sailing!

Check out our website: www.gtsailing.club

name: Off-Road
description: GT Off-Road exists to compete in the Baja SAE engineering competitions. Baja SAE is an intercollegiate engineering design competition for undergraduate and graduate engineering students with the objective of simulating real-world engineering design projects and their related challenges. Each team is competing to have its design accepted for manufacture by a fictitious firm and must design, build, test, promote and compete with a vehicle within the limits of the rules. They must also generate financial support for their project and manage their educational priorities. Each team's goal is to design and build a single-seat, all-terrain, sporting vehicle whose structure contains the driver. The vehicle should aspire to market-leading performance in terms of speed, handling, ride, and ruggedness over rough terrain and off-road conditions. Performance will be measured by success in the dynamic events which are described in the Baja SAE Rules, and are subject to event-site weather and course conditions.
summary: GT Off-Road exists to compete in the Baja SAE engineering competitions. Baja SAE is an intercollegiate engineering design competition for undergraduate and graduate engineering students with the objective of simulating real-world engineering design pro..

name: Executive Round Table
description:
Executive Round Table was founded in 1956 as an organization to promote the entrepreneurial spirit and develop outstanding leadership and character among its members. Since its founding, ERT has hosted many prominent keynote speakers, such as Christopher Klaus, former Georgia Senator Sam Nunn and motion picture director Alfred Hitchcock. Our membership includes the finest students that Georgia Tech has to offer, major industry sponsors such as Michelin, Equifax, Alvarez & Marsal, and Revenue Analytics, to name a few, and some of the best and most accomplished Georgia Tech faculty. ERT holds monthly dinner meetings, as well as an annual Student Faculty Industry conference.

We are accepting applications until January 18th @ 7PM, click the link to apply: https://forms.gle/LvuUsRYmX1aihWr97
summary: Executive Round Table was founded in 1956 as an organization to promote the entrepreneurial spirit and develop outstanding leadership and character among its members.

name: Asha for Education
description: Asha for Education is a fully volunteer run non profit organization that is involved with the education of underprivileged children in India. We at Asha GT organize fundraisers and group runs (marathon and half marathon) and participate in gameday concession stands. The entire amount raised is passed on to NGOs working at the grass roots level in India towards spreading the light of education, with zero overheads. We welcome anyone motivated towards this cause to join us and contribute towards making a better world.
summary: Asha for Education is a fully volunteer-run non-profit organization fundraising for the education of underprivileged children in India. We organize cultural and athletic events and take part in GT game-day concessions to fundraise for this cause.

name: Startup Exchange
description:
We do not do recruitment through Engage - applications to join the organizing team usually open in late October and will be announced on our social media

Startup Exchange at Georgia Tech is the place for builders at Georgia Tech to build and launch their most ambitious ideas. Exceptional Georgia Tech companies have been started by members in Startup Exchange, including Stord, Sora Schools, Ox, Glovo, and Memora Health, with a combined valuation of $4B+.

These high-value startups are disrupting their respective industries, and their success demonstrates the impact of SX on bringing the most ambitious students together. SX alumni have also received numerous accolades, such as the Forbes 30 under 30 list, the Kleiner Perkins Fellowship, the Bessemer Ventures Fellowship, and other notable awards/lists.

Past speakers have included Michael Siebel of Y Combinator, Chris Klaus, and Lara Hodgson.

SXGT is part of Startup Exchange, an intercollegiate organization for college builders, makers, and creators.

YOU DO NOT NEED TO HAVE A STARTUP IDEA TO BE PART OF STARTUP EXCHANGE!
summary: Startup Exchange at Georgia Tech is the place for builders at Georgia Tech to build and launch their most ambitious ideas.

name: Men's Water Polo Club
description: Georgia Tech Water Polo Club is a competitive club water polo team that plays in the Southeast Division of the Collegiate Water Polo Association (CWPA). The official CWPA season is in the fall; however the team also competes in the spring at various invitationals. Tournament locations range from UNC, Chapel Hill, NC to FSU, Tallahassee, FL to Tulane University, New Orleans, LA and many locations in between. The team meets for practice Mondays and Wednesdays from 7:30pm to 9:30pm, and Sundays 10:30am to 12:00 pm in the diving well at the CRC.
summary: Georgia Tech Water Polo Club is a competitive club water polo team that plays in the Southeast Division of the Collegiate Water Polo Association (CWPA). The official CWPA season is in the fall; however, we compete at other tournaments in the spring too.

name: Delta Tau Delta
description: Delta Tau Delta's motto, "Lives of Excellence", is the foundation on which we base our values of brotherhood, leadership, academics, and service. Our Chapter's legacy is evident by the connections we have with our many successful alumni, and through the significant contributions Delts have made on the Georgia Tech campus.
summary: Delta Tau Delta's motto, "Lives of Excellence", is the foundation on which we base our values of brotherhood, leadership, academics, and service. Our Chapter's legacy is evident by the connections we have with our many successful alumni, and involvement.

name: MOVE
description:
MOVE (Mobilizing Opportunities for Volunteer Experiences) is Georgia Tech's umbrella organization for a variety of community service and volunteer opportunities. MOVE’s Mission is to strive to motivate and connect the Georgia Tech community to engage in diversified service to better serve our community at large and serve as a resource for other Service Organizations on campus. You can make a difference in your community with nothing more than a smile and a desire to serve - no experience necessary or required!                                                                                                                                                                                         
Our organization consists of 4 committees - Animal Welfare, Medical Assistance, Sustainability, and Community Outreach - that address various issues within the Georgia Tech and the Greater Atlanta community.                                                              

MOVE Service Grant:

During the Fall and Spring Semesters, we actively review submissions to our "MOVE Service Grant Application." If your group, organization, etc. needs funding for a serviced based event, please apply! If you want to apply through an RSO, to find the application on Engage, go to your Manage Organization page -> Finances -> Create Request -> Create Budget Request -> Select MOVE Service Grant 2026. If you want to apply as an individual, go to User Profile -> Submissions -> Budgeting -> Create New Request -> select MOVE Service Grant Spring 2026

 

Join our GroupMe!

 

***Please note that you do not have to be a part of a MOVE committee or even a part of MOVE to participate in Service Events hosted by MOVE or MOVE's Committees.
summary: MOVE is Georgia Tech's umbrella organization for a variety of community service and volunteer opportunities. You can make a difference in your community with nothing more than a smile and a desire to serve - no experience necessary or required!

name: Ballroom Dance Club
description: The Ballroom Dance Club at Georgia Tech is dedicated to increasing participation in social ballroom dancing at Georgia Tech and in the greater Atlanta community and to representing Georgia Tech at collegiate and amateur ballroom dance competitions around the country.
summary: The Ballroom Dance Club at Georgia Tech is dedicated to increasing participation in social ballroom dancing at Georgia Tech and in the greater Atlanta community and to representing Georgia Tech at collegiate and amateur ballroom dance competitions arou...

name: Ramblin' Reck Club
description: Since its founding in 1930, Ramblin’ Reck Club has been an organization of students committed to the education and promotion of Tech spirit, history, and tradition. Members of the Club, who are from all areas of the Tech student community, work together in fulfilling the dreams of such Tech legends as Coaches Alexander and Dodd and Deans Griffin and Dull, that students can share and partake in enthusiasm for the legacy and traditions of Georgia Tech. We do so by preserving and executing traditions such as the Mini 500, Freshman Cake Race, and Ramblin' Wreck Parade, and educate new students through Traditions Night and the T-Book. Our most important responsibility is maintaining our beloved mechanical mascot the Ramblin' Reck and sharing the joy and Tech spirit it inspires with the student body and the Georgia Tech community.
summary: Ramblin' Reck Club is an organization committed to the education and promotion of Tech spirit, history and tradition as well as the caretaking of the Ramblin' Reck.

name: Band Club
description: The Georgia Tech Band Club performs the administrative and fundraising duties for the various bands on campus.
summary: The Georgia Tech Band Club performs the administrative and fundraising duties for the various bands on campus.

name: Order of Omega
description: Order of Omega is a national Honor Society for juniors and seniors (by credit hours) in the Greek community. To be considered, you must have at least a 3.65 GPA, be a member of a Panhellenic, IFC, MGC or NPHC fraternity or sorority, and be of junior or senior standing. Members are chosen based on scholastic, leadership, and involvement in the Georgia Tech and Greek Community. Order of Omega will host several events throughout the year including Round Table discussions, Guest Speakers, Philanthropy Events, Networking Events with Alumni, and general meetings. Members of Order of Omega should strive to develop their leadership abilities and professionalism during their involvement with this organization.
summary: Order of Omega is a national Honor Society for juniors and seniors in the Greek community. To be considered, you must have at least a 3.65 GPA, be a member of a Panhellenic, IFC, MGC or NPHC fraternity or sorority, and be of junior or senior standing.

name: Academic Quizbowl Team
description:
A nationals-winning collegiate level academic team that competes around the country in Quiz Bowl tournaments.

"It's like Jeopardy!, but with teams."

Join our Discord for more information!
summary: A nationals-winning collegiate level academic team that competes around the country in quizbowl tournaments.

name: Pride Alliance
description:
Overview

Pride Alliance places emphasis on community building, activism, and providing safe spaces to all in the LGBTQIA+ community. The organization continually strives for progress and the inclusion of all identities in all spaces. In a society that is becoming increasingly aware of its diverse elements, Pride Alliance provides a space for dialogue and the enhancement of understanding among the members of the Georgia Tech community concerning issues faced by gender and sexual identity minorities. 

 

Pride Alliance provides members who identify as LGBTQIA+ (lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, questioning, intersex, asexual, aromantic, and others) and their allies an organization where they can develop and cultivate a positive self-awareness. Pride Alliance has recently worked for specific LGBTQIA+ non-discrimination policy, gender inclusive housing and restroom policies, and social events for both members and outsiders. Pride Alliance is committed to serving the LGBTQIA+ people of The Georgia Institute of Technology.

 

Mission Statement

Pride Alliance strives to provide a supportive and educational environment for both members and non-members of the LGBTQIA+ community; to function as a resource for those who want and/or need information about LGBTQIA related issues; and to provide a positive LGBTQIA awareness and presence in Georgia Tech. We host a variety of fun events, informative discussions, and collaborations with other student organizations on campus.

Check out our website: pride.gatech.edu

 

Primary Events & Initiatives

 

Campus-wide Events 

  • Atlanta Pride Parade
  • PrideFest! [Coming Out Week]

Internal Events

  • Bi-Weekly General Meetings 
  • Collaborations with other RSOs
  • One-off Special Acvtivities
  • Local LGBTQIA+ Speakers
  • Weekly Newsletter with Upcoming Event Information
  • LGBTQIA+ Career Advice and Workshops 
  • oSTEM Conference [Out in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics]

 

Pride Alliance × Georgia Tech’s Motto of Progress & Service

  • Pride Alliance exhibits Progress & Service by continuing to advocate for the progression of society towards the acceptance and celebration of all in the LGBTQIA+ community. It is important to give back to our community through service trips, volunteering, and through the promotion of the myriad of resources available at Georgia Tech. In order to fulfill our mission to promote inclusion of all identities in all spaces, progress and service must be a part of our daily operations. 

 

Member Impact Anecdotes

 

"When I first came to Tech, I was still very new to the LGBT+ community (I'm a baby gay). Pride Alliance not only gave me the opportunity to meet more queer people like myself, it also helped educate me and engaged me further into the LGBT+ community. I've felt more comfortable in my sexuality since I've known about GT Pride Alliance, and the comfort and enjoyment I get from being a member encouraged me to run for an exec position! I hope to provide the same enjoyment and comfort I received to all new and existing members."

~ Jaden West, CS, Class of 2022


"Pride Alliance has been an extremely positive experience for me. As someone who didn't know any LGBTQ people before coming to Tech, Pride Alliance gave me a safe space to explore my identity and find community. I met many of my closest friends at Pride Alliance events, and I have been able to meet great role models and learn a lot from older students. Pride Alliance means a lot to me because of the comfort and fun it provided me with as a new member of the community."

~ Quincey Nielson, EnvrE, Class of 2025
summary: Pride Alliance is a Georgia Tech student organization for LGBTQIA students and their allies. We host 5 types of events: social, volunteering, education, professional development, and activism! Reach out if you have any questions or suggestions! :-)

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
description:
ASCE is an organization open to undergraduate and graduate students interested in Civil and Environmental Engineering. Students in ASCE have the opportunity to learn more about the field of civil and environmental engineering and get recruited for internship and full-time opportunities through weekly corporate lunch meetings. Other benefits of membership in ASCE include: - Engineer and design the Steel Bridge and Concrete Canoe - Network with industry professionals through weekly lunch meetings - Get exclusive access to the annual ASCE Career Fair - Connect with other CEE students at our social events! - Philanthropy and STEM outreach - Leadership opportunities. Come join us at Mason 1133 every Tuesday at 11 am!

 

To become a member, please fill out our Membership Form.
summary: ASCE is an organization open to all interested in Civil and Environmental Engineering. Students in ASCE have the opportunity to learn more about the stated industry, and make connections with students who have similar interests.

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon
description: Sigma Phi Epsilon is an outstanding fraternity that prides itself on its three cardinal principles, virtue, diligence, and brotherly love. We seek to build balanced men and improve the community around us.
summary: Fraternity

name: GT Motorsports
description:
GT Motorsports is an extracurricular, student-run organization for students across every major and department at Georgia Tech. The team provides hands-on experience by giving participants the opportunity to work on a medium-scale engineering project that has both time and budget constraints. Each year, members of the team collectively spend tens of thousands of hours designing, analyzing, building and testing a four-wheeled vehicle to compete in the annual Formula SAE (FSAE) student design series.

If you are interested in joining or would like to learn more please access gtms.gatech.edu to get a more in depth look at the team or email gtms@gatech.edu with any questions.
summary: GT Motorsports is an extracurricular, student-run organization for students across every major and department at GT with a keen interest in automotive and racing. We provide members an experiential learning environment focused on Formula style vehicles.

name: Student Government Association
description: The Georgia Tech Student Government Association is a multi-branch service organization that allocates the Student Activity Fee to student organization through the Undergraduate House of Representatives (UHR) and Graduate Student Senate (GSS); manages and maintains organization charter status through the Joint Campus Organizations Committee (JCOC); upholds student integrity through the Undergraduate Judiciary Cabinet (UJC); and enriches the student experience through Executive Committees and initiatives. We solve campus problems by working with student groups, the Georgia Tech administration, and other key campus stakeholders to find areas of improvement. We advocate for student initiatives by serving on Faculty Committees, sitting on voting boards, and lobbying at the State Capitol. Our mission is to to empower student organizations, embody student opinions, preserve student integrity, and enrich the student experience.
summary: The Georgia Tech Student Government Association is a multi-branch service organization that allocates the Student Activity Fee to student organization through the Undergraduate House of Representatives (UHR) and Graduate Student Senate (GSS); manages a...

name: Graduate Student Government Association
description: The purpose of the Graduate Student Government Association is to enhance the quality of student life at Georgia Tech by providing student participation in the governance of the Institute. We are happy that you are here and hope that you are able to use the resources that we have provided for the Georgia Tech population. Please feel free to reach out to us with questions, and we look forward to hearing from you!
summary: The purpose of the Graduate Student Government Association is to enhance the quality of student life at Georgia Tech by providing student participation in the governance of the Institute. We are happy that you are here and hope that you are able to use...

name: Swim Club
description:
www.gtswimclub.com

GT Swim Club is a competitive swim team that welcomes swimmers of all abilities. We hold practices from 7:30-9:00PM at the CRC Monday-Wednesday led by our coaches. There is an additional informal practice on Thursdays 7:30-9:00 PM. We travel to approximately 2-3 meets per semester, and host/attend regionals & national championships through College Club Swimming. There is a Spring Break training trip every year where we enjoy the beach while training for Nationals. We are three-time Nationals Champions!

If you are interested in joining, please contact us (president@gtswimclub.com).
summary: www.gtswimclub.com GT Swim Club is a competitive swim team that welcomes swimmers of all abilities.  We hold practices from 7:30-9:00PM at the CRC Monday-Wednesday led by our coaches.  There is an additional informal practice on Thursdays 7:30-9:00 PM.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
description: We are SHPE, the Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers at Georgia Tech, a student organization seeking to empower Hispanics to fulfill their potential as well as teach other communities, of all races and majors, about Latin culture. We offer our members countless opportunities to develop as students, professionals, and leaders each day.
summary: SHPE empowers students, of all races and majors, to develop professionally and academically in addition to maintaining the social element of Hispanic culture. We offer our members countless opportunities to develop as students, professionals, and le.

name: Women's Club Volleyball
description:
The Georgia Tech Women’s Club Volleyball team is a nationally competitive, women’s inter-collegiate club sports team for indoor volleyball. The team participates in 3-5 tournaments per semester and travels to other colleges to compete. Ultimately, the season culminates at the NCVF Championships in the spring.

 

For Spring 24 Tryouts Informationm, follow our Instagram @gtclubvball for updates!
summary: The Georgia Tech Women’s Club Volleyball team is a nationally competitive, women’s inter-collegiate club sports team for indoor volleyball. The team participates in 3-5 tournaments per semester and travels to other colleges to compete.

name: Human Factors and Ergonomics Society at Georgia Tech
description: The Georgia Tech chapter of the Human Factors and Ergonomics Society (HFES-GT) is a group of undergraduate and graduate students interested in designing systems and interfaces based on human capabilities and limitations. Our group meets once a month during the school year in the Kendeda 230. It contributes to the human factors field in many ways, whether by educating the public about the field, networking with professionals, or working on projects for clients that need our skills.
summary: The Georgia Tech chapter of the Human Factors and Ergonomics Society (HFES-GT) is a group of undergraduate and graduate students interested in the design of systems and interfaces based on human capabilities and limitations.Our group meets once a month...

name: Aerospace Design-Build-Fly Club
description:
What we do
We design, build, and fly sleek and innovative remote-control airplanes for the annual SAE Aero Design and AIAA DBF competition. We have a legacy of success and help our members build their aircraft design skills, hands-on experience, professional development, leadership skills, and systems engineering expertise.  

We also offer a 3-Credit Class Option opportunity (AE x355). So long as you come to work sessions, you will get an A!

Meeting Times
Meetings times are TBD and will be decided as we get closer to the semester. We usually meet during the evening. 

Meeting Location
Weber 102 and 117.

Lab Tours are Live

We ask that all prospective members register for a lab tour as it helps tremendously with onboarding and team selection!

If you are interested in seeing the lab, contact us to schedule a visit!

How to Join
Contact us (rpadmanaban3@gatech.edu or mjohnson646@gatech.edu) and we will set you up with a lab tour and help you find a team that you fit with.  

Learn More
Learn more about our team through our Website (http://dbf.gatech.edu) and our Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/gatechdbf/)
summary: The team designs, builds, and flies custom remote control airplanes for the annual SAE Aero Design East and AIAA DBF competition.

name: Anime O-Tekku
description:
"There's always time to study later." - Official Club Motto Anime O-Tekku has been Georgia Tech's anime club since 1995. A group of fans put together a student organization that provided a venue in which to watch anime from Japan a couple times a month for a period of time (about 6 hours). The club grew through a huge mailing list, word-of-mouth, and Georgia Tech community support. Anime O-Tekku is now one of the oldest still-surviving anime clubs in the state, and welcomes new members from Georgia Tech and the surrounding community.

Join our discord here, where we communicate our club events and hang out with each other: https://discord.gg/n3xywpTPFm
summary: "There's always time to study later." - Official Club Motto. Anime O-Tekku has been Georgia Tech's anime club since 1995. A group of fans put together a student organization that provided a venue in which to watch anime from Japan each weekend...

name: Vertical Flight Society
description: The Vertical Flight Society at Georgia Tech or VFS - GT works to promote vertical flight learning, knowledge, and development. VFS members work in various aspects of the aerospace industry, with the GT chapter focusing on promoting research activities, conference attendance and other events related to vertical lift. Our chapter engages with the local aerospace industry and promotes STEM fields in general through outreach activities. Thanks for visiting our site, consider attending our meetings to learn more, and feel free to contact our board members. Cheers!
summary: The Vertical Flight Society at Georgia Tech or VFS - GT works to promote vertical flight learning and professional development. VFS members work in various aspects across the private sector, national labs, universities, and start-up companies.

name: Qurbani
description: Georgia Tech Qurbani, established in 2006, is a non-profit, student-run, nationally-ranked male competitive Bollywood Fusion dance team: the first and only in Atlanta! We choreograph and dance to many different kinds of styles and compete nationwide. We also love to perform for Georgia Tech campus events, and off-campus events throughout the city of Atlanta to spread our love for Indian culture. We began as a bhangra and hip-hop team and to this day both these styles have a heavy influence on our routines. Our current routines are considered more Bollywood as we now incorporate many other upbeat, high-energy forms of dance including Tollywood, Bollywood, and contemporary styles. We rely heavily on very high-energy performances along with the highest level of execution which we then couple with smooth, fun styles of dance. Whether it's the story of two best friends coming together, a casino robbery, a prison escape, Tom and Jerry, the 7 Deadly Sins, or the Minions, a Qurbani performance is much more than just a series of movements strung together - each dance tells a story. Every year, we participate in some of the most prestigious dance competitions in the country and compete against many of the best collegiate teams! We have represented Georgia Tech at nationals numerous times. Each year, we bring a liveliness and unforgettable experience to the stage!
summary: Georgia Tech Qurbani, established in 2006, is a non-profit, student run, nationally-ranked competitive Bollywood Fusion dance team: the first and only in Atlanta! We choreograph and dance to many different kinds of music and compete nationwide. We also...

name: Yellow Jacket Flying Club
description: The Yellow Jacket Flying Club is an aviation oriented organization at Georgia Tech, in Atlanta, GA. The YJFC was founded by several GT students who wanted to fly in 1945, and has a rich history of promoting aviation in the Georgia Tech community and the entire Atlanta area. The club exists to provide opportunities for students, alumni, faculty, and staff to pursue their passion for aviation. Whether it is simply hanging out with other aviation enthusiasts, or actually learning how to fly, the YJFC has something for everybody! By teaming with a local non-profit (YJFC Inc, 501(c)(3)), members of the YJFC also have the opportunity to learn to fly at a substantially lower cost, making it even easier to get a pilot's license. This non-profit has a fleet of 4 Cessna 172 aircraft that are open to all club members to fly 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year! While the YJFC has been based at several airports over the years, we currently fly out of Dekalb-Peachtree Airport (PDK) in Chamblee, GA, which is a short drive up I-85 or MARTA ride from campus. For more information about the club, check out the pages below! We are always looking to hear from club alumni about what they've done after leaving Georgia Tech. If you're a club alum, let us know!
summary: The Yellow Jacket Flying Club is an aviation oriented organization at Georgia Tech, in Atlanta, GA. The YJFC was founded by several GT students who wanted to fly in 1945, and has a rich history of promoting aviation in the Georgia Tech community.

name: Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students
description: The Georgia Tech chapter of the Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students was founded in Spring 2013 by 7 undergraduate students. Our mission is to promote the success and excellence of our students as well as the importance of diversity in the work force. We approach our mission by discovering ways to serve the needs and interests of minority students interested in the healthcare industry in fields such as medicine, dentistry, pharmacy, public health, scientific research, etc. We hope to provide Georgia Tech students with the information they need to know to become stronger medical school applicants by connecting them with medical school representatives, physicians, and medical students, all while creating a strong pre-health community on campus.
