We host weekly meetings that are scheduled depending on the members' schedules every semester. Our mission is to foster spiritual growth by hosting these meetings that consist of Bible studies, fellowship, outings, and refreshments. Please feel free to check us out!
summary: Operation Seventh-Day Adventist at Georgia Tech seeks to foster spiritual growth and develop a community for all Tech students. We host weekly meetings that are scheduled depending on the members' schedules every semester.

name: Students Organizing for Sustainability
description:
We bring sustainability-oriented people together to inspire positive change in our community by hosting various events, such as guest speakers panels and Green Meetings, and allowing students to lead change through their own projects. We also run and maintain the Georgia Tech Community Garden.

Members can get involved in the following ways:

  • Participate in some of our regularly scheduled events:
    • We host Community Garden Workdays every week for students to plant, harvest, and learn how to use gardening tools
    • We host Urban Agriculture Workdays every Tuesday morning at 11 for students to upkeep the Kendeda rooftop garden and learn to cook with food they harvest!
    • We host monthly community events for students to volunteer in local neighborhood cleanups and forest restorations
    • We host monthly social events for students to meet other members and create a sense of community among SOSers
  • Engage in discussions and leadership opportunities with some of the events below:
    • We host monthly general meetings which generally include current events discussions by members, project team updates, occasional guest speakers, and general updates
    • Members may propose their own projects which tackle a specific sustainability concern at Georgia Tech or in the Atlanta community with the support of our organization
      • Please fill out the Project Proposal Form if you’re interested (don’t worry if you don’t yet have a team, we can connect you with other interested members!)
  • All organization events and meetings can be found in the GT-SOS Calendar
  • For the current SOS structure, please see our bylaws. If you wish to become a member, please fill out our Membership Form and get added to our Slack Workspace!
  • Click here for project specific GroupMe's and other club info!
summary: We are a student-founded and student-run organization that is dedicated to the promotion of environmental, economic, and social sustainability practices in our community and on our campus; we also run the Community Garden by the Instructional Center.

name: The Technique
description:
The purpose of the Technique is to serve the campus community by providing information, analysis and opinions that reflect the needs and interests of the student body at Georgia Tech. Founded in 1911, the Technique serves as the campus newspaper for the Tech community. See our full mission statement: nique.net/about Staff meetings (for writers) are held weekly on Tuesdays at 7:00 p.m. in Rm. 2150 of the John Lewis Student Center. To submit a letter to the editor, email opinions@nique.net. To purchase an ad, visit mediakit.nique.net. For all general questions, contact editor@nique.net

We accept all skill levels and majors! We're always looking for students interested in writing, photography, design, web development, podcast recording and much more. Whether you've never written an article in your life or you're a professional photographer, we're excited to welcome you to our staff.

Check out some of our full issues online here: https://www.scribd.com/user/198757487/The-Technique

Check out this awesome video of some of our Technique Alumni talking about their time on the Technique staff: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QlxUH29lmj4
summary: The purpose of the Technique is to serve the campus community by providing information, analysis and opinions that reflect the needs and interests of the student body at Georgia Tech. Founded in 1911, the Technique serves as the campus newspaper...

name: Student Organization Finance Office
description: The Student Organizations Finance Office(SOFO) provides financial support to ensure the success of Georgia Tech’s student-led organizations. SOFO serves as the student groups’ on-campus bookkeeper by effectively managing collected dues, fundraising, and expenditures as well as travel planning, expenses, and reimbursements. The Student Organization Finance Office supports these student organizations by assisting them with their financial management needs.
summary: The Student Organizations Finance Office(SOFO) provides financial support to ensure the success of Georgia Tech’s student-led organizations.

organizationType.name: Division of Student Engagement and Well-being

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc.
description: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated (AKA) is an international service organization that was founded on the campus of Howard University in Washington, D.C. in 1908. It is the oldest Greek-lettered organization established by African-American college-educated women. Alpha Kappa Alpha is comprised of graduate and undergraduate chapters in the United States, the U. S. Virgin Islands, the Caribbean, Canada, Japan, Germany, Korea and on the continent of Africa. Its membership is comprised of distinguished women who boast excellent academic records, proven leadership skills, and are involved in the global community through advocacy and service. Alpha Kappa Alpha has dedicated itself to improving the quality of life for citizens worldwide and promoting peace.
summary: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated (AKA) is an international service organization that was founded on the campus of Howard University in Washington, D.C. in 1908. It is the oldest Greek-lettered organization established by African-American colleg...

name: Office of Student Media
description: The mission of the Office of Student Media at the Georgia Institute of Technology is to provide the Institute and Atlanta communities with news, information and a forum for the exchange of ideas, while providing an educational learning environment for students interested in creative expression and media management. Through the production of a student newspaper, yearbook, literary magazine, undergraduate research journal, free speech publication, online media and a non-commercial FM radio station, students gain practical experience in news gathering and dissemination, management skills, production, advertising and other vital business aspects of media operations. The Institute's student media organizations operate with complete editorial independence under the leadership of the Office of Student Media and their respective governing boards.

name: Fellowship of Christian Graduate Students
description:
We believe that God has called and equipped Christian graduate students and faculty to be a redeeming influence among the people, ideas, and structures of the university and professions. Our vision is to see students and faculty transformed, campuses renewed and world changers developed. In the power of the Spirit, we share the following four commitments: Spiritual Formation, Evangelism & Service, Community, Integration of Faith, Learning, & Practice.

Check out our website at https://sites.gatech.edu/fcgs/. Email us at gtfcgs@gmail.com or join our facebook group at https://www.facebook.com/groups/GTFCGS/ . We are affiliated with Intervarsity Graduate and Faculty Ministries (http://www.intervarsity.org/gfm/).
summary: We are a welcoming community of Christian grad students who study the Word together. We believe that God has called and equipped Christian graduate students and faculty to be a redeeming influence among the people, ideas, and structures of the university

name: Beta Beta Beta Biology Honors Society
description:
Beta Beta Beta Biology Honor Society is a professional organization dedicated to improving the understanding and appreciation of the biological sciences. We aim to unite students with a common passion by bridging the gap between the collegiate and professional, allowing members to get an intimate understanding of the kind of work they will encounter in their scientific careers. The Sigma Beta (Georgia Tech) chapter of Tri-Beta accomplishes this through Research and Social activities. In Research activities, members get firsthand exposure to ongoing research areas and open lab positions across campus. In Social activities, members get to relate with like-minded people about anything within and outside of the world of science. Membership is open to any and all students, regardless of major or experience, who possess a strong interest in biology! 

Stay connected with us on GroupMe and Instagram! 

THESE ARE THE BEST WAY TO KEEP UP WITH US.

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/98785233/JUVFK9is

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/gttribeta/
summary: Beta Beta Beta, or commonly known as Tri-Beta, is a National Biological Honor Society. The purpose of our society is to recognize achievement, promote scholarship and research, and expand knowledge of the biological sciences.

name: Biomedical Engineering Society
description:
The BMES Student Chapter at Georgia Tech works to promote biomedical engineering and its utilization by introducing students to the profession of biomedical engineering and the roles and obligations of the professional biomedical engineer. We host a variety of Industry, Social, Philanthropy, Mentorship and Research events to introduce students to the different aspects and career tracks available to BME students. You can learn more about our different committees on our website by clicking the globe button below.

How to Join: To become a member of GT BMES, you should join our Slack channel where we send all of our communications! (https://join.slack.com/t/georgiatechbmes/shared_invite/zt-v7c2plpd-dK__gfmNnCNUGeqxIVQU1w) 

Message one of the officers to find out more about what you need to do to become an official member of our BMES Chapter!
summary: The BMES Student Chapter at Georgia Tech works to promote biomedical engineering and its utilization by introducing students to the profession of biomedical engineering and the roles and obligations of the professional biomedical engineer.

name: Theta Xi Fraternity
description: As the only Christian Fraternity on Georgia Tech's campus and the only Christian Chapter of Theta Xi nationwide, Theta Xi presents a unique opportunity for Christian, college men. Joining Theta Xi means that you become a part of a brotherhood that emphasizes campus leadership, social activities, academic achievement, athletic success, civic involvement, and spiritual growth.Theta Xi is one of Georgia Tech's largest social fraternities, and has one of the largest houses.
summary: As the only Christian Fraternity on Georgia Tech's campus and the only Christian Chapter of Theta Xi nationwide, Theta Xi presents a unique opportunity for Christian, college men. Joining Theta Xi means that you become a part of a brotherhood that emph...

name: Graduate Association of Physicists
description: The Graduate Association of Physicists (GAP) is a graduate student organization in the Georgia Tech School of Physics that aims to provide mentoring, networking and career development opportunities to physics PhD students. Our plans include bringing in speakers who earned PhDs in physics and ended up within and outside of academia, organizing physics community outreach events, mentoring for first year physics PhD students and more.
summary: The Graduate Association of Physicists (GAP) is a graduate student organization in the Georgia Tech School of Physics that aims to provide mentoring, networking and career development opportunities to physics PhD students.

name: Rowing Club
description: GT Crew/Rowing Club is one of the largest and most successful sports clubs on campus. It’s one of the only sports you can pick up your freshman year with no prior experience and potentially become a national champion the next. We travel and compete, consistently beating seasoned rowers with scholarships. It's an opportunity to be a part of a team, make the closest friends you'll have here at Tech, get in amazing shape, and experience something new.
summary: GT Crew/Rowing Club is one of the largest and most successful sports clubs on campus. It’s one of the only sports you can pick up your freshman year with no prior experience and potentially become a national champion the next. We travel and compete, co..

name: The Wesley Foundation at Georgia Tech
description:
Tech Wesley is a ministry of the United Methodist Church to the Georgia Tech community. By offering ministries of witness, nurture, outreach, fellowship, and stewardship, we seek to provide a well-balanced approach to faith development. Students, faculty, staff, alumni, and friends of Georgia Tech are welcome to participate in all of our programs, regardless of religious affiliation, or lack thereof. Open Hearts. Open Minds. Open Doors.

 

Follow us at @gatechwesley on Instagram!

Join our Slack Workspace with the link below for all day-to-day announcements!

https://join.slack.com/t/techwesley/shared_invite/zt-5zpixw90-n7A4eI9q_ywPhIZV~tOiuw
summary: Tech Wesley is a ministry of the United Methodist Church to the Georgia Tech community. By offering ministries of witness, nurture, outreach, fellowship, and stewardship, we seek to provide a well-balanced approach to faith development.

name: Yellow Jacket Archery Club
summary: The Yellow Jacket Archery Club exists to facilitate the growth and development of archers at Georgia Tech. We support competitive and recreational archers of all skill levels and styles. No equipment or experience necessary. We shoot four times a week.

name: Consult Your Community
description:
Consult Your Community (CYC) is a student-run national organization that provides pro bono consulting services for low-income or minority small businesses across America. The Georgia Tech chapter provides small business owners in the Atlanta area with the necessary resources and recommendations in marketing, finance, accounting, technology, and operations to help revitalize, strengthen, and grow their business.

 

Each semester, we construct teams of analysts and project leaders to develop management consulting deliverables to our clients. We work with 7-10 projects every semester across a wide variety of industries including retail, restaurant, technology, professional services, healthcare, and nonprofit.

 

Throughout the semester, CYC Analysts get training, guidance, and support from the best consulting firms and faculty to identify and execute strategies for business. After completing the Analyst Training Program, our members have access to workshops, information sessions, mentors, and networking opportunities from some of the top consulting firms in Atlanta. Not to mention, our members provide a close knit group of qualified and interested undergraduates that are pursuing careers in consulting.

 

You can find more information about us on our website, as well as our Instagram @cycgatech.
summary: Consult Your Community (CYC) is a student-run organization that provides pro bono consulting services for low-income or minority small businesses in Atlanta and teaches undergraduate members the principals of business and consulting.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers
description: Founded in 2007, GT SASE is one of the oldest SASE collegiate chapters in the nation. In short, SASE is a professional organization devoted to helping you develop the skills and confidence necessary to land your ideal job in the workplace throughout the nation. Our vision is to increase the involvement of Asian professionals and students in the engineering and science fields by providing targeted academic and professional guidance through networking and professional services. In addition, we aim to develop our members into well-rounded scholars who are prepared to lead and make a positive impact in society throughout their careers. We also provide ample opportunities for carefree fun, networking and community involvement. We have co-hosted many events with various corporate partners and organizations, including info sessions, workshops, volunteer programs. In 2017 and 2019, we hosted the SASE Southeast Regional Conference, a huge networking opportunity that attracted more than 50 employers/speakers and over 100 college students across the southeast. As an official GT SASE member, you will receive special benefits, including invitations to special events and information about internships. We warmly welcome all Tech students regardless of major, race, or graduation year.
summary: Founded in 2007, GT SASE is one of the largest SASE collegiate chapters in the nation. In short, SASE is a professional organization devoted to helping you develop the skills and confidence necessary to land your ideal job in the workplace.

name: Yellow Jacket Space Program
description: Welcome to the Yellow Jacket Space Program! Our mission is to be the first collegiate team to send a liquid-fuel vehicle to outer space. No experience needed!
summary:
Georgia Tech's Space Program

https://www.gtspaceprogram.com/

name: Phi Mu
description:
Primary contact: gtphimu.president@gmail.com

Primary philanthropy contact: gtphimu.philanthropy@gmail.com

The sisters of the Theta Zeta chapter of Phi Mu welcome you to our website! Here at Georgia Tech, Phi Mu is more than a sorority, letters, or a creed…it is our home. We hope that you enjoy learning more about us, and get a glimpse of what we pride ourselves on: sisterhood, scholarship, our philanthropic endeavors, individuality, and of course, the history and tradition of our sorority.
summary: The sisters of the Theta Zeta chapter of Phi Mu welcome you to our website!  Here at Georgia Tech, Phi Mu is more than a sorority, letters, or a creed…it is our home.  We hope that you enjoy learning more about us, and get a glimpse of what we pride ou...

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description:
The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) at Georgia Tech is a cultural/social organization that appreciates and shares Vietnamese Culture. You don’t have to be Vietnamese to join!

GT VSA promotes networking and making long-term connections by hosting many social events to foster this connection. VSA hosts flagship events like our annual Fall Moon Festival and Spring Charity Gala. We also have smaller events like bowling, hiking, board games, and other socials that are very informal.

 

Being a member allows you to gain invaluable experiences in leadership and planning in addition to soft skills such as public speaking, networking, fundraising, financial management, and more. Join GTVSA to be part of these great events and experience Vietnamese culture. Vietnamese Student Association is a diverse group that welcomes everyone!

Click here to join our Discord Server, our primary communication hub:
https://discord.gg/fBUe4AxUKM

https://youtu.be/1vYGw2tzs6g?t=55
summary: Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) at Georgia Tech is a social organization that shares Vietnamese culture, promotes networking and making long term connections, and hosts many social events that encourage these.

name: Dance Company
description:
Dance Company offers weekly classes that focus on choreography for a variety of shows in and around the GT campus. We have offered dances in many styles including ballet, pointe, contemporary, lyrical, modern, hip-hop, tap, and jazz. Dancers of all levels are welcome to join our dance company! We host an annual showcase each fall, and we have many other performance opportunities throughout the year. 

 

Format for Fall 2025

DCGT is planning to offer weekly classes and rehearsals in person for the Fall 2025 Semester! 

DCGT members can participate in team-bonding activities and socials throughout the semester. We try to host at least one social every semester for members to hang out outside of school and dance class. Last year, we hosted a game night and a karaoke night. For this upcoming semester, we may also plan events such as a welcome back workshop and some photo shoots on GT's campus!

 

This semester, we will host our annual DCGT Fall Showcase, where our members can join performances as choreographers and dancers. We also invite other on-campus dance groups to showcase their dances in our show. Details and additional opportunities are to be announced at a later date. Stay tuned!

 

How to Join:

Anyone can become a member of DCGT - no dance experience is required! Please join our Engage page and our Microsoft Teams group (use the link below).

 All communications will be on Microsoft Teams. Join here

Also, join our Engage, and feel free to check out our Linktree for links to our social media and platforms we use. By joining our Engage, we'll add you to our newsletter mailing list so you know when all the DCGT events are and what the next steps are for joining.

Dues this semester will be collected after a trial period (where you can come to as many classes as you want for free). Dues are $5 for classes and an additional cost for a merch item (t-shirt[$10], sweatshirt[$20], water bottle[$8]). You can venmo us dues or pay in cash.

*The dues deadline will be announced soon!

We are looking forward to a fun semester, and we hope you will come dance with us!
summary: Dance Company offers weekly classes that focus on choreography for a variety of shows in and around the GT campus. We have offered dances in many styles including ballet, pointe, lyrical, modern, hip-hop, tap, and jazz. Dancers of all levels are welcome.

name: Asian American Student Association
description: The Asian American Student Association at Georgia Tech is a cultural organization that seeks to promote diversity and provide a community for Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI) students on campus. We hope to expand the definition of being Asian American, educate the student body on AAPI cultures, encourage conversations about current social and political issues, and expand the student experience. The organization is open to any Georgia Tech student. Our linktree has all the resources on how to be involved: linktr.ee/gtaasa
summary: The Asian American Student Association at GT is a cultural organization seeking to promote diversity and provide a community for Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI) students on campus. We hope to expand the definition of being an Asian American

name: Tau Kappa Epsilon
description: As one of Georgia Tech's largest social fraternities, Tau Kappa Epsilon exemplifies the ideals of leadership, character, and diversity. It is our priority to create a healthy environment that facilitates strong brotherhood, campus involvement, and healthy academic habits.
summary: As one of Georgia Tech’s largest social fraternities, Tau Kappa Epsilon exemplifies leadership, character, and diversity while fostering a healthy environment focused on brotherhood, campus involvement, and personal growth.

name: Men's Ultimate Frisbee
description: Men's Ultimate Frisbee is a close-knit, competitive team that competes at a national level. We practice throughout the year and travel across the Southeast to compete against other universities. We have an A and B team to best serve players' goals and the development of our program.
summary: Men's Ultimate Frisbee is a close-knit, competitive team that competes at a national level. We practice throughout the year and travel across the Southeast to compete against other universities.  We have an A and B team to best serve players' goals and...

name: Engineering World Health
description:
Engineering World Health is a volunteer organization that helps repair medical equipment. Together we'll attend MedShare repair sessions and hear from experienced industry professionals. 

Every year, over $765 billion dollars worth of medical equipment is discarded. Our mission is to bring awareness and sustainable solutions to the global inequities in health care in the developing world. Engineering World Health organizes monthly repair sessions at the MedShare warehouse in Decatur and holds events and workshops on campus throughout the semester to bring awareness to the current state of global healthcare and to resources that will allow us to provide low-cost, innovative solutions and address the issues at hand. During the repair sessions, our members volunteer their time and engineering skills to troubleshoot and repair medical equipment donated by Atlanta hospitals. The restored medical equipment is then shipped to hospitals in countries in need. Make a world of difference! We also hold kit sessions for students to learn how to build a device using the awesome tools in the Invention Studio. No experience is necessary, and all majors are welcome!

To learn more email abasu70@gatech.edu and follow us on Instagram @ewhgt!
summary: Engineering World Health is a volunteer organization that helps repair medical equipment. Together we'll attend MedShare repair sessions and hear from experienced industry professionals...

name: Chamber Choir
description:
The Georgia Tech Chamber Choir is Tech's select, mixed vocal ensemble. Founded in 1998, the choir has established itself as one of the premiere college vocal ensembles in the Southern United States. Singers are selected through audition, and the Chamber Choir attracts the finest choral musicians on the Tech campus. Its members are all-state-level singers who have extensive background in ensemble singing and sight reading. The Chamber Choir performs challenging literature from many time periods and styles, especially emphasizing on Renaissance, Baroque, and modern works.

Each semester, Chamber Choir also hosts Pitch-a-Piece: a student-run concert of a cappella music from a wide variety of genres and time periods. Chamber Choir tours yearly, with past destinations including Cyprus in Southeastern Europe; San Francisco, California; and New York, New York at Carnegie Hall.

Auditions for Chamber Choir take place at the beginning of each Fall semester. Audition information is posted to our website near the beginning of Fall. Feel free to contact the chamber choir officers to ask any questions or express interest in the group!
summary: The Georgia Tech Chamber Choir is Tech's select, mixed vocal ensemble that performs challenging literature in multiple concerts each year under the direction of Dr. Joshua Palkki. Chamber Choir tours yearly, and hosts a student-run concert each semester.

name: Meditation Club
description:
The purpose of the Meditation Club is to promote the well-being of Tech students, faculty, and staff through the practice of meditation. To do so, we offer guided group meditations every week! This means there are plenty of opportunities for you to join us and develop a consistent meditation practice.

Our meetings are open for anyone to join!

To learn more about the Meditation Club and when we meet, visit our Website.
summary: The Meditation Club provides a time and place for student well-being through the practice of meditation. Join us for weekly in person and virtual meditation sessions!

name: Equestrian Club
description:
Founded in 1995, the Equestrian Club aims to provide equestrians with the opportunity to ride at Georgia Tech. We offer Hunt Seat style of riding, and have both a club and competitive team. The team competes with the Intercollegiate Horse Show Association.

 

If you are interested in joining, please fill out this form:

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSc00j6RZb4-Z5U7p0ga8iQYEoh5nOQxS7AzUfr1pG_8YCQJug/viewform?usp=sf_link

Please note that we accept new members at the beginning of each semester (we cannot accept new members in the middle of a semester). 

Also, check out our website and Instagram for more information about our club and our members! 

https://sites.google.com/site/gtequestrian/

https://www.instagram.com/gtequestrianteam/
summary: Founded in 1995, the Equestrian Club aims to provide everyone with the opportunity to ride no matter what skill level. We offer Hunt Seat style of riding, and have both a club and competitive team. The team competes with IHSA several times per year.

name: Alpha Sigma Phi
description: The Zeta Eta chapter of Alpha Sigma Phi was founded on April 21, 2012 with 33 dedicated brothers. Despite the deeply rooted Greek culture at Georgia Tech, we have managed to stand out as highly motivated and passionate about making Greek life the best it can possibly be. We are looking for men of the highest character here at our Institute. Alpha Sig offers many different opportunities for top men on campus, including: leadership development, campus involvement, community service, strong academics, sporting events, and of course brotherhood and an unrivaled social experience.
summary: The Zeta Eta chapter of Alpha Sigma Phi was founded on April 21, 2012 with 33 dedicated brothers. Despite the deeply rooted Greek culture at Georgia Tech, we have managed to stand out as highly motivated and passionate about making Greek life the best ...

name: Pulse
description: Pulse is a classical Indian dance team founded in 2010 at the Georgia Institute of Technology. We are dedicated to testing rhythm and melody to the fullest extent. Our goal is to create innovative dance routines that push the limits of our imagination and creativity. GT Pulse is heavily committed to promoting diversity at Georgia Tech through an expression and display of Indian classical dance. Pulse provides its members with opportunities to perform at competitions and philanthropic events. Most notably, we were honored to win the Origins National Championship in 2022. GT Pulse has placed first at the following national competitions: ETSU SACE Diwali Show (2010), ATL Tamasha (2011), Laasya (2013), Mayuri (2014), Mayuri (2016), UTD Hansini (2017), CWRU Samasana (2020), Dhirana (2022), and Laasya (2025). GT Pulse has also won second place at So You Think Tech Can Dance (2010), Dhirana (2014), Laasya (2015), Laasya (2016), Laasya (2017), UTD Hansini (2018), Dhirana (2019), Mayuri (2023), and Nachte Raho (2024). Finally, we've also won 3rd at UMD Mayuri (2018), Laasya (2019), Samasana (2020), and Dhirana (2025)!
summary: Pulse is a classical Indian dance team founded in 2010 at the Georgia Institute of Technology. We are dedicated to testing rhythm and melody to the fullest extent. Our goal is to create innovative dance routines rooted in traditional elements.

name: Beta Theta Pi
description: Being a Beta is an experience that will change your life. Here at Beta Theta Pi, we believe in "making good men better" while enjoying all aspects of college life. We strive to improve with each new member. Our desire to be the best is tangibly shown in our winning of the Dean Dull Best Overall Fraternity award 30 of the past 41 years. This award, which is based on academics, campus involvement, athletics, and philanthropic service to the community, is not a goal or aspiration but merely a byproduct of our everyday efforts. Even though we constantly have one of the highest GPAs among fraternities, we place a great deal of emphasis on consistently having one of the best social calendars on campus as well. Each year we have several band parties, Beta Lei (our annual beach-themed party), semi-formal, formal, mixers with sororities, and various Brotherhood events. We also pride ourselves on our athletics finishing among the top fraternities every year. Above all, Betas are recognized as gentlemen.
summary: Being a Beta is an experience that will change your life. Here at Beta Theta Pi, we believe in "making good men better" while enjoying all aspects of college life. We strive to improve with each new member. Our desire to be the best is tangibly shown i...

name: Pi Kappa Phi
description: Transcendent. Loyal. Fun. Prominent. Compassionate. Involved. Respected. All of these words have different definitions, but at Georgia Tech they mean the same thing: Pi Kappa Phi. On December 10, 1904, three students united in Charleston, South Carolina to form a club that would propel them to the leadership of their university. This organization became Pi Kappa Phi and the trend continues today. By 1913, the Iota chapter of Pi Kappa Phi was founded on Georgia Tech's campus. Whether it is in intramural athletics, academics, or philanthropy, our diverse membership make us a leader in every category. We are the only Greek organization in the country to operate our own philanthropy, Ability Experience. Furthermore, we offer the services of an academic adviser. When you become an associate member of Pi Kappa Phi, you embark upon a journey of mutual respect; our house is your house and you are treated like a brother. When you finally graduate from GT you will join an elite group of alumni.
summary: Transcendent. Loyal. Fun. Prominent. Compassionate. Involved. Respected. All of these words have different definitions, but at Georgia Tech they mean the same thing: Pi Kappa Phi. On December 10, 1904, three students united in Charleston, South Carolin...

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
description:
Georgia Tech’s student chapter of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) serves as a connection to the national aerospace professional organization. Events hosted by our organization are open to undergraduate and graduate students of all majors and focus on enriching students' understanding of and appreciation for the aerospace industry through interactions with professionals and industry leaders, research and technical development opportunities, etc.

------

GT AIAA brings in representatives & alumni from industry/research/academia to talk about their work in the Aerospace field and opportunities for our student members.

------

We also focus on networking and recruitment, hosting numerous events. Participants from the past few years include NASA JPL, NASA Marshall, GE Aviation, SpaceX, Blue Origin, Delta Airlines, Southwest Airlines, Saab Sensis, The Boeing Company, SpaceWorks, Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, General Atomics, and many more.

------

AIAA as a whole is a national organization dedicated to addressing the professional needs of the aerospace industry. They hold several conferences a year and publish a number of peer reviewed journals. For more information about the national organization, visit their website: aiaa.org.

------

Meetings are not a set time, so be sure to follow the Instagram and join the Slack to stay updated on upcoming events. Click "View past events" to see check out the type of events we typically host!

------

Join our Slack! 

gtaiaa.slack.com (Sign in with GT Credentials)
summary: GT's student branch of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) serves as a connection to the national aerospace professional organization. We host networking events, tours, & more to help students get involved in industry

name: The Tower Undergraduate Research Journal
description: The Tower is Geogia Tech's undergraduate research journal. We publish undergraduate student submissions on individual and team research projects, reviews of recent research literature and opinion pieces related to research, among others.
summary: The Tower is Georgia Tech's undergraduate research journal. We publish undergraduate student submissions on individual and team research projects. reviews of recent research literature and opinion pieces related to research, among others.

name: Korean-American Scientists and Engineers Association
description: KSEA-YG is a networking organization where Korean-American young generation in science and engineering fields can meet, communicate, and help each other. KSEA was established in 1971 as a non-profit professional organization and has grown to over 3000 registered members with 67 local Chapters (including four Branches) and 14 technical groups across the United States. KSEA Georgia Chapter is an association of scientists and engineers who live and work in Georgia. Because of an immense number of students and young professionals in the science and engineering field in Georgia, we are launching a Young Generation (YG) chapter.
summary: KSEA-YG is a networking organization where Korean-American young generation in science and engineering fields can meet, communicate, and help each other. KSEA was established in 1971 as a nationwide non-profit professional organization

name: Sport Parachute Club
description: We jump out of airplanes. If you want to become a licensed skydiver and learn a wide range of awesome skills while getting the chance to compete at a national level, or if you just want to try out some indoor skydiving, this is the club for you.
summary: We jump out of airplanes. If you want to become a licensed skydiver and learn a wide range of awesome skills while getting the chance to compete at a national level, or if you just want to try out some indoor skydiving, this is the club for you.

name: Global Medical Missions Alliance
description: GMMA (Global Medical Missions Alliance) at Georgia Tech strives to mobilize and to serve the global community by empowering students and aspiring healthcare professionals and by networking with other missions organizations to bring God's healing to all nations. We prepare and participate in international medical mission trips where students will shadow physicians and support medical missionaries. Our organization will be inviting guest speakers to share their testimonies, holding fundraisers for mission trips, and fellowshipping as a body of Christ with other members. We, as a national organization will also take part in going to biannual conferences, regional conferences, and global missions outreach in the local community.
summary: GMMA (Global Medical Missions Alliance) at Georgia Tech strives to mobilize and to serve the global community by empowering students and aspiring healthcare professionals and by networking with other missions organizations to bring God's healing to all...

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: The student chapter of the American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME) at the Georgia Institute of Technology is a dedicated student-run organization which strives to provide professional development opportunities for mechanical engineering students. Our weekly meetings consist of world-renowned companies who present about the work they do in their respective industries and what job opportunities they offer. Additionally, our organization hosts the annual ME Career Fair as well as additional networking opportunities throughout the school year. Our organization provides an avenue for engaging ME students with the Woodruff School of Mechanical Engineering and with industry while celebrating the spirit of being a mechanical engineer at Georgia Tech.
summary: The student chapter of the American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME) at the Georgia Institute of Technology is a dedicated student-run organization which strives to provide professional development opportunities for mechanical engineering students.

name: Alpha Omega Campus Ministry
description: We are a campus ministry radically devoted to following Jesus in any way he commands us to. We strongly emphasize basing our doctrine and lives on the Bible (1 Timothy 4:16, Acts 17:11-12). We also understand the importance of deep, spiritual relationships and the crucial role basic discipling relationships played in the scriptures and continues to play in our lives. We love growing together as a spiritual family pursuing spiritual formation, Godly community and fun :). Come check us out if you're interested!
summary: We are a campus ministry radically devoted to following Jesus in any way he commands us to. We strongly emphasize basing our doctrine and lives on the Bible (1 Timothy 4:16, Acts 17:11-12). We are committed to building a strong community rooted in God.

name: Taiwanese Student Association
description: The Taiwanese Student Association (TSA) at Georgia Tech is a non-profit student organization that focuses on enriching the college experience of both undergraduate and graduate Taiwanese students who shared common interest and background in Taiwanese culture. Every year, we assist new incoming Taiwanese students to become familiar with the city of Atlanta and Georgia Tech. We hold activities and entertainments frequently to enhance the bonding among all TSA members.
summary: The Taiwanese Student Association (TSA) at Georgia Tech is a non-profit student organization that focuses on enriching the college experience of both undergraduate and graduate Taiwanese students who share interest and background in Taiwanese culture.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description: The National Pan-Hellenic Council, Incorporated (NPHC) is composed of nine International Greek letter Sororities and Fraternities:  Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc., Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc., Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc., Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc., Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc., Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc., Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc., Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc., and Iota Phi Theta Fraternity, Inc. NPHC promotes interaction through forums, meetings and other mediums for the exchange of information and engages in cooperative programming and initiatives through various activities and functions. Georgia Tech is currently home to eight of the organizations in the “Divine Nine”; Nu Mu Chapter of Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity Inc., Nu Beta Chapter of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority Inc., Lambda Delta Chapter of Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity Inc., Delta Kappa Chapter of Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Inc., Xi Alpha Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Inc., Nu Beta Chapter of Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity Inc., Gamma Rho Chapter of Zeta Phi Beta Sorority Inc., and Sigma Upsilon Chapter of Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority Inc.
summary: The National Pan-Hellenic Council, Incorporated (NPHC) is composed of nine International Greek letter Sororities and Fraternities.

name: Veggie Jackets
description:
Veggie Jackets is a student organization for the vegan/vegetarian community at GT. We aim to bring together the veggie community at GT with social events, food, volunteer opportunities, and informative guest speaker events. For the Animals. For Health. For the Earth.

Check out our Linktree ( https://linktr.ee/veggiejackets ) to access our GroupMe, newsletter, or Discord server, and more!
summary: Georgia Tech student organization focused on advocating for a plant-based lifestyle for the animals, the planet, and our health! 🐄🌎💪

name: Delta Sigma Phi
description: Founded at City College of New York on December 10, 1899, Delta Sigma Phi is a national fraternity that boasts over one hundred chapters across the country, and the Alpha Gamma Chapter at Georgia Tech has been an integral part of campus since its inception on January 10, 1920. As our national fraternity motto states, “Better Men, Better Lives,” Delta Sigma Phi prides itself on cultural and intellectual diversity, a tightly knit brotherhood, and balanced living. Our men uphold these standards through volunteer and philanthropy work with organizations such as the Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta and Habitat for Humanity. Members also strive to give back to campus through involvement with SGA, IFC, GT Motorsports, Ski Club, GT Student Foundation, FASET Orientation, and GTVC amongst others. Above all, we prove our commitment to the academic success of our members by consistently achieving one of the best GPAs among all fraternities on campus. We reward that hard work with brotherhood events, participation in the yearly Greek Week and Homecoming competitions, our trademark spring ShipWreck party, date nights, mixers with sororities, and other social activities. If you are looking to join an outstanding group of gentlemen, we hope to see you stop by Delta Sigma Phi.
summary: Delta Sigma Phi was the first Fraternity founded on the principle of universal brotherhood – a fraternity of men defined by the quality of the individuals and not their beliefs. In the spirit of this ideal, our Tech chapter became the first Fraternity ...

name: Esports
description:
The original collegiate organization supporting both the competitive and casual gaming community at Georgia Tech! Georgia Tech Esports is among the largest organizations on campus since its formation around two decades ago. Our mission is to make gaming at Georgia Tech accessible and enjoyable for everyone—whether you're a casual or a competitive player while also maintaining our campus position as one of the top schools in the world of competitive gaming. The organization, with the help of the Campus Recreation Center, RHA, GTAA, and SGA, assists in supporting our collegiate teams as they compete at both regional and national levels while simultaneously organizing multi-collegiate LAN events and exciting activities for Georgia Tech gaming communities.

Join our Discord to learn more:
https://discord.gg/UtBR6ssCfb
summary: GT Esports is among the largest organizations on campus and serves students as a hub for casual and competitive gaming. The organization boasts 20+ casual gaming communities, 30+ competitive teams, and two National Championships in the past year alone.

name: Institute of Transportation Engineers
description: Our focus here at ITE@GT is on providing learning, networking and career opportunities throughout the year for our members. Every year, we hold our annual cookout with ITE Georgia bringing dozens of local professionals to meet and network with our members. In addition, we plan monthly meetings with invited speakers from local agencies, companies and even national firms, connect students with professionals, and give our members opportunities to find internships or even set up their careers- the ultimate proof that ITE@GT is providing for its members. Every year, we also fund student members to attend TRB, and host social events and show our ultimate dedication and commitment to the transportation industry.
summary: Our focus here at ITE@GT is on providing learning, networking and career opportunities throughout the year for our members. Every year, we hold our annual meeting with ITE Georgia bringing dozens of local professionals to meet and network with our memb..

name: Junior STEM
description:
Junior STEM is an organization that aims to encourage and facilitate student involvement in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math (STEM) initiatives to promote and increase the retention of underrepresented students and minorities in STEM careers. Many of our initiatives are bidirectional in order to impact students on-campus as well as students in local K-12 schools. Our organization volunteers weekly with local K-12 schools by demonstrating and working with STEM related concepts. We also volunteer at STEM fairs and other STEM related events such as Lego Robotics Competitions, Science Olympiad, Future Tech, Middle Grades Events, etc. We also host our own Step into STEM event, a large fair where K-12 students get hands on experience with STEM and learn more about the possibilities in the evergrowing field.

Join our GroupMe here:  https://groupme.com/join_group/96181875/d2YkYoRK 

For more information, please contact: juniorstem.gatech@gmail.com
summary: Junior STEM is an organization that aims to encourage and facilitate student involvement in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math (STEM) initiatives to promote and increase the retention of underrepresented students and minorities in STEM careers

name: Trailblazers
description:
Trailblazers is an official student organization of the Georgia Institute of Technology. The purpose of Trailblazers is to increase student and faculty appreciation for the outdoors through trail adventure and exploration. The Trailblazers Executive Committee coordinates trips combining environmental service projects with outdoor recreation to allow participants to build new friendships while serving and learning about the environment. All Georgia Tech undergraduate and graduate students (members and non-members), along with faculty and staff, are eligible to participate on Trailblazers outdoor service and adventure trips. Trips planned by the Trailblazers Executive Committee include multiple Local Projects throughout the year and Alternative Break Trips during Fall Break and Spring Break. We hope you choose to join us for a future adventure!

If you want to keep up to the minute with events we're doing, join our GroupMe!
summary: Trailblazers is an official student organization of the Georgia Institute of Technology. The purpose of Trailblazers is to increase student and faculty appreciation for the outdoors through outdoor service and recreation.

name: Phi Sigma Kappa
description: Phi Sigma Kappa or Phi Sig is a social fraternity within the Interfraternity Council. We strive to embody our Cardinal Principles of Brotherhood, Scholarship, and Character in our everyday lives. Our brothers participate in on-campus organizations like FASET, Georgia Tech For the Kids, Wreck Camp, Student Government Association, engineering competition teams, and many more. Our brothers also regularly participate in research, internships, and co-ops. Our calendar includes brotherhood events, date nights, mixers, registered parties, formal, destination events, philanthropy, and mental health programming.
summary: Phi Sigma Kappa or Phi Sig is a social fraternity within the Interfraternity Council. We strive to live out our Cardinal Principles of Brotherhood, Scholarship, and Character in our everyday lives.

name: Kappa Sigma Fraternity
description: The Alpha-Tau chapter of Kappa Sigma has been at Georgia Tech for more than 125 years and has continuously served and bettered the Institute's community. Our brotherhood prides itself on being strong, well-rounded, ambitious, and involved in every part of campus life.
summary: The Alpha-Tau chapter of Kappa Sigma has been at Georgia Tech for more than 125 years, and has continuously served and bettered the Institute's community. Our brotherhood prides itself on being strong, well-rounded, ambitious, and involved in every par.

name: Psi Upsilon
description:
Welcome to Psi U!

GT’s Only Gender-Inclusive Social Fraternity, 

where the phrase, “I never thought I’d join a fraternity” is thrown around a lot 

 

We are a mix of just about every interest or passion! 

Come in and you’ll see brothers working on Computational Media homework, painting another part of the upstairs wall, tailgating for football, or battling each other in Mario Kart, DnD, and Chess.

Some of our brothers play roller derby. Some bake cookies. Some play in bands for our house shows or wax philosophical about why we lost our last two chapter houses to parking lots.

 

We all enjoy different things…

But at the end of the day, we all end up under the same roof!

 

Come hang out at rush and see what we’re all about!
summary:
Welcome to Psi U!

GT’s Only Gender-Inclusive Social Fraternity 
where the phrase, “I never thought I’d join a fraternity” is thrown around a lot 

We are a mix of just about every interest or passion!

name: Reformed University Fellowship
description:
RUF is a community for the convinced and unconvinced to explore Christianity, learn more about who Jesus is and what it means to follow him with others at Georgia Tech.

 

RUF hosts a weekly large group service, weekly small groups, as well as other fun events throughout the year. We will be adapting our program to hybrid, virtual, and in person gatherings. We look forward to getting to know you! Please reach out to us if you have any questions, we would love to get you connected.
summary: RUF is a campus ministry that seeks to be a community for the convinced and unconvinced to explore Christianity, learn more about who Jesus is and what it means to follow him with others at Georgia Tech.

name: Theta Chi Fraternity
description:
Since its founding in 1856, Theta Chi Fraternity has grown to more than 244 Chapters and colonies across North America and has initiated over 185,000 Brothers. The Alpha Nu Chapter of Theta Chi Fraternity was founded at Georgia Tech in 1923 and in the almost 100 years since, our Fraternity has been building Resolute Men in all areas of life. Our desire to become better men begins with our commitment to scholarship at Georgia Tech and includes leadership, philanthropy, personal & professional development and of course, Brotherhood.

Our academic standing on campus is strong; we’ve been working hard to achieve our scholarship goals and consistently have a Chapter GPA that meets or exceeds the IFC’s All-Men’s average GPA.  

On campus, our Brothers are leaders in a number of organizations, including Greek Week, the Interfraternity Council, GT Student Foundation, GT Student Ambassadors, the Student Government Association, Club Sports, and many other student organizations. Additionally, we have quickly become leaders in the Greek community and campus-wide in mental health advocacy. Our Brothers are involved as Well-Being Activators, Healthy Jacket Peer Educators and activists in the Mental Health Student Coalition and in these roles, they have played a big part in bringing QPR suicide-prevention sessions to the Greek community, played an instrumental role in the planning and execution of Georgia Tech’s first Intercollegiate Mental Health Conference and continue to work with the Interfraternity Council and Georgia Tech administration to develop and implement new initiatives.

Socially, we continue to have a packed calendar semester after semester, highlighted by a number of Brotherhood and date events, mixers, parties and out-of-house events like Mountain Weekend, Beach Weekend and our Red Carnation Formal each spring. In three of the past four years, we have also been winners of Georgia Tech’s annual Greek Week competition. In philanthropy and service, in recent years we have formed partnerships with Foster Georgia Tech, United Service Organizations, Fernbank Museum and Make Money Go Round. Our local philanthropy, LifeLine Animal Services, an Atlanta based nonprofit working to end pet euthanasia in healthy and treatable animals by providing accessible pet care. Many of our philanthropy and social events are held at our Chapter house on Techwood Drive. Built in 2003, the Alpha Nu house is able to comfortably house forty-four Brothers and boasts being the only house on campus that enjoys a heated swimming pool. 

Amid all these different facets, Theta Chi is a place to grow and create a bond of Brotherhood that lasts a lifetime. We are ordinary men doing extraordinary things.
summary: Since its founding in 1856, Theta Chi Fraternity has grown to more than 219 chapters and colonies across North America and has initiated over 170,000 brothers. The Alpha Nu Chapter of Theta Chi Fraternity was founded at Georgia Tech in 1923. Since then...

name: La Unidad Latina, Lambda Upsilon Lambda Fraternity, Inc.
description:
Since its inception in 1982, La Unidad Latina has remained on the vanguard of political and community empowerment by developing influential leaders that strive to exert knowledge and power into its peers in order to attain mutual success. We commit ourselves to academic excellence, leadership development and cultural enlightenment, enhanced by a diverse cognizant membership. La Hermandad strives to preserve and promote an inclusive intellectual environment for its members, in addition to the general community.

 

In the execution of its conceived mission, La Unidad Latina will:

•Provide Latino and minority students with the academic, cultural and social support necessary to excel in institutions of higher learning.

•Increase opportunities for Latino and minority children and adolescents to achieve in elementary and secondary schools.

•Develop leaders that shall provide, develop and implement the tools for community empowerment.

•Inspire ethnic pride and cultural awareness in the Latino and minority community.

•Support the efforts to enhance the growth and well-being of the Latino and minority community.

•Collaborate with individuals, organizations and institutions that will join our efforts to improve the conditions of the Latino and minority community.
summary: Founded on December 2nd, 2004, the Alpha Sigma Chapter of La Unidad Latina, Lambda Upsilon Lambda, Fraternity Inc. was the first Latino based Greek organization on Georgia Tech's. We strive to continue the legacy of two decades of making Elite Hermanos.

name: Collegiate Panhellenic Council
description: The Collegiate Panhellenic Council is made up of 8 NPC sororities (ΑΧΩ, ΑΞΔ, AΔΠ,ΦΜ, ΖΤΑ, ΑΦ, ΑΓΔ, KAΘ) and 1 associate sorority (ΑΩΕ). Sorority chapters of CPC work to grow and develop women during their collegiate years by providing academic support, philanthropic opportunity, and value-based networks of women. The Collegiate Panhellenic Council supports the Circle of Sisterhood Foundation, working to bring educational services and school building to girls living in third world countries. CPC works closely with the 3 other Greek councils: IFC, MPC, and the NPHC.
summary: The Collegiate Panhellenic Council is made up of 8 NPC sororities (ΑΧΩ, ΑΞΔ, AΔΠ,ΦΜ, ΖΤΑ, ΑΦ, ΑΓΔ, KAΘ) and 1 associate sorority (ΑΩΕ). Sorority chapters of CPC work to grow and develop women during their collegiate years by providing academic s..

name: AIESEC
description:
AIESEC is an international youth-run, not-for-profit, non-political organization partnered with the United Nations that empowers young people to make a progressive social impact. We promote global leadership by offering cross-cultural internships, as well as developing our members in a local setting.

To learn more about AIESEC and our mission, visit https://www.aiesecus.org/!

Apply Today; Become a member!
summary: AIESEC is an international youth-run, not-for-profit, non-political organization that empowers young people to make a progressive social impact.

name: Taal Tadka
description:
About

Taal Tadka is Georgia Tech's 1st and only South Asian fusion a cappella group! We perform medleys of songs in Hindi, English, and other languages. We are co-ed, so all are welcome! We attend competitions and music workshops, and have a retreat every year. As our name suggests, we believe in creating a "spiced-up rhythm" through just our voices to encompass all cultures!

Learn more about us through Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, or email us!

 

If you love singing, we'd love you! Interested in auditioning? Here is the link to our audition interest form! We can't wait to hear you sing!
summary: Taal Tadka is GT's 1st and only South Asian fusion a cappella group! We perform medleys of songs in Hindi, English, and other languages. As our name suggests, we believe in creating a "spiced-up rhythm" through just our voices to encompass all cultures!

name: Phi Gamma Delta
description: We are an IFC fraternity at Georgia Tech. On campus, we pride ourselves on academics, athletics, and our commitment to serve Georgia Tech and the community as a whole.
summary: We are an IFC fraternity at Georgia Tech. On campus, we pride ourselves on academics, athletics, and our commitment to serve Georgia Tech and the community as a whole.

name: Eta Kappa Nu
description: Eta Kappa Nu (ΗΚΝ), founded in 1904, is the international electrical and computer engineering honor society of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE). The Beta Mu Chapter at Georgia Tech, chartered in 1941, seeks to recognize outstanding juniors and seniors in the School of ECE and College of Computing and to encourage the advancement of their academic and professional careers.
summary: Eta Kappa Nu (HKN) is the honor society of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE). Georgia Tech's Beta Mu chapter seeks to recognize and encourage outstanding juniors and seniors in the School of ECE and College of Computing.

name: North Avenue Review
description:
North Avenue Review is Tech's free speech, open forum magazine, which means we publish whatever we receive! We are very proud to have flourished over the past 30 years and to continue providing the Tech community with a platform to share thoughts, opinions, poems, art, or anything in-between. Our magazine publishes twice a semester in print and once a month for individual online exclusive pieces. Anyone can become a staff member, and we’re always open to taking on more interested students. Staff members can work on writing, editing, layout/graphic design, marketing, or anything in-between. All students are welcome to submit and are not subject to becoming staff members if not interested.

If you're interested in joining, please e-mail editor@northavereview.com or stop by a meeting! Follow our Instagram @northavereview for meeting and event updates. We have meetings every Wednesday 6 - 7 PM in the Student Media Center (near Dancing Goats in the Student Center)
summary: North Avenue Review (NAR) is Tech's free speech magazine. Established in 1989, we launched in response to student protests against Tiananmen Square. To this day, NAR provides all students and faculty with a platform to share thoughts, opinions, and more.

name: RoboGrads
description:
The purpose of the RoboGrads is to:

  1. Represent the interests of the students in IRIM
  2. Represent the interests of the students in the Robotics PhD program to the faculty and university
  3. Disseminate accurate and up-to-date student-specific information about the Robotics PhD program
  4. Promote robotics research and education at Georgia Tech
  5. Sponsor social events to engage IRIM students in a non-academic setting
  6. Liaise with other robotics organizations to provide a relevant network for Robotics students
  7. Give opportunities for robotics-related service in the community
summary: Robotics Graduate Student Organization.

name: Omega Phi Alpha
description: Omega Phi Alpha is a national service sorority with hundreds of collegiate members who actively serve their communities. Throughout more than 30 chapters at universities across the United States, OPA sisters strive to further the sorority’s principles of friendship, leadership and service. As alumnae, sisters become lifelong, service-minded leaders. To hear more about our sorority, please watch our youtube video here.
summary: Omega Phi Alpha is a national service sorority with hundreds of members who actively serve their communities. In 30 chapters across the United States, OPhiA sisters strive to further the sorority’s principles of friendship, leadership, and service.

name: Korean Student Association
description: KSA is the official Korean Student Association at Georgia Tech. We serve as a community for graduate students and postdoctoral researchers with Korean heritage, and support networking, collaboration, and cultural connection. The total number of graduate student members exceeds about four hundred.
summary: KSA is the official Korean Student Association at Georgia Tech. We serve as a community for graduate students and postdoctoral researchers with Korean heritage. The total number of graduate student members exceeds about four hundred.

name: SOON Movement
description: Soon Movement is an interdenominational campus ministry under Cru committed to help fulfill the Great Commission of Jesus Christ in this generation. "All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me. Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, and teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you. And surely I am with you always, to the very end of the age." (Matthew 28:18-20)
summary: Soon Movement is an interdenominational campus ministry under Cru committed to help fulfill the Great Commission of Jesus Christ in this generation. "All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me. Go and make disciples of all nations..."

name: Korean Undergraduate Student Association
description: KUSA (Korean Undergraduate Student Association), started in the early 1990s, is the forefront Korean cultural organization on campus. There are multiple KUSA chapters in the state of Georgia alongside the one of Georgia Tech; it is a very well-spread and known organization all around the United States. KUSA offers numerous services such as academic support, athletics, and networking opportunities.
summary: Started in the early 1990s, the Georgia Tech Korean Undergraduate Student Association (GT KUSA) is the forefront Korean cultural organization on campus. GT KUSA offers numerous services including athletics, academic support, and networking.

name: Yellow Jacket Fencing Club
description: The Yellow Jacket Fencing Club is the official Georgia Tech club for fencing, the sport of sword fighting! Founded in the Fall of 2004, the club has grown from a handful of members to well over 100 students and alumni! We offer all 3 weapons and accept fencers of all experience levels, even those with no experience at all. YJFC is also a great community; we hold lots of social events and always have fun traveling to tournaments. So come stab your friends!
summary:
Stab your friends!

The Yellow Jacket Fencing Club is the official GaTech club for fencing, the sport of sword fighting! We offer all 3 weapons and accept fencers of all experience levels, even those with no experience at all.

name: SKY
description:
SKY Happiness Retreat : https://tiny.cc/skygtretreat

SKY Campus Happiness is a mental health and well-being focused organization that offers comprehensive well-being and resilience programs that are designed to help students relieve stress, increase clarity of mind and achieve their true potential.

We believe that through decreased stress and clarity of mind, students can thrive in life and lead with greater purpose. Our vision is to make meditation and breath-work accessible and possible for all students, faculty and staff. We are honored that Georgia Tech recognizes our contribution and strength because of which we have been awarded the Gold-Certified Healthy by the Georgia Tech Health Initiatives. 

 

The SKY Campus Happiness Program incorporates an empirically-validated approach and includes evidence-based breathwork, yoga and meditation practices. Our activities go beyond practising holistic techniques, and encompasses practical life skills and service. 

The SKY Campus Happiness approach for total well-being:

  • Emotional Intelligence: Cultivate science-backed skills for emotional well-being
  • Breathwork & Meditation: Teach evidence-based SKY® Breath Meditation practice
  • Inclusion & Belonging: Build positive communities based on authenticity, trust, and respect
  • Service: Inspire an attitude of service and responsibility
  • Leadership: Empower participants to find purpose and realize their fullest potential

 

We host activities and workshops on campus led by trained instructors that provide tangible tools to help fellow students enjoy a stress free mind, a disease free body and a doubt free intellect. Our goals involve creating empowered student leaders who excel in academic and personal spheres, and are able to serve the campus community.

 

Take a look at how stress has an adverse effect on our brain and how the SKY breathing practices are effective in reducing stress and increasing focus, clarity and productivity.

Watch This 

 

Take a look at what a recent research study at Yale reveals about the effect of SKY meditation on stress and mental health

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Vlu3arLc0WE
summary: SKY Campus Happiness is a mental health and well-being focused organization that offers comprehensive well-being and resilience programs that are designed to help students relieve stress, increase clarity of mind and achieve their true potential.

name: The Baha'i Club
description:
The Baha'is at GT is a student organization that seeks to assist college students to serve others in a community building process. The Baha'i Faith is an independent, world religion that strives to bring unity between all peoples. We discuss and act on issues of world peace, spirituality, the equality of men and women and the progress of humanity in all fields through interfaith devotional gatherings, study circles, and service projects.

The club seeks participants from all religious, ethnic, and economic backgrounds to collectively take part in activities that enhance our capacity for service and engage younger youth and children in Atlanta in regular empowerment programs. Two examples of these community-building activities are a children's class and a Junior Youth Spiritual Empowerment Program that is ongoing at Centennial Place Apartments, in which junior youth, aged 12-15 participate weekly in a group of mutual support that assists them to become agents of change in their communities. Junior youth study materials that develop language expression and understanding of spiritual qualities such as honesty, humility, and trustworthiness. Together the junior youth come up with and plan service projects based on the needs of their community, and also engage in art and science activities. The animators, or facilitators of the groups are mostly college students who are unpaid volunteers that seek to serve as true friends and wise advisors to the junior youth who are navigating a challenging and critical time of life in which patterns of thought and behavior are formed. We welcome all students, regardless of their religious or non-religious backgrounds, to participate in the activities of the club and contribute to the betterment of their community.
summary: GT Baha'is is a student organization that seeks to assist college students to serve others in a community building process. The Baha'i Faith is an independent, world religion that strives to bring unity between all peoples.

name: Pakistani Student Association
description: PSA is a non-profit cultural organization that serves as platform to bring together Pakistani students and promote Pakistani culture at Georgia Tech. We hold events and mixers, on a regular basis, to develop a well-knit Pakistani Student Community and to provide students with a familiar context to Pakistan.
summary: PSA is a non-profit cultural organization that serves as platform to bring together Pakistani students and promote Pakistani culture at Georgia Tech. We hold events and mixers, on a regular basis, to develop a well-knit Pakistani Student Community and ...

name: The Living Room
description: The Living Room is a Home Away from Home for college students. We meet Monday nights at 7:30PM at Buckhead Church with free dinner, music, relevant teaching, and students from Tech and all over Atlanta. Park on site or take the shuttle from GT West Campus: 8th + Hemphill (7:00 pm) East Campus: Gate 8 at Bobby Dodd Stadium on Techwood (7:15 pm) Instagram // @thelivingroomatl thelivingroomatl.com
summary: The Living Room is a Home Away from Home for college students.We meet Monday nights at 7:30PM at Buckhead Church with free dinner, music, relevant teaching, and students from Tech and all over Atlanta.Park on site or take the shuttle from GTWest Campus...

name: Kappa Kappa Psi: Iota
description: Kappa Kappa Psi operates primarily as a student service and leadership recognition society whose chief aim is to assist the Director of Bands in developing the leadership and enthusiasm that is required of the Georgia Tech Bands. Our goals are to provide the band not only with organized and concentrated service activities, but to give our membership valid and wholesome experiences in organization, leadership and social contacts. The honorary nature of membership is based on our premise that “it is an honor to be selected to serve” the Georgia Tech band, the department of music, Georgia Tech, and the cause of band music in the nation’s colleges and universities.
summary: Kappa Kappa Psi operates primarily as a student service and leadership recognition society whose chief aim is to assist the Director of Bands in developing the leadership and enthusiasm that is required of the Georgia Tech Bands.

name: PianoForte
description:
PianoForte (the Student Pianist Organization) is a student-run organization that was re-established in the Fall of 2018. We create a network for solo pianists on campus and works towards increasing practice, performance, and developmental opportunities for members.

_

Our executive board is actively working towards increasing practice opportunities for the pianist community by seeking access to inaccessible, on-campus pianos and also by sourcing piano donations to bring to Tech, such as the recent installment of the West Village piano, as well as maximizing performance opportunities through collaborations with the community at Tech and around campus, such as our concerts at the senior community in Midtown's Lutheran Towers highlighting one pianist every week.

_

Our long-term hopes in creating this organization are to serve as the ultimate resource for anyone who plays, wishes to play, or enjoys listening to the piano and to enrich the culture of Georgia Tech by working with Administration and Faculty to both disperse publicly-accessible pianos throughout campus and establish recitals and concerts for the community.
summary: PianoForte (the Student Pianist Organization) is a student-run organization that was re-established in the Fall, 2018. It creates a network for solo pianists on campus and works towards increasing accessible practice opportunities for its members. Our...

name: Erato
description: ERATO is Georgia Tech's Literature and Arts magazine. Students are responsible for designing and publishing a journal each spring that showcases various types of writing and artwork submitted by members of the GT community. Staff meetings are held in the Student Center room 2150 (Student Media Office). Visit erato.gatech.edu or @erato.gt on Instagram for submission and event information. Send any questions to eratojournal@gmail.com.

description: Wreck is Georgia Tech’s incredibly awesome Women’s Club Ultimate Frisbee team. We are a mix of former and current soccer, softball, basketball and volleyball players, track runners, swimmers, sailors, and people who have never played an organized sport before. We are open to anyone who is looking to have some fun with a super cool group of girls while playing an organized sport. We compete with teams from all over the nation and have practice two to three times a week.

We upload dance covers on YouTube, participate in multiple performances on campus, and hold multiple workshops and showcases!

name: Pi Kappa Alpha
description: Currently the largest international Fraternity, Pi Kappa Alpha (Pike) was founded in 1868 on the premise of recruiting Scholars, Leaders, Athletes, and Gentlemen. Each Pike brother seeks to excel in every aspect of his life, whether it be at work, on the field, or in the classroom. With a strong focus on academic achievement, the chapter provides a multitude of resources, from used textbooks to in-house tutoring. Furthermore, the chapter has a long-standing tradition of athletic dominance in Greek Intramural Sports, specifically in football, soccer, and basketball. Joining the Georgia Tech chapter of Pi Kappa Alpha opens new doors to student organizations and other Greek organizations such as sororities and honor fraternities. Over the past four years, Pi Kappa Alpha at Georgia Tech has had mixers with all CPC social sororities on campus, in addition to having one member win Mr. Georgia Tech 2014. Brothers also currently hold executive positions in student-run organizations such as Order of Omega Honors Fraternity, Interfraternity Council, Habitat for Humanity, and Consult Your Community. Each initiated member gains access to an alumni network over 270,000 strong, united under the bonds of brotherhood. Not to be underestimated, this network has helped many of our recent alumni find both internship and work opportunities. For Pi Kappa Alpha, fraternal brotherhood is a lifelong commitment. The fraternity truly believes that the collegiate years are just the first chapter in friendships that will transcend time.
summary: Currently the largest international Fraternity, Pi Kappa Alpha (Pike) was founded in 1868 on the premise of recruiting Scholars, Leaders, Athletes, and Gentlemen. Each Pike brother seeks to excel in every aspect of his life, whether it be at work, on t...

name: China Care Club
description: We are a registered nonprofit student-run org that is dedicated to helping Chinese adoptees in the metro-Atlanta area connect with their birth heritage! Our monthly Playgroup programming is a great way for volunteers to contribute to a good cause, meet new people, and have fun. We focus mainly on arts-and-crafts, games, and other fun interactive experiences with the kids! We also raise funds to support the early care and education of vulnerable children in China, Vietnam, Mongolia, & Hong Kong SAR. In addition, we try to have monthly social events throughout the year for our members to connect with one another as well and host at least one major cultural event each semester (Cure Your Cravings, China Care Carnival) for the entire campus community in order to promote our cause. If you want to invest in our local AND global community, join China Care! :)
summary: We are a student organization dedicated to raising funds for vulnerable children in Asia. We also volunteer with local adopted Chinese children to help them connect with their Chinese heritage through monthly Playgroups!

name: Runnin' Wreck

Runnin' Wreck provides a friendly running community to runners of all ability levels, offering many different types of runs: group runs, speed workouts, and long runs, and races at either Atlanta Track Club Events or NIRCA races.

We meet every Monday, Wednesday, and Friday at 5:30 across the street from the CRC, and we have workouts on Tuesdays and Thursdays at 5:30 that meet at McCamish Pavilion. Feel free to come run with us any time!

Fall 2025 GroupMe: Click here to join! Password is Fall25

The Counseling Center accomplishes its mission by offering services that facilitate students’ personal development, assist in the alleviation, remediation, and prevention of distress, as well as services that educate students in ways that develop self-awareness, self-reliance, and self-confidence.
