name: Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers
description:
We are the largest professional undergraduate Industrial Engineering organization in the entire nation with over 300 active members. Our aim is to positively influence the experience of students in the IE community by providing unique professional development, technical development, and other developmental and social opportunities.

 

GT IISE is most known for our Career Fairs where we have 80+ companies recruiting GT ISYE students, company info sessions, Six Sigma & Lean certifications, Tableau & Excel Workshops, Scholarships, Conferences, Spring Picnic, and more!

 

 

Join IISE at Georgia Tech: https://forms.gle/cDFz7zeKq5XRtF2F7

Check our Instagram for latest updates: https://www.instagram.com/gtiise/

Join our Slack Group: iisegeorgiatech.slack.com

Become an IISE National member (benefits include early access to our career fairs, discounted certification rates, opportunity to attend conferences and join Exec): iise.org/Details/?id=560
summary: Welcome to the Georgia Tech chapter of the Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers! We are the premier student organization that provides Industrial Engineering students with professional, technical, and social opportunities.

name: First-Year Activities Board
description:
First-Year Activities Board, or FAB, strives to build leadership in our members through planning and promoting social events and services that are geared towards creating a shared first year experience. Although the name FAB implies only a devotion to the first year class, FAB's impact on campus reaches far beyond the First-Year Experience Program. FAB is a community open to everyone and is filled with bright, enthusiastic first year students who set out to make Georgia Tech a better place.

 

Our team of professionals and student executive leaders emphasize teamwork, communication skills, budget management, planning, and marketing techniques to engage students at Georgia Tech. Members are developed into campus leaders through planning successful events, interacting in a teamwork environment, and collaborating with other organizations. They take the leadership skills gained in FAB and apply them to a diverse range of student organizations throughout the rest of their college careers, leaving behind a lasting impact on campus.

 

Want to apply? FAB is on the FLO Common Application! Head to https://b.gatech.edu/39FyEbQ to access information about all FLOs. Feel free to check our social media platforms for more up to date information on applications and all things FAB.

 

Have any questions or want to learn more? Feel free to reach out to any of our student advisors below, and visit our social media!
summary: First-Year Activities Board, or FAB, strives to build leadership in our members through planning and promoting social events and services that are geared towards creating a shared first year experience...

name: First-Year Leadership Initiative
description:
First-Year Leadership Initiative (FLI) is the Georgia Tech Student Foundation (GTSF)'s first-year leadership organization (FLO). Selected from a competitive pool of applicants, FLI members learn and apply the Student Foundation's tenet of philanthropy, moving forward by giving back. From exclusive professional development events to project management to community building events within both FLI and the rest of GTSF, FLI puts its participants on the path to become significant contributors to the Georgia Tech community while also bringing strong student leaders together.

 

Members contribute largely to campus-wide events such as Thanks-for-Giving by creating and planning educational activities for attendees, and are placed into a small group that both participates in fun events happening around Atlanta and works on lower-scale events to continue the conversation and appreciation of philanthropy around Georgia Tech. They also participate in programming designed to prepare students for their professional life and are assigned a mentor within GTSF to guide and advise FLIers through their transition into life at Georgia Tech. 

 

FLI meetings are on Monday nights at 7pm at the Alumni House
summary: Freshmen Leadership Initiative (FLI) is the Georgia Tech Student Foundation (GTSF)'s first-year leadership organization (FLO). FLI members learn and apply the Student Foundation's tenets of philanthropy, community and professional preparedness.

name: Phi Kappa Psi
description: The Phi Kappa Psi Fraternity engages men of integrity, further develops their intellect and enhances community involvement. With a legacy built on acceptance and trust, each brother realizes his highest potential through a lifelong experience of service and excellence.
summary: The Phi Kappa Psi Fraternity engages men of integrity, further develops their intellect and enhances community involvement. With a legacy built on acceptance and trust, each brother realizes his highest potential through a lifelong experience of servic...

name: Barbell Club
description: Barbell Club is a supportive community of dedicated fitness enthusiasts focused on supporting athletes of all skill levels and barbell related disciplines. We're comprised of males and females who look to aid and support each other in reaching their goals safely and efficiently. Our club powerlifting team competes in 2-3 USAPL meets a year where top athletes can qualify for Collegiate Nationals which are held once a year. You do not have to be an elite athlete to join our powerlifting team! Several of the members that compete in these meets are first time competitors. Our club is not just geared towards those that enjoy powerlifting. As stated in our club motto, we are looking to build a supportive community of dedicated gym goers different from that of any university. We have members in our organization who compete in Olympic weightlifting, crossfit, strongman, bodybuilding, etc. Our non-competitive tier is also big! We are an organization that is welcoming to new and veteran gym goers. Several novice trainees develop a passion for fitness by going through our mentorship program and having an experienced club member instruct them in whatever their goals might be.
summary: Barbell Club @ GT is a supportive community of dedicated fitness enthusiasts focused on supporting athletes of all skill levels and barbell-related disciplines. We welcome any and all who look to support each other in reaching their goals!

name: The Navigators
description:
Welcome! We are the Navigators, an interdenominational Christian organization that focuses on loving Jesus Christ and helping others get to know Him. 

We meet every Tuesday at 7 p.m. in the Alumni House for a joint Bible study and "Nav Night". One of our cornerstones is our Bible study, where we gather to dig into God’s word together. The studies are an opportunity for authentic learning, for asking questions, and for journeying closer with Christ. Our Nav Night is a time to worship God together in many different forms: music, prayer, discussion, messages, and more. We also organize many extracurricular hangouts, like shared meals, outdoor activities, and game nights. 

You are welcome here, no matter if you’ve grown up in the church or have never attended in your life. We look forward to meeting you this semester and if you’d like to get connected with us, fill out this form here, and check out our Instagram, Facebook, and website linked below!
summary: Welcome! We are the Navigators, a nondenominational Christian organization that focuses on loving Jesus Christ and helping others get to know Him. We meet every Tuesday at 7 p.m. for a joint Bible study and Nav Night...

name: Indonesian Student Association
description:
The Indonesian Student Association (ISA) at Georgia Tech is a student-run organization founded in 1996.

Annually, the organization participates in a series of on-campus events to raise awareness of the Indonesian culture.

The organization also hosts a string of events that brings the Indonesian community at Georgia Tech closer together.

Outside of Georgia Tech, we are also known as PERMIAS Georgia Tech; a chapter of PERMIAS Nasional since it's re-establishment in 2013.

At its core - we are here to connect, to facilitate, and to serve.
summary: We are a cultural non-profit organization aimed at promoting Indonesian culture in Georgia Tech and the Atlanta community, as well as providing support for all Indonesian students in Georgia Tech.

name: Westminster Christian Fellowship
description:
Westminster Christian Fellowship (WCF) is a student-run religious organization. We're about:

 

Welcoming Diversity

Loving Authenticity

Promoting Growth

 

In order to follow those values our events include dinners with discussions, worship, and small groups. We also participate in campus outreach as well as service to our broader community.

 

Our regular events are:

Dinner and Discussion: Wednesdays @ 6:30 pm

 

Stop by the building at 724 Techwood Drive, check out our website gt-wcf.org to see how to get involved, or follow us on Instagram @gtwcf!
summary: Westminster Christian Fellowship (WCF) is a student-run religious organization. Our mission is "Love and serve God. Love and serve others."

name: Chinese Student Association
description:
We, the Chinese Student Association, are committed to creating an atmosphere where Chinese, Chinese-Americans, and all those interested in Chinese culture can come together. From organizing small get-togethers to large-scale events integrated with college and community life, CSA allows students to meet others to whom they can relate in various community, cultural, and social settings.

As an awareness and promoting organization, CSA also addresses and discusses issues concerning Chinese and Chinese-Americans as students and as a community.

While Chinese Student Association is devoted to Chinese culture and community within the university, it welcomes and encourages people of all race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, and national origin to participate in, contribute to, and become part of CSA.
summary: We, the Chinese Student Association, are committed to creating an atmosphere where Chinese, Chinese-Americans, and all those interested in Chinese culture can come together. We welcome and encourage people of all backgrounds to be a part of CSA!

name: Chi Alpha
description: Our mission is to Engage students with the Gospel and the truth of Jesus Christ, to Equip students to evangelize to their friends and classmates, bringing the Good News to wherever they go, and to Encourage each other in the faith, through discipleship times, life groups, and worship services.
summary: Our goal is to connect college students to God, each other and the world by Inspiring them through Faith, Hope and Love. We do this by way of prayer, worship, fellowship, discipleship, and mission.

name: National Organization of Minority Architecture Students
description: A Student Chapter of a Professional Organization We the students of the Georgia Tech Chapter of the National Organization of Minority of Architects have come together to enhance our education, network with professionals, and begin to define solutions which will ensure a healthy living and working environment for the total community. We find, as minorities studying architecture, a community of purpose and cultural experience that warrants our combined efforts in the advancement of our future profession, our respective activities in it, and the needs of the communities we will serve. Minorities in architecture are qualified to provide professional services in all areas of our environment, but because of their particular sensitivity to the minority community; they have a unique qualification to provide services and solve problems therein. By consolidating our thinking, economic power, political power and other resources, our ability to achieve these goals is greatly enhanced. To these ends, we establish this organization which is built on the bonds of the common professional interests that brings us together; and equally, on the bonds of friendship and fraternity that will sustain and enrich our association. We have organized to build our knowledge and expertise, and join hands with other organized disciplines to prepare to address issues confronting our communities.
summary: A Student Chapter of NOMA. We the students of the Georgia Tech Chapter of NOMAS have come together to enhance our education, network with professionals, and begin to define solutions that will enhance the architectural world.

name: Grace House
description:
Grace House provides students with an opportunity for friendship, direction, an authentic community, service, and a home away from home. Grace House is an open and affirming Christian organization, fully living in our Baptismal covenant. We seek to serve Christ in ALL people, love our neighbors as ourselves, and strive for justice and peace among ALL people, and respect the dignity of EVERY human being.

 

All means y'all, and all y'all are made in God's image. We are a community of queer people, people of color, and people of varying abilities, and we value and welcome people of all races, origins, gender identities, sexual orientations, socioeconomic standings, family statuses, abilities, and faiths.

 

Our 3 Values:

  1. Radical Love: We openly and vocally express the radical love of God through practicing inclusivity and hospitality and seeking justice for all.
  2. Safe Inquiry: We value open conversations, inquiry into inclusive justice, and safe conversations.
  3. Inclusive Justice: We seek justice for all God’s creatures, especially the poor and marginalized, working actively with them and reflecting the work of God in their lives. Our goal is to make every single person feel welcome and understood as they are.
summary: Grace Coffee House is a joint venture of the Episcopalian and Lutheran ministry at Georgia Tech. ALL are welcome.

name: MBA Consulting Club
description: For full-time MBA students who relish a challenge, who know how to find answers, and who just want to make things work, the Scheller MBA Consulting Club offers you a chance to develop your skill set and gain exposure to alumni and organizations who are looking for talents such as yours. By joining the Consulting Club, you will have a chance to learn how to crack case interviews, join alumni networking events, and get advice from fellow MBA students on how to tailor your consulting resume and get through the consulting interview process. Activities: Consulting Panel (Listen to fellow MBA candidates talk about their internships at various consulting companies and get tips on how to get in) Case Interview Prep (Practice for case interviews with fellow MBAs, learn to deal with different frameworks, and learn to handle case interviews with ease) Coffee Chats (Speak with consulting company alumni and recruiters and get to know your target companies more) Company Visits (Go on-site to your target company and get a feel of the kind of environment you hope to work in) Alumni Networking (Network with various alumni from the different companies you wish to join and work with them to get to know your prospects) Resume Review (Work with club members to review and customize your resume for your target company)
summary: For MBA students (and other Graduate students) interested in learning about consulting and the opportunities it provides. Members gain exposure to alumni and organizations looking for Scheller graduates and receive interview preparation for consulting.

name: Men's Club Volleyball
description:
We are an intercollegiate club volleyball team that travels around the Southeast to compete in around five tournaments each semester with matches against college and adult teams. At the end of the year, we compete in a national tournament with the best volleyball clubs from around the country. We're an indoor team but our guys also play a little grass & sand. Our goal is to develop great volleyball players and grow the game!

Check out our website! (https://sites.gatech.edu/mensclubvball/)
summary: We are an intercollegiate club volleyball team that travels around the Southeast to compete in around ten tournaments each semester with matches against college and adult teams. We're an indoor team but our guys also play a little sand.

name: Residence Hall Association
description: The hundreds of events in and around all 49 residence halls across campus every year, the voice of thousands of residents, and one of the leading student leadership organizations at Tech—that’s us, and if you live on campus, you’re a part of it! The Residence Hall Association (RHA) is the second largest student organization on campus, and we’re made possible by the over 8,000 residents who live in Georgia Tech Housing. We help breathe life into Tech traditions and classic events—like Homecoming, Intramurals, and Week of Welcome—and provide funding for residents, RAs, Hall Directors, and other student organizations to help build community and make our halls into homes. In years past, we’ve been the voice behind the integration of the Menstrual Product Program into residence halls, the creation of a redesigned maintenance request portal, and the adoption of free laundry for our residents.
summary: RHA is the second largest student organization at Tech, with nearly 9,000 on-campus residents and members and an annual budget of over $250,000. Join RHA to engage in leadership, community building, event planning, advocacy, and more!

name: CRU
description: We are a caring and supportive community passionate about connecting students to Jesus. Our vision is to develop spiritual leaders to truly know and follow Jesus Christ at Georgia Tech, in Atlanta, and throughout the world. Feel free to join our GroupMe at https://groupme.com/join_group/67416241/eVbPGRvO for all updates on events.
summary: We are a caring and supportive community passionate about connecting students to Jesus. Our vision is to develop spiritual leaders to truly know and follow Jesus Christ at Georgia Tech, in Atlanta, and throughout the world.

name: Tekstyles
description:
Tekstyles is Georgia Tech's premier hip hop dance club. The club operates with the goal of promoting the Atlanta dance scene on Georgia Tech's campus. The only requirements are an interest in dance and a desire to improve, learn, and share what you know. All styles of dance are welcome from breaking, hip hop, popping, locking, house, krumping, choreography, etc.
Practice Schedule: https://calendar.google.com/calendar/u/0/embed?src=gttekstyles@gmail.com&ctz=America/New_York&fbclid=IwAR0iYj-T4kNJw2dlgiV8KK9OEm4yTf5B-y-2ykudK-Z4Lgesxzc0J2CVu68

For the most updated information on our club, message our Instagram @gttekstyles or join or discord by messaging our Instagram page:

https://www.instagram.com/gttekstyles/?hl=en
summary: We are a community of hip hop dancers who promote the Atlanta hip hop dance community within Georgia Tech. From breakdancing to choreography, all styles of dance are appreciated and practiced in Tekstyles.

name: Christian Students
description:
Atlanta Christian Students at Georgia Tech is a student group composed of believers in Christ from various backgrounds, and we stand absolutely for the faith common to all Christians. We love the Lord Jesus Christ, endeavor to give Him “the first place in all things” (Col. 1:18), and have the utmost respect for His Word. We meet simply as Christians without any denominational affiliation because we treasure the testimony of the oneness of the Body of Christ, and our desire is to cooperate with Him for the accomplishment of God’s eternal purpose (Eph. 3:11) by ministering the unsearchable riches of Christ (Eph. 3:18) for the building up of the Body of Christ (Eph. 4:12).

If you are interested in joining, please fill out this form: Interest Form
summary: Atlanta Christian Students at Georgia Tech is a student group composed of believers in Christ from various backgrounds, and we stand absolutely for the faith common to all Christians. We love the Lord Jesus Christ, endeavor to give Him “the first place…

name: Women's Rugby
description:
As a club, we compete in 15s and 7s rugby against other colleges in Georgia and surrounding states. Fall and spring are both competitive seasons with some friendlies. Fall we play 15s, spring we plays 7s. Our main focus is building a foundation for women's rugby at an intermediate level. Many of our players go on to play for women's league teams like our local teams: ATL 2.0 Selects and Atlanta Harlequins. We always welcome new players, regardless of previous experience. Motivation and commitment are all you need, so come on out and join us!

Spring 2026 PRACTICE SCHEDULE:

Monday, 6-8 pm at Couch South Field (Burger Bowl)

Wednesday, 7-9 pm at Roe Stamps Field 4
summary: We compete in 15s and 7s rugby against other colleges in Georgia and surrounding states during the fall and spring semesters. Fall and spring are both competitive seasons with some friendlies. Fall we play 15s, spring we plays 7s.

name: Board Game Club
description:
Georgia Tech's premiere board gaming club. We have regular open board gaming sessions in the student center. Hope to see you there! We ask that people do not take any components from our boxes of games, and that everyone respects each other. Join our Discord: https://discord.gg/vWAxqsSBAW

If you have read this far, go back and join the Discord. If you have any questions, you can message in the server or DM an officer. We check emails much less often than Discord.

If you have reached this point, go back 3 lines and join the Discord ^^^
summary: Georgia Tech's premiere board gaming club. We have regular open board gaming sessions in the student center.

name: Ramblin' Rocket Club
description:
Welcome to Ramblin' Rocket Club! Our mission is to enrich the education of students with their technical and leadership skills with projects ranging from competitive rocketry to high-altitude ballooning. We are made up of five individual teams: Georgia Tech Experimental Rocketry (GTXR), High Power Rocketry (HPR), High Altitude Balloonery (HAB), Guidance, Navigation, and Control (GNC), and Ramblin' Competition Rocketry (RCR). 

 

Introductory Video

 

Website: ramblinrocketclub.org

If you are a current member, please check our Slack to keep updated with current news & events. See you in G5!
summary: We are Georgia Tech's Amateur/Competitive Rocketry Club. Our mission is to enrich the education of students with their technical and leadership skills with projects ranging from competitive rocketry to high-altitude ballooning.

name: Journey Christian Fellowship
description: Welcome to Journey Christian Fellowship! We are a Christian campus ministry here at Georgia Tech that is a part of Journey Church of Atlanta. Whether you are curious about God or wanting to deepen your faith, Journey is a place all are welcome to call home. Find a spiritual community and discover God’s wonderful love for you by joining a Family Group—sign-ups for the upcoming year can be found here! For more details on our core beliefs and values, check out our church website by clicking on the icon below. Feel free to reach out to us via Engage, Facebook, or Instagram!We can't wait to meet you at our upcoming events! Check out our church Linktree for more information.
summary: Welcome to Journey Christian Fellowship! We are a Christian campus ministry here at Georgia Tech that is part of Journey Church of Atlanta (JCA). Whether you are curious about God or want to deepen your faith, JCA is a place where all are welcome.

name: Energy Club
description:
The Energy Club is a multidisciplinary club focused on energy education, engineering, and competition. We seek to inspire and prepare the next generation of individuals knowledgeable in the complex energy-related world. We strive to bring together the technology, policy, and business of the energy landscape to better understand its issues and contribute to the dialogue.

We host events where people in the energy industry can speak to our members, we support Energy-related engineering projects, we take part in energy-related collegiate competitions, we host the annual Southeastern Energy Conference in the Spring, and the Energy and Sustainability Career Fair in the Fall.

Fill out our Interest Form: https://forms.office.com/r/EGpgydaAEU

Join our Slack: https://join.slack.com/t/gatechenergyclub/shared_invite/zt-2p5eshl05-Q8pI4Ndes~9WcZGA35pgVQ
summary: The Energy Club is a professional club dedicated to promoting energy education, innovation, and sustainability. We aim to advance discussion about pressing energy challenges, engage diverse sectors to impact the energy industry, and foster a passion for

name: Men's Rugby
description: Rugby is the “ruffian sport played by gentlemen.” GTRFC, or the GT Rugby Football Club, is a student-run organization governed by students and coached by experienced rugby professionals. We practice 2-3 times per week with additional opportunities for weight training and conditioning. Academics always come first and the rugby team has consistently had one of the highest average GPA’s among club sports at GT. Our coaching staff is highly dedicated to our rookies and places a heavy emphasis on establishing the basic skills of rugby. We schedule numerous matches and scrimmages designed specifically to allow new players to get experience and learn the game. Many of our current varsity players had never played rugby prior to coming to Tech. Our club is self-governed by the members with funding from Student Government and Alumni. GT Rugby is famous for its social aspect with ample opportunities to bond with teammates, travel to other college campuses, across the Southeast. We compete throughout all three semesters.
summary: Rugby is the “ruffian sport played by gentlemen.” GTRFC, or the GT Rugby Football Club, is a student-run organization governed by students and coached by experienced rugby professionals. We practice 2-3 times per week with additional opportunities for ...

name: Women's Water Polo
description: The Georgia Tech Women's Water Polo team competes against other college club teams within the Southeast Division of the CWPA (Collegiate Water Polo Association) and is always welcoming new players, regardless of experience!
summary: The Georgia Tech Women's Water Polo team competes against other college club teams within the Southeast Division of the CWPA (Collegiate Water Polo Association) and is always welcoming new players, regardless of experience!

name: Chi Phi
description: The Chi Phi Fraternity is one of the oldest social fraternities in the country, and dates back to 1824 when three separate organizations bearing the name "Chi Phi" existed across the country, all with no knowledge of the others. In 1904, the Omega Chapter of the Chi Phi Fraternity was chartered at the Georgia Institute of Technology, and in 1924, the Omega Chapter officially got a new house located at 720 Fowler Street NW in Atlanta, GA. Here, we seek to Build Better Men who embody our three core values: Truth, Honor and Personal Integrity.
summary: The Chi Phi Fraternity found its origin in 1824, making it 200 years old. Having brothers hailing from Georgia, the US, and the world. Here you will meet people from all walks of life who embody our core values of Truth, Honor, and Personal Integrity

name: The Campus Kitchen Project
description:
Campus Kitchens is a volunteer organization dedicated to improving sustainability and reducing food waste on campus. We hold multiple weekly volunteering shifts to package leftover dining hall food and also pick up excess food from Greek houses, retail locations, and events on campus. This food, which is normally thrown away, goes to helping food-insecure students on campus through Klemis Kitchen and homeless individuals in the Atlanta area. 

[UPDATED] If you are interested in volunteering at shifts, please sign up here: https://www.signupgenius.com/go/10C0C45ABA728A3FFCF8-61790740-ckpspring#/

Additionally, our shifts are tentative based on food availability, so please keep an eye on your email for an announcement from SignUpGenius before the shift to confirm it! Happy volunteering!
summary: Campus Kitchens is a volunteer organization dedicated to improving sustainability and reducing food waste on campus. We hold weekly volunteering shifts on Tuesdays, Fridays, and Saturdays to package the leftover dining hall food.

name: Delta Chi
description: The Delta Chi fraternity at Georgia Tech is a chapter that promotes excellence and inclusion through our brotherhood of diverse and unique fraternity men. The Georgia Tech chapter of Delta Chi was founded by a group of men looking for an alternative Greek experience from what was offered at the time of our inception. Our founding members believed every action should be based on four principles. To this day, these principles determine the decisions we make as both a chapter and as individuals. These principles are that academics come first, that absolutely no hazing shall occur, that chivalry is not dead, and that friends are always welcome.
summary: The Delta Chi fraternity at Georgia Tech is a diverse and inclusive chapter founded to offer an alternative Greek experience, guided by core principles that emphasize academics, a strict no-hazing policy, chivalry, and an open, welcoming brotherhood.

name: Student Hospital Connections
description:
Founded in 2007, Student Hospital Connections is a pre-health organization focused on providing Atlanta-based clinical opportunities for students interested in the healthcare field. Our main purpose is to connect GT student to volunteering opportunities at local Atlanta hospitals, such as  Emory University Hospital Midtown, the Winship Cancer Institute, and other clinical and non-clinical opportunities within and surrounding Atlanta. We also aim to remove the financial burden associated with traveling to volunteering opportunities within Atlanta through our reimbursement and carpool program. As a growing organization, we are constantly doing our best to serve our members by improving events and member benefits. We strive to provide our members and the GT community the support needed to successfully venture out into the healthcare world! We host informational meetings where members can learn how to better navigate the pre-health landscape, fundraisers that go towards providing reimbursements and helping local causes, and casual social events. 

 

We have expanded our reach by partnering with the Good Samaritan Health Center, MedShare, and various other organizations in Atlanta, and have several committees to encourage member involvement and leadership. 

New Member registration is open year-round. Our committee applications open up at the start of each semester. Check out our video below to learn more about SHC and what we have to offer.

 

Learn More about SHC:

https://youtu.be/84t2boHsBEc 

 

Contact Information:

Email: shcgatech@gmail.com

Website: https://simplistic-face-707.notion.site/Student-Hospital-Connections-fa9538c19c3145e5b8faa74fcd1850ba

Instagram: @gatechshc
summary: SHC is a pre-health organization focused on providing Atlanta-based clinical opportunities for students interested in the healthcare field. We aim to remove some of the financial burden associated with traveling to clinical opportunities within Atlanta.

name: Society of Physics Students
description: The Society of Physics Students is a social and academic club that invites any Georgia Tech student with an interest in physics, mathematics, or science in general. Being a Physics major is not a requirement to join our organization. Our weekly meetings include talks from invited faculty, fun physics-based activities, and a variety of social events, most of which are at 5pm on Fridays. Check out what we've been up to on our website (we're very active)! We have a lounge space in room S203 of Howey. Membership in this club will serve to connect you with faculty and staff within the School of Physics, provide you with a wonderful social network of students with an interest in science, and enable you to explore potential career and research opportunities related to physics (such as discounts on trips to national research facilities!). We are also very active on Discord (invite link in Discussion section below)! Email us about joining!
summary: The Society of Physics Students is a social and academic club that invites any Georgia Tech student with an interest in physics, mathematics, or science in general. Being a Physics major is not a requirement to join our organization!

name: Alpha Delta Pi
description:
In 1971, our chapter of Alpha Delta Pi, referred to as Zeta Omicron, was founded at Georgia Tech. The chapter strives to better their own character, hold themselves to high educational standards, and strengthen their bonds of friendship through faith and loyalty which we live out through our motto: "We Live for Each Other."

Alpha Delta Pi’s colors are Azure Blue and White, our symbols are the Lion and Diamond, our flower is the Woodland Violet, and our philanthropy is Ronald McDonald House Charities.

Our mission is to provide women with a meaningful lifelong member experience that empowers them to reach their full potential through leadership, educational, philanthropic, and social opportunities.
summary: Alpha Delta Pi is one of the National Panhellenic sororities on Georgia Tech's campus. Alpha Delta Pi is committed to sisterhood, values and character, high academic standards, and service.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi
description: Kappa Alpha Psi , a college Fraternity, now comprised of functioning Undergraduate and Alumni Chapters on major campuses and in cities throughout the country, is the crystallization of a dream. It is the beautiful realization of a vision shared commonly by the late Revered Founders Elder Watson Diggs; John Milton Lee; Byron Kenneth Armstrong; Guy Levis Grant; Ezra Dee Alexander; Henry Tourner Asher; Marcus Peter Blakemore; Paul Waymond Caine; Edward Giles Irvin and George Wesley Edmonds. It was the vision of these astute men that enabled them in the school year 1910 - 11, more specifically the night of January 5, 1911, on the campus of Indiana University at Bloomington, Indiana, to sow the seed of a fraternal tree whose fruit is available to, and now enjoyed by, college men everywhere, regardless of their color, religion or national origin. It is a fact of which KAPPA ALPHA PSI is justly proud that the Constitution has never contained any clause which either excluded or suggested the exclusion of a man from membership merely because of his color, creed, or national origin. The Constitution of KAPPA ALPHA PSI is predicated upon, and dedicated to, the principles of achievement through a truly democratic Fraternity. Taken from: "A SHORT CHRONICLE OF KAPPA ALPHA PSI FRATERNITY"
summary: Kappa Alpha Psi, a college Fraternity, now comprised of functioning Undergraduate and Alumni Chapters on major campuses and in cities throughout the country, is the crystallization of a dream. It is the beautiful realization of a vision shared commonl...

name: Women's Transportation Seminar
description: Women's Transportation Seminar at Georgia Tech is an international organization dedicated to building the future of transportation through the global advancement of women. WTS-GT seeks to: (1) Give students interested in transportation-related professions opportunities to participate in professional activities and networking events that expose them to the transportation industry; (2) Offer forums for interaction between transportation professionals, faculty members, and undergraduate and graduate students interested in transportation-related careers; and (3) Provide forums for interaction and mentoring between students and the Atlanta chapter of Women’s Transportation Seminar.
summary: Women's Transportation Seminar at Georgia Tech is an international organization dedicated to building the future of transportation through the global advancement of women. WTS-GT seeks to: (1) Give students interested in transportation-related professi...

name: College Republicans
description:
We're a conservative group at Georgia Tech, committed to putting America first. Our mission includes fighting for the unborn, the American family, and the American way of life. Join us weekly for discussions and guest speakers. 

Get the latest updates here: https://groupme.com/join_group/67101174/9XSXzsGG
summary:
We're a conservative group at Georgia Tech, committed to putting America first. Join us weekly for discussions and guest speakers. 

Get the latest updates here: https://groupme.com/join_group/67101174/9XSXzsGG

name: Georgia Outreach: Advancing the Lives of the Special-needs
description: GOALSoccer is a philanthropy organization that is partnered with the Special Olympics. We work to enrich the lives of children with special needs through the sport of soccer. Through our Buddy program, we partner children one-on-one with a student volunteer, who acts as a mentor, a coach, and a best bud! Our goal is to provide these children with the opportunity to experience the value of team sports, learn a new game, and make life long friends.
summary: GOALSoccer is a philanthropy organization that is partnered with the Special Olympics to enrich the lives of children with special needs through the sport of soccer.

name: The Hive
description:
Please DO NOT request to join through Engage.

 

The Hive is a community where ideas, innovation, and creativity come to life. We work to cultivate a multidisciplinary culture of student makers and designers who are passionate about learning new skills, helping other students, and above all, creating, by providing an easily accessible engineering space. All Georgia Tech students, regardless of year or major, are allowed to come inside and utilize the three floor makerspace, free of charge.

 

In order to become more involved in our organization, join the mailing list, come to the space during open hours, or apply to be a Peer Instructor! Peer Instructor applications are released via email at the end of every semester, to begin volunteering the following semester.

 

Check out our website at hive.ece.gatech.edu and come follow us on Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn!
summary: The Hive is the student organization responsible for running the Interdisciplinary Design Commons.

name: Roller Hockey
description: We are the club Roller Hockey team at Georgia Tech. We are a relatively relaxed club and are always looking for new members of any skill level. We generally practice once a week in the CRC auxiliary gym and occasionally play games against other schools. There is no minimum time requirement for joining - you can attend practices/games whenever they fit in your schedule.
summary: We are the club Roller Hockey team at Georgia Tech. We are a relatively relaxed club and are always looking for new members of any skill level. We generally practice once a week in the CRC auxiliary gym and occasionally play games against other schools.

name: Phi Delta Theta
description: The Georgia Delta chapter of Phi Delta Theta was founded on June 11th, 1902, and is one of the oldest, most well-respected fraternities on Georgia Tech's campus. Phi Delta Theta prides itself on being a fraternity that provides a culture of strong brotherhood, excellence in scholarship, and high character. Phi Delts are involved in activities throughout campus from the varsity football team to student government. The chapter continues to excel in the classroom due to an emphasis of academics. The strength of the bond of brothers is what makes the chapter unique, and will continue to make Phi Delta Theta a distinguished presence at Georgia Tech.
summary: Founded in 1902, Georgia Tech's Phi Delta Theta fosters brotherhood, academic excellence, and high character. Members excel in campus activities like sports and student government, maintaining a proud, distinguished presence on campus.

name: New Student and Transition Programs
description: The Office of New Student and Transition Programs supports new and continuing student success in the Georgia Tech community through a wide range of programs. These initiatives focus on fostering the transition and engagement of students in the Tech community: FASET Orientation, Wreck Camp, Transition Programs, Connect First-Year Leadership Organization, Transfer Programs, and Leadership Opportunities.
summary: The Office of New Student and Transition Programs supports new and continuing student success in the Georgia Tech community through a wide range of programs.

name: Materials Research Society
description: MRS is a national organization devoted to promoting and advancing materials science. As stated by the national organization, "the Materials Research Society (MRS) is an organization of materials researchers worldwide that promotes communication for the advancement of interdisciplinary materials research and technology to improve the quality of life." As the Georgia Tech chapter, we seek to host events that adhere to this mission, including inviting speakers, putting on networking events, and working as a resource for graduate students. This organization is aimed towards graduate students, whereas Materials Advantage is aimed towards undergraduates.
summary: MRS is a national organization devoted to promoting and advancing materials science. As stated by the national organization, "the Materials Research Society (MRS) is an organization of materials researchers worldwide that promotes communication for the...

name: Kappa Alpha Order
description: Kappa Alpha Order remains a strong brotherhood by embodying the ideal upon which it was founded. This ideal was the chivalrous manhood personified by the gentlemen with his kindness, generosity, sense of personal honor, respect for women, and unflinching courage in the face of overwhelming odds.
summary: Kappa Alpha Order remains a strong brotherhood by embodying the ideal upon which it was founded. This ideal was the chivalrous manhood personified by the Southern gentlemen with his kindness, generosity, honor, respect for women, and reverence for god.

name: Student Neuroscience Association
description: Student Neuroscience Association is dedicated to fostering student interest in neuroscience through social events, mentor mentee program, volunteering, dissections, student speaker events, and industry speaker events. If you would like to find out more about us and what we do, please join our GroupMe https://groupme.com/join_group/89509391/LCTIiVqH and follow our Instagram @gtbsneuro (https://www.instagram.com/gtbsneuro/)!
summary: The Student Neuroscience Association at GT is dedicated to fostering student interest in neuroscience through volunteering, dissections, visits to local neuro-related research centers, speaker events, and social events.

name: American Institute of Architecture Students
description: The American Institute of Architecture Students (AIAS) is an independent, nonprofit, student-run organization dedicated to providing unmatched progressive programs, information, and resources on issues critical to architecture and the experience of education. The mission of the AIAS is to promote excellence in architectural education, training, and practice; to foster an appreciation of architecture and related disciplines; to enrich communities in a spirit of collaboration; and to organize students and combine their efforts to advance the art and science of architecture. Our Georgia Tech chapter is primarily devoted to connecting our members with invaluable resources that will enhance their growth as students and future professionals: firm contacts, architectural tools, seminars, resume and portfolio crits, and other events. We are also devoted to strengthening our local communities by providing no-charge design and labor services.
summary: The American Institute of Architecture Students (AIAS) is an independent, nonprofit, student-run organization dedicated to providing programs, information, and resources on issues critical to architectural education and the architectural profession.

name: Graduate Business Council
description: The purpose of the GBC is to: A. Serve as the student government organization for the full-time MBA program at Georgia Tech. B. To promote social and professional interaction among current students, alumni, faculty, staff, and all other potential stakeholders. C. To address student concerns and facilitate the rectification of any student issues in a professional and timely manner by serving as the MBA student body representative to faculty, staff, administration, etc. D. To oversee and handle the administration of the GBC budget in a responsible and forthright manner to handle duties of the GBC such as such social events, alumni events, intramurals, etc.
summary: The purpose of the GBC is to: A. Serve as the student government organization for the full-time MBA program at Georgia Tech. B. To promote social and professional interaction among current students, alumni, faculty, staff, and all other potential stakeh.

name: Hindu Youth for Unity, Virtues, and Action
description: Hindu YUVA (Youth for Unity, Virtues, and Action) aims to provide a platform to preserve, practice, promote, and protect Dharma. Hindu YUVA holds weekly meetings, where we have team-building games, discussions, yoga, and presentations. We also hold events on campus and celebrate Hindu festivals with the campus community, such as Ganesh Chathurthi, Diwali, Shivaratri, Holi, and more!
summary: Hindu YUVA (Youth for Unity, Virtues, and Action) holds weekly meetings, where we have yoga, team-building games, discussions, workshops and presentations.

name: Undergraduate Consulting Club
description:
Georgia Tech Undergraduate Consulting Club is an organization that is dedicated to creating a platform for students passionate about consulting. Established in the 1970s, GT UCC has established a community of talented students and created a network to help students enter into prestigious consulting firms. By providing students an opportunity to become involved in hands-on pro bono projects, members of UCC are able to gain relevant consulting experience. Over the years, we have also established strong relationships with top consulting firms. The three pillars of the organization, networking, case preparation, and pro bono projects, propel our success. Throughout the semester, our members receive intensive interviewing preparation, enjoy the exclusive networking opportunities that are offered only to UCC and participate in challenging yet meaningful pro bono projects.

Learn more by checking out our website or finding us on Instagram @ucc_gt
summary: The Undergraduate Consulting Club is the oldest & most prestigious consulting club on campus. It offers a wide variety of pro-bono projects, networking events, case competitions, and trainings that give educational, hands-on experience to the industry.

name: Sigma Alpha Epsilon
description: Georgia Phi Chapter of Sigma Alpha Epsilon
summary: Georgia Phi of Chapter of Sigma Alpha Epsilon

name: Astronomy Club
description: Promoting amateur astronomy on Tech’s campus and its neighborhood. We meet every Monday at 7:30pm in the Howey Physics building, room announced in our discord, and go to the observatory on the roof (weather permitting). Our other activities include camping overnight at Deerlick Astronomy Village, scheduling visits to the Fernbank Planetarium, movies, solar observing, participating in local astronomy camping events, hearing from guest lecturers, and participating in miscellaneous other space-related events throughout the year. Check out our website for more information! You can also find our other social links including one to the Discord server where all announcements are sent.
summary: Promoting amateur astronomy on Tech’s campus and its neighborhood. We meet every Monday at 7:30pm in Howey, and go to the GT observatory (weather permitting). We participate in an assortment of other astronomy activities during the semester.

description: This portal serves the Division of Student Life's internal programs and events. Membership is limited to current professional staff members.

name: Triathlon Club
description: We are a cohesive, welcoming, and supportive team that encourages everyone to use the sport of triathlon to make the most of their time at Georgia Tech. We compete in the SECTC, generally with 3 events per semester, plus a conference and a national championship. We are open to athletes of all skill levels and are grad student friendly.
summary: We compete in the SECTC. We generally compete in 3 events per semester, plus a conference and a national championship.

The Marksmanship Club provides the opportunity to discuss, learn about and practice marksmanship and firearms. We strongly welcome beginners and masters alike.

Legacy Website Operational

name: Society of Plastics Engineers
description:
The objective of the society is to promote the scientific and engineering knowledge relating to plastics. SPE is the largest, most well-known plastics professional society in the world. We are the professional "home" for nearly 16,000 plastics professionals around the globe. For more than 70 years we've been the "go to" technical information, training, networking, and knowledge sharing source for plastics professionals who want to advance their knowledge and their careers.

Connect with us here on Engage, and on GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/69245292/A5xxfGb1
summary: The Society of Plastics Engineers provides undergraduate and graduate students at GT with an opportunity to participate in a professional society which provides networking opportunities and scientific and engineering knowledge related to polymers

name: Sigma Nu
description: Founded in 1896, the Gamma Alpha Chapter of the Sigma Nu Fraternity is one of the oldest fraternities on Georgia Tech's Campus. Our brotherhood is a close-knit group brought together by the common values of love, truth, and honor. We take pride in our philanthropic endeavors, academic success, and brotherhood functions. We are a brotherhood active in all sectors of campus life. We take pride in doing our best at all we attempt, out of respect for each other and those that came before us.
summary: Sigma Nu identifies itself by providing members with a socially enhanced Georgia Tech experience. As a larger social fraternity, we believe our brothers are able to get the most out of Tech, both socially and academically.

name: Brazilian Student Association
description: The Brazilian Student Association (BRASA) was created to promote Brazilian culture by uniting the Brazilian community at Georgia Tech and introducing our traditions and customs to the greater campus community. BRASA at Georgia Tech also aims to provide academic and professional development opportunities by establishing contact between its members and various companies in Brazil and in the United States.
summary: The Brazilian Student Association at Georgia Tech

description: SSO is a student organization focused on giving students an opportunity to practice their Spanish, make new friends, and learn about Latin culture in fun and engaging ways. We will have several events throughout the semester. Previous events include Hispanic movie nights, Spanish coffee hours, and game nights!
summary: SSO is a student organization focused on giving students an opportunity to practice their Spanish, make new friends, and learn about Latin culture in fun and engaging ways. All are welcome, no matter your level of Spanish!

name: HyTech Racing
description:
At HyTech Racing, our mission is to build the next generation of engineers through the pursuit of performance as a tool. In this process we equip every student with a deep understanding of all aspects of vehicle design and development. Each member of our team has a chance to learn about all components of vehicle design and production through research, mechanical and electrical design, machining, component sourcing, project management, manufacturing and testing.

 

If you're interested in joining, please request to join our Microsoft Teams for new members (on our linktree).

 

HyTech Racing welcomes all majors and experience levels! For more information, email us at gthytech@gmail.com .
summary: HyTech Racing is an award-winning electric racing team at Georgia Tech. We design, build, and race a new electric vehicle each year and participate in annual FSAE competitions with teams from other prestigious engineering universities around the world.

name: Aarohi
description:
Aarohi is Georgia Tech's society for the Indian classical arts. We promote artistic expression and cultural diversity & awareness on campus by fostering and showcasing music and dance art forms. We host shows and concerts as well as a variety of member events with the aim of building community, cultivating talent, and providing a space for artistic & cultural learning. 

You can join us through our membership form, and you can check out our YouTube channel, website, and social media to keep up with everything we're doing! Please feel free to reach us at aarohigt@gmail.com.

name: Society of Women in Business
description: It is our mission as Society of Women in Business (“SWIB”) to foster the empowerment of women in the workplace through establishment of corporate connections and enhancement of business knowledge. As a powerful networking organization, SWIB will give its members the confidence and tools needed for them to succeed in an ever-changing and competitive business world. Our organization allows women to achieve their goals, think strategically, and emerge into the capable leaders who will flourish in all of their future endeavors, whatever they may be. We are the elite business women of Georgia Tech and we are ready to excel in all that we do both professionally and personally.
