name: Humans vs. Zombies
description:
Humans vs Zombies is a four-day long game of tag across campus. Players start out as human, except for a select few who start as zombies. If a zombie tags a human, they become a zombie. Humans can stun zombies for a short time by hitting them with socks, marshmallows, or Nerf blasters. 

Spring 2026 game week will be April 1-4. Stay tuned for updates!

For updates and information on the game, join our Discord server.
summary: Humans vs Zombies is a week long game of tag across campus.  Players start out as human, except for a select few who start as zombies.  If a zombie tags a human, they become a zombie.  Humans can stun zombies for a short time by hitting them with socks...

name: Ice Hockey Club
description: Georgia Tech Ice Hockey, founded in 1973, is the longest continuously running college hockey team in the South. Part of the ACCHL conference and under the ACHA M2 league, the team plays in 20+ regular-season weekend games all across America from August to February, with potential playoff games in March. The team hosts 2 on-ice practices a week and organizes occasional off-ice training, giving the players an excellent balance between athletics and academics while offering friendships to be kept for a lifetime.
summary: Georgia Tech Ice Hockey, founded in 1973, is the longest continuously run college hockey team in the South. Part of the ACCHL, the team plays in 20+ regular-season weekend games all across America from August to February, with playoff games in March.

name: Phi Kappa Sigma
description:
Phi Kappa Sigma, or Skullhouse, is a brotherhood committed to lifelong growth and membership. Our fraternity was founded in 1850 to develop Men of Honor. We pride ourselves in our unique membership with a diverse group of brothers involved in various interests, majors, and skills. We are unified through our strong ties of brotherhood and through our core values of Trust, Honor, Respect, Knowledge, Wisdom, Responsibility, and Integrity.

Visit our website, gtskulls.org and email our Recruitment Chair, Vaughn Coston, at chi@gtskulls.org for more information about Spring Rush!
summary: Phi Kappa Sigma, or Skullhouse, is a brotherhood committed to lifelong growth and membership. Our fraternity was founded in 1850 to develop Men of Honor. We pride ourselves in our unique membership with a diverse group of brothers involved in various i...

name: Alpha Omega Epsilon
description:
An associate chapter chartered in 2006, the Sigma chapter of Alpha Omega Epsilon was founded to give minority groups in STEM a sense of community and support within male-dominated fields. Their members work actively to empower others professionally and academically, as well as provide a sense of community where under-represented genders can grow as individuals and future leaders. 

Alpha Omega Epsilon’s colors are Royal Blue, White, and Silver; their symbol is the Dolphin; their flower is the White Rose; and their philanthropies are Dress for Success and the FIRST Robotics.

Please check out our website for more information about us!
summary: Alpha Omega Epsilon is social and professional sorority dedicated to empowering women in STEM. We provide opportunities for both the personal and professional growth of our sisters, as well as foster friendships that will last a lifetime.

name: Society of Women in Physics
description: The Georgia Institute of Technology’s Society of Women in Physics (SWiP) encourages the interest and leadership of women in physics. We spread awareness of the achievements and impact of female/minority physicists as well as career opportunities to students of all ages through our service and outreach. We also promote and support both academic and professional development of current female/minority physicists. SWiP strives to bring together physicists of all career levels to establish mentoring relationships and by supporting a safe and welcoming community for all.
summary: The Georgia Institute of Technology’s Society of Women in Physics (SWiP) encourages interest and leadership of women in physics. We spread awareness of the achievements and impact of female physicists as well as career opportunities to women of all ages.

name: Greek Programming Board
description: The Greek Programming Board is a body comprised of representatives from the four Greek councils, joined with the purpose of implementing collaborative Greek programming initiatives.
summary: The Greek Programming Board is a body comprised of representatives from the four Greek councils, joined with the purpose of implementing collaborative Greek programming initiatives.

name: Medical Robotics Club
description:
Do you love robotics, medicine, programming, design, or all of the above? Come join us at the Medical Robotics Club to help design robots that save lives! 

You'll get a chance to participate in one of our project-based teams and get hands-on experience with programming, design, machining, prototyping, and more! Some of our current projects include a prosthetic arm with haptic feedback to restore the sense of touch, working with labs or industry, and building a low-cost, 3D-printed prosthetic for below-knee amputees!

No experience? No problem! Robotics 101 will get you up to speed on using microcontrollers, CAD, 3D printing, machining, and circuitry as you work in teams.

Join our Discord Server https://discord.gg/RAQ2CZpFvC to learn more!
summary: We are a club for students interested and passionate about robotic applications in healthcare and medical endeavors​. We work to explore, learn, and familiarize ourselves with design and programming to get hands-on experience solving real-life problems!

name: Psychology Association
description: Psychology Association at Georgia Tech (ΨA at GT) is an organization open to all majors who are interested in psychology. It's an excellent opportunity to meet with the head advisor of the psychology department and connect with other students in your major or minor. Whether it's movie nights, DIY crafting, finals-week destress events, or grad student panels, ΨA helps students build strong community, form meaningful friendships, and gain new insights while exploring and promoting psychology as GT.
summary: Psychology Association at Georgia Tech (ΨA at GT) is an organization open to all majors who are interested in psychology! The Psych Association brings students together through social activities and learning opportunities.

name: Scheller Marketing Association
description:
A marketing association composed of technically and creatively minded individuals at the Georgia Institute of Technology. A community of closely knit students pursuing their passion and interests in marketing, while blending business analytics with innovative business tactics. A unit of an extensive professional network connecting Georgia Tech with the Atlanta and national marketing industries. 

SPRING 2026 APPLICATION HERE!
summary: As a marketing association composed of technically and creatively minded individuals, we strive to provide students opportunities to develop their skills in marketing, build their professional network, and achieve success in their careers.

name: Hong Kong Student Association
description: We, the Hong Kong Student Association, aim to unite the Hong Kong population at Georgia Tech, the Cantonese diaspora, and anyone interested in Cantonese culture and issues. We hold a variety of social and cultural events for you to find your community, be informed, and have fun!
summary: We aim to unite the Hong Kong population at Georgia Tech, the Cantonese diaspora, and anyone interested in Cantonese culture, language, and issues.

name: Electro-Chemical Society
description: We are the Electro-Chemical Society. Our aim is to build a community for people seeking to understand and work in the field of batteries, fuel cells & solar cells, or in general enjoy the fascinating world of electro-chemistry.
summary: We are the Electro-Chemical Society. Our aim is to build a community for people seeking to understand and work in the field of batteries, fuel cells & solar cells, or in general enjoy the fascinating world of electro-chemistry.

name: Caribbean Students Association
description:
The Caribbean Students Association of Georgia Tech (GT CaribSA) is a center for the facilitation and promotion of Caribbean culture on campus.

 

We seek to achieve the following objectives:

 

1. Provide a welcoming environment for free cultural expression by Caribbean students.

2. Help students of Caribbean descent to connect with their roots.

3. Promote awareness of Caribbean culture on the Georgia Tech campus and help non-Caribbean natives to learn more about our culture.
summary: The Caribbean Students Association of Georgia Tech (GT CaribSA) is a center for the facilitation and promotion of Caribbean culture on campus. We seek to achieve the following objectives:1. Provide a welcoming environment for free cultural expression by.

name: International Ambassadors at Georgia Tech
description:
International Ambassadors at Georgia Tech (aka GTIA) is an organization founded by the Office of Undergraduate Admissions in December 2012 as a student recruitment team, and currently is a chartered student organization with advisors in Undergraduate Admissions and the Office of International Education. We support prospective, admitted and enrolled international students throughout the year to help them transition smoothly to Georgia Tech. In addition, we host events on campus to celebrate diversity and champion inclusion and benefit international students (such as our Night Market, Language Cafes, Culture Fest and more). Through these initiatives, We bring Georgia Tech to the World, and the World to Georgia Tech.

Our mission for the organization:

1. To promote an accurate and holistic view of the Georgia Institute of Technology globally, in support of efforts by the Office of Undergraduate Admission. We help to increase the number of talented and qualified international students applying to the Georgia Institute of Technology and, subsequently, encourage admitted international students to enroll at the Georgia Institute of Technology.

2. To aid incoming international students in engaging with the Georgia Institute of Technology community, to push for their early personal growth, and to preserve and increase the Institute’s diversity.

3. To engage the general student body at the Georgia Institute of Technology in events and activities that promote international culture and diversity, in support of efforts by the Office of International Education.
summary: GTIA champions diversity & inclusion on campus through providing support to the current and incoming international student community as well as through vibrant cultural events such as Night Market and Culture Fest that celebrate diversity on campus.

name: Alpha Pi Mu (Industrial Engineering Honor Society)
shortName: Alpha Pi Mu (Industrial Engineering Honor Society)
description: Our Mission is to prepare our members for professional success, encourage collaboration between students and members of the faculty, and serve the needs of the Industrial Engineering community, while recognizing outstanding achievement in the academic field of Industrial Engineering.
summary: Our Mission is to prepare our members for professional success, encourage collaboration between students and members of the faculty, and serve the needs of the Industrial Engineering community, while recognizing outstanding academic achievement.

name: Solar Racing
description:
GT Solar Racing is a nonprofit, student-run organization that designs and builds solar-powered race cars for track and cross-country competitions worldwide. Our mission is to push the cutting edge of hyper-efficient vehicle technology while offering real opportunities for our members to improve their design, manufacturing, and leadership skills.

 

We are a group of 150+ dedicated students from all fields of study and walks of life with a passion for innovation. Outside of the core engineering focus, our team also encompasses business, outreach, and race operations. We believe that we have a place for any team player with an excitement to learn. We are incredibly passionate about developing students who will be leaders in their respective fields, and devote considerable time to training and skills development. Students of all experience levels are welcomed and have the opportunity to design and manufacture high-level mechanical, electrical, solar, and financial systems.

 

Don't hesitate to reach out! We'd love to answer any questions you have about joining the team or even just chat about solar cars!

 

https://solarracing.gatech.edu/
summary:
GT Solar Racing is a multi-disciplinary group of motivated students who are passionate about advancing vehicle and sustainable energy technologies through building solar-powered race cars.

Check out https://solarracing.gatech.edu/ for more information!

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Inc.
description: On Friday evening, November 17, 1911, three Howard University undergraduate students, with the assistance of their faculty adviser, gave birth to the Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Inc. This event occurred in the office of biology Professor Ernest E. Just, the faculty adviser, in the Science Hall (now known as Thirkield Hall). The three liberal arts students were Edgar A. Love, Oscar J. Cooper and Frank Coleman. From the initials of the Greek phrase meaning “friendship is essential to the soul,” the name Omega Psi Phi was derived. The phrase was selected as the motto. Manhood, scholarship, perseverance and uplift were adopted as cardinal principles. A decision was made regarding the design for the pin and emblem, and thus ended the first meeting of the Omega Psi Phi Fraternity . Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc., Delta Kappa Chapter was chartered on Georgia Tech’s campus on November 20, 1976, making it the first Black Greek Organization chartered at Georgia Tech. The man who played a key role in getting Delta Kappa chartered was Brother James Mack, a graduate of Morgan State University and the Dean of Pledges for the first two lines here at Georgia Tech.
summary: On Friday evening, November 17, 1911, three Howard University undergraduate students, with the assistance of their faculty adviser, gave birth to the Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Inc. This event occurred in the office of biology Professor Ernest E. Just, t...

name: Amateur Radio Club
description:
The Georgia Tech Amateur Radio Club, W4AQL, is dedicated to providing fun for all students to learn about radio communications and allowing ECE students to make practical use of content they learn in the classroom. Whether it's your phone's wireless chips, the GPS signals tracking your location down to inches, or the radio towers on Van Leer: all of it is based in radio technology! W4AQL participates in contests about once a semester and holds Open Shack nights (with pizza provided) to talk about and show off other topics in the realm of wireless.

The club maintains an FM repeater at 145.15 MHz, various HF antennas connected to two stations in our dedicated club room, and we hold monthly test sessions (information here: https://w4aql.gtorg.gatech.edu/test.php) along with the meetings and Open Shacks above.

Our primary means of communication is through Discord: https://w4aql.gtorg.gatech.edu/discord
summary: One of the oldest clubs at Tech, the Amateur Radio Club (known by its callsign W4AQL) hosts activities for students interested in radio or looking to make use of what they learn! We host meetings and Open Shacks for anyone to visit in Van Leer E496-A.

name: National Society of Black Engineers at Georgia Tech
description:
The mission of the National Society of Black Engineers is to increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community. Founded in 1976, the Georgia Tech Society of Black Engineers, in line with the society's mission, was established increase academic excellence and promote professional development among Black students at Georgia Tech. Over the past years, the chapter has received much recognition throughout the society for it's academic achievement and contributions to the Black community. Some of these include being named National Distinguished Chapter of the Year in 2011, National Large Chapter of the Year in 2005, 2007 and 2019, Regional Large Chapter of the Year 2018, 2019, and 2023 and the Georgia Tech Organization of the Year 2008 and 2016, and many more.

To join, please visit our website, gtsbe.org, and read more on the membership tab.
summary: The mission of the National Society of Black Engineers is to increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community. Founded in 1976, the Georgia Tech Society of...

name: Alpha Xi Delta
description:
"First at Tech, First in Sisterhood!" Welcome to the Gamma Eta Chapter of Alpha Xi Delta! Here at Georgia Tech, Alpha Xi Delta is a devoted sisterhood filled with enthusiasm, diversity, and a proud legacy. We are a group of fun and passionate women, and during our years as sisters, we have made wonderful memories and lifelong friendships. Our vision at Alpha Xi Delta is "inspiring women to realize their potential,” and we have been doing that at Georgia Tech since 1954, as the first sorority on Tech’s campus. If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me!

 

Go Jackets!

Ayala Miller, President
summary: "First at Tech, First in Sisterhood!"

name: GEMS
description: GEMS is the Georgia Tech Evening MBA Students organization. Our primary goal is to bring the Evening MBA students together to grow, improve, and leave a lasting, positive impression on the institute. We have fantastic groups including social, philanthropy, athletic, consulting, women in business, international and many more.
summary: GEMS is the Georgia Tech Evening MBA Students organization. Our primary goal is to bring the Evening MBA students together to grow, improve, and leave lasting positive impression on the Institute.

name: Tau Beta Pi
description: The Tau Beta Pi Association (commonly Tau Beta Pi, ΤΒΠ, or TBP) is the oldest engineering honor society in the United States and the second oldest collegiate honor society in America. It honors engineering students who have shown a history of academic achievement as well as a commitment to personal and professional integrity. Specifically, the association was founded "to mark in a fitting manner those who have conferred honor upon their Alma Mater by distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as students in engineering, or by their attainments as alumni in the field of engineering, and to foster a spirit of liberal culture in engineering colleges."
summary: The Tau Beta Pi Association (commonly Tau Beta Pi, ΤΒΠ, or TBP) is the oldest engineering honor society in the United States and the second oldest collegiate honor society in America.

name: ATL Tanishq
description: ATL Tanishq was founded in the spring of 2013 by a group of students from Georgia Tech. Tanishq is an all-female Bollywood fusion dance team that strives to integrate Eastern and Western dance styles. We advocate for women empowerment and the importance of embracing your true self together through performance. As a team, we seek challenge through dance and aspire to spread our passion for the arts. To view our most recent performances, you can search "ATL Tanishq 2024" in YouTube.
summary: Tanishq is an all-female Bollywood fusion dance team from Atlanta, GA that strives to integrate diverse dance styles. They advocate for women empowerment and the importance of embracing your true self together through performance.

name: Miracle
description: Dance Marathon is a national movement encouraging this generation of college students to fight for the next. Since 1991, this movement has raised over $350 million for Children’s Miracle Network Hospitals, and Miracle at Georgia Tech proves to be a unique link in the network of 300 Dance Marathon programs across the country. Miracle is the largest student-led philanthropic organization on Georgia Tech’s campus, and our Dance Marathon event serves not only as the culmination of a yearlong effort to support Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta’s Heart Center, but also to showcase why we dance for our Miracle Families!
summary: Miracle is the largest student-led philanthropic organization on Tech’s campus supporting Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta's Sibley Heart Center.  We are a part of Dance Marathon, a national movement, encouraging this generation to fight for the next

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association at Geogia Tech
description: The Chinese Students and Scholars Association (CSSA) is dedicated to fostering a supportive and inclusive community for students of Chinese heritage as well as those with broader Asian cultural backgrounds. Our purpose is to celebrate the richness and diversity of Chinese culture through events, traditions, and dialogues, while also providing emotional and social support for students navigating life abroad. By creating a space that values cultural identity and shared experiences, CSSA aims to build bridges across communities, enhance mutual understanding, and contribute to the cultural diversity of the campus.
summary: The Chinese Students and Scholars Association (CSSA) is dedicated to fostering a supportive and inclusive community for students of Chinese heritage as well as those with broader Asian cultural backgrounds.

name: Japan Student Association
description:
Welcome to Georgia Tech Japan Student Association!
ジョージア工科大学日本学生会へようこそ!

 

Georgia Tech Japan Student Association aims to spread Japanese culture and awareness throughout the Georgia Tech community through various Japanese themed events! You can learn more about Japanese foods, language, people, traditions, and more!

 

You don't need to join the organization through Engage to be a member; just show up to any of our Hanashi Hours (conversation practice) or any events (note: you generally do need to pay dues to fully participate in events - see dues below)! Please join our Discord & Instagram for any announcements :)

 

Hanashi Hour (Conversation Practice): Fridays 5-7pm at Swann 320

Dues (Fall 2025 - may change): $15 / semester, $25 / year (fall + spring)

You can either show up and pay in cash or Venmo @uedaritsu534 as "JSA Dues."
summary:
Welcome to Georgia Tech Japan Student Association!
ジョージア工科大学日本学生会へようこそ!

Georgia Tech Japan Student Association aims to spread Japanese culture and awareness throughout the Georgia Tech community through various Japanese themed events!

name: Christian Campus Fellowship
description:
http://www.gtccf.org/about/

CCF is a community of Georgia Tech students living life, serving, and growing together in Christian faith. There are tons of activities and ways to get involved, so take a look and jump into one of the many opportunities! CCF is a Christian organization at Georgia Tech founded in 1987 by our campus minister, Rick Harper, and his wife Beth. Our focus is sharing the love of God with students at Georgia Tech and the wider Atlanta community.
summary: CCF is a community of Georgia Tech students living life, serving, and growing together in Christian faith.

name: Kappa Alpha Theta
description: Kappa Alpha Theta was founded in 1870 as the first Greek-letter fraternity for women. We value our history and our long tradition of leading the way for women's groups and women in higher education. The core of our organization is the spirit of sisterhood: belonging, support and encouragement, accountability, and common purpose. We seek to create spaces in which we can respect and accept individual differences and find strength in inclusion.
summary: Kappa Alpha Theta was founded in 1870 as the first Greek-letter fraternity for women. We value our history and our long tradition of leading the way for women's groups and women in higher education. The core of our organization is the spirit of sisterh...

name: Transfer Student Association
description:
Our mission is to help transfer students acclimate to the unique atmosphere of Georgia Tech and optimize their path to graduation. We offer mentorship, social opportunities and relevant resources so that our transfer community feels well-equipped to succeed at Georgia Tech. Above all, we want to help transfer students feel at home during their Georgia Tech experience. Check us out!

Get connected with other Transfer Students via GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/103216454/DVpcbfsX
summary: Our mission is to help newly acquired or already established transfer students acclimate to the unique atmosphere of Georgia Tech and optimize their path to graduation.

name: Women's Club Soccer
description: We are the Women's Club Soccer Team at Georgia Tech. Our playing level is between intramural and varsity. We are a competitive team, earning a spot at the NIRSA Regional and National Tournaments in recent seasons. We hold practice three times a week and play other college teams on most weekends, often traveling. During the fall season, we compete against teams in the Southeast region for a spot at the regional and hopefully the national tournament. In the spring, we are in a regional league with other Georgia teams.
summary: We are the Women's Club Soccer Team at Georgia Tech. Our playing level is between intramural and varsity. We hold practice 2-3 times a week and play other college teams on weekends, often traveling. Our main season is the Fall.

name: Data Science at GT
description:
DSGT is the largest data science organization on campus. Our motto is "Data science with a focus on the community."

We aim to create a community of data science students who are passionate about giving back to their own communities. Our members come from a variety of academic backgrounds, and we firmly believe anyone can be a data scientist. We do three main activities as an organization:

1) 12-week long Bootcamp, a highly specialized and rigorous data science curriculum to help new members learn data science quickly

2) Projects that are community-oriented and allow students to apply their data science and machine learning skills

3) Our flagship annual data science hackathon, Hacklytics

If you are looking for a club of like-minded individuals from diverse backgrounds that is wholly community-centric, this is the club for you!

*Our organization DOES NOT use Engage as our primary method of communication. Please head to our website datasciencegt.org for information on how to join the club. Join our general Slack for all updates and communications.

Sign up to be a member!
summary:
- "Data science with a focus on community"
- Largest data science organization on campus
- ALL MAJORS are welcome to join
- Bootcamp | Projects | Hacklytics

name: Chi Epsilon
description: Chi Epsilon is an elite Civil and Environmental Engineering Honor Society. It is an invitation-only organization and membership is extended to students who rank in the upper 30% of their respective Junior, Senior, and Graduate class of the department.
summary: Chi Epsilon is an elite Civil and Environmental Engineering Honor Society. It is an invitation-only organization and membership is extended to students who rank in the upper 30% of their respective Junior, Senior, and Graduate class of the department.

name: Hillel
description: Hillel at Georgia Tech is a pluralistic community for Jewish students on campus. We hold social, religious, Israel, and social action events throughout the Fall and Spring semesters.
summary: Hillel at Georgia Tech is a pluralistic community for Jewish students on campus. We hold social, religious, Israel, and social action events throughout the Fall and Spring semesters.

name: Sports Business Club
description:
Do you enjoy sports? Have you ever thought of all the behind-the-scenes work that goes into putting a team on the field? Are you interested in a potential career in the sports industry? Do you think partnering with the Atlanta Hawks on a case competition is exciting? If so, you’d be interested in joining the Sports Business Club at Georgia Tech! Follow us on Instagram @gt_sbc or email communications.sbcgt@gmail.com to learn more!

The Sports Business Club serves as the main platform at Georgia Tech to provide students insight into the inner-workings of businesses and organizations in the sports industry. We are a tightly-knit group of students who are passionate about the world of sports, and we work to cultivate students’ interests and ambition of one day working in a sports-related organization. By joining the Sports Business Club at Georgia Tech (SBCGT), you are joining both a professional and social platform where students can collectively learn about various areas of sports business from professionals that currently work for Atlanta’s top sports organizations. In a city that lives for its sports, there is no better place for you and your friends to take advantage of your sports fandom, and to further your understanding of the different sectors of the sports industry. 

Moreover, as a member of the Sports Business Club, you are able to participate in a wide range of thrilling activities and exclusive events such as touring state-of-the-art stadiums in Atlanta, networking with distinguished professionals in the sports world, attending sporting events as a group, partnering with local sports teams to solve business problems, and listening to guest speakers talk about their careers in sports. Just this past year, our club hosted a variety of professionals to come speak and engage with our students, including Atlanta United’s Director of Marketing, Georgia Tech’s Athletic Director, The Atlanta Braves’ Manager of Corporate Partnerships, and The Atlanta Hawks data analyst! We have had amazing speakers including, Mark Cuban (Businessman and Governor of the Dallas Mavericks) and Rich McKay (CEO of the Atlanta Falcons), and we will continue to have great speakers going forward!

Our close connections with local and national sports organizations allow our members to get their foot in the door with large sports organizations. For example, during each meeting, we send our speaker a booklet of resumes of our club members and discuss the potential job and internship opportunities they may have. In the past, we have had multiple members get hired by Atlanta’s sports organizations. The next one could be you!

If you are interested in joining SBCGT, follow us on Instagram @gt_sbc, as well as officially register to join our club on our Engage page here  

If you have any questions about our club or want to talk to one of our current students, please email communications.sbcgt@gmail.com

We can’t wait to see you!
summary: The Sports Business Club aims to stimulate interest about the sports industry by exploring the inner-workings of sports organizations and businesses, and by providing insight into sports-related careers through a network of industry leaders and alumni.

name: Xi Kappa Fraternity, Inc.
description: Xi Kappa Fraternity, Inc. is a 501(c)(7) non-profit organization that cultivates individual growth and promotes strong fellowship through various programs. Our national philanthropy is mental health awareness, emphasizing advocacy and awareness in the Asian American and Pacific Islander (AAPI) community. Founded in 1998, Xi Kappa was established as the first Asian-interest fraternity in the Southeast United States.
summary: Xi Kappa Fraternity, Inc. is a 501(c)(7) non-profit organization that cultivates individual growth and promotes strong fellowship through various programs. Our national philanthropy is mental health awareness, emphasizing advocacy and awareness in the ...

name: Honor Advisory Council
description:
We are a welcoming group of students passionate about building and maintaining Georgia Tech's commitment to academic integrity. We do this in three ways: directly advising students, holding ethics and academic integrity seminars, and hosting fun outreach events to involve the Tech community. Our group also holds bonding nights and service events so that our members can get to know each other well!

We work closely with the Office of Student Integrity by advising students found responsible of academic misconduct through a personalized action plan. Our members serve as resources for students by being knowledgeable about the honor code and professional ethics and by holding ethics seminars for anyone in the community to attend. We also promote awareness of the honor code through various events which include our annual Honor Week and AI & AI Con, which consists of a series of outdoor games, giveaways, speaker events, arts & crafts, banner signings, etc.

There are many opportunities for exposure to professional/leadership development, event planning, marketing, and more within Honor Advisory Council! There is a required interview process in order to become a member. Please reach out at our email honor@gatech.edu or fill out the application form on our LinkTree (https://linktr.ee/gthac) if you are interested!

Watch this to learn more about us!
summary: We are a student-led group open to both undergraduate and graduate students that teaches about Academic Integrity and the Honor Code through fun events including conferences, giveaways, and arts & crafts. We also mentor students through OSI sanctions.

name: Zeta Beta Tau
description: The brothers of ZBT are successful leaders and gentlemen. Established nationally in 1898 and at Georgia Tech in 1916, our brotherhood is an institution of excellence. We endeavor to be involved philanthropically, academically, and athletically.
summary: The brothers of ZBT are successful leaders and gentlemen. Established nationally in 1898 and at Georgia Tech in 1916, our brotherhood is an institution of excellence. We endeavor to be involved philanthropically, academically, and athletically.

name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
description: The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers is the world’s largest technical professional community and was led by some of the brightest minds in the industry, including Alexander Graham Bell and William R. Hewlett. The IEEE student branch at Georgia Tech is over 100 years old and is the largest IEEE student branch in the nation. The Georgia Tech IEEE has also been recognized as an Outstanding Student Branch and an Exemplary Student Branch for over 5 years running. Don’t wait. Join GT-IEEE. Become part of the professional society that is shaping the future of the engineering industry.
summary: The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers is the world’s largest technical professional community and was led by some of the brightest minds in the industry, including Alexander Graham Bell and William R. Hewlett. The IEEE student branch at..

name: Yellow Jacket Baseball Club
description: A group of people that enjoy the game of baseball and want to continue playing the sport after high school. Open to all skill levels, but mainly seeking members with prior baseball experience. We compete in the NCBA's South Atlantic West Conference.
summary: A group of people that enjoy the game of baseball and still want to play it. Open to all skill levels, but we also compete in the National Club Baseball Association.

name: FirstGen
description: The FirstGen Mission: FirstGen exists to foster community and a sense of pride among the first-generation college student population at Georgia Tech. FirstGen seeks to make an impact on our campus and community.
summary: FirstGen is focused on creating an inclusive community for First-Generation College Students at Tech. We want to foster a sense of pride among us through peer mentoring and fun social events. We also facilitate the Growing Up in Science Speaker Series!

name: Bangladesh Student Association
description: We are a non-partisan organization aimed at bringing together the Bangladeshi student community at Georgia Tech. Here we promote Bangladeshi culture, and give Bengalis of GT a place to belong, build their own community, and network with people from all around Bangladesh, America, and other countries are Asia.
summary: We are a non-partisan organization aimed at bringing together the Bangladeshi student community at Georgia Tech, promoting Bangladeshi culture, and helping those in need in Bangladesh and elsewhere. Follow us on Instagram to be kept up to date: bsa.gt

name: Samulnori Club at Georgia Tech
description:
We aim to:

A. Provide Georgia Tech students with opportunity to learn and enjoy playing Samulnori, Korean traditional percussion music.

B. Increase cultural diversity on Georgia Tech campus

C. Enhance the diversity of musical environment on Georgia Tech campus
summary: We provide Georgia Tech students with opportunity to learn and enjoy playing Samulnori, Korean traditional percussion music.

name: Association of Environmental Engineers and Scientists
description: AEES is a student-run organization within the School of Civil and Environmental Engineering (CEE). We connect undergraduate and graduate students with industry, faculty, and fellow peers through regular lunch meetings, volunteering opportunities, and social events. Meetings are held every other Thursday from 11am to 12pm in the Mason Building classroom 2117. Please join the AEES GroupMe at the following link: https://groupme.com/join_group/109372319/Y6ExE2KO. Please register to be an AEES member with the following Google form: https://forms.gle/SRdRG6LqXDVWbN618
summary: AEES is a student-run organization for environmentally-minded engineers and scientists. We connect undergraduate and graduate students with industry, faculty, and fellow peers through regular lunch meetings, volunteering opportunities, and social events.

name: TEDxGeorgiaTech
description: TEDxGeorgiaTech is an independently organized TEDx event in Atlanta, Georgia. We are proud to be entirely student-run. Our goal is to showcase ideas from our own community that inspire change on Georgia Tech's campus and beyond through Open Mic Nights, Student Speaker Salons, and Conferences. The TEDxGeorgiaTech organizing sub-teams include Speakers, Community Engagement, Production, Programming, and Marketing. To find out more about our team, please go to our Instagram @tedxgeorgiatech for the most relevant information, visit our website tedxgeorgiatech.com and Linktree, and subscribe to our newsletter.
summary: TEDxGeorgiaTech is an independently organized TEDx event in Atlanta, Georgia. We are proud to be entirely student-run. Our goal is to showcase ideas from our own community that inspire change on Georgia Tech campus and beyond.

name: HexLabs
description:
We do not do recruitment through Engage - applications to join the organizing team usually open in late October and will be announced on our social media.


HexLabs is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit and student organization consisting of Georgia Tech undergraduate students focused on being the drivers of computer science outreach in the Georgia Tech, state, and national communities. Through throwing large-scale collegiate hackathons of upwards of 1000 people to small-scale hackathons and workshops, our organization aims to spread the spirit of application, innovation, and creativity in computer science education.

By partnering with companies and organizations large and small, we are able to cultivate environments that bring value to the lives of the communities we serve all at no cost to the participants. The motivation behind throwing free events is to allow for any person of any background to have the capacity to take full advantage of a positive experience. Our organization strives to provide a resource-rich environment that ultimately ends up in allowing a participant to learn and apply new skills, connect with other like-minded innovate individuals, and bring any of their ideas to life.

Visit hexlabs.org for more information about our newsletter, community Discord, upcoming events and how to join the team. To keep up with all things HexLabs, join our newsletter at hexlabs.org and follow us @thehexlabs on Facebook, Instagram and Twitter.
summary: Through throwing large-scale collegiate hackathons of upwards of 1000 people to small-scale hackathons and workshops, our organization aims to spread the spirit of application, innovation, and creativity in computer science education. (previously HackGT)

name: Suit Up Professional Preparation
description:
The purpose of S.U.P.P. is to provide opportunities for the first-year class in career and professional preparation while creating a comfortable space for student leaders to grow and connect with others. We provide around 20 selected first-year students the opportunity develop their leadership skills and prepare them for their future careers. These students will receive career advice through planned meeting activities and participate in organizing events and projects that provide career preparation aids targeted at the first-year class.

We are organized into 3 committees: Marketing - in charge of social media and event promotions, Communications - in charge of event invitations and spread of information - and Logistics - in charge of coordinating resources for events and projects. 

Some key events and programs for SUPP include: resume writing workshop, mock career fair, and LinkedIn workshop. SUPP partners frequently with the Career Center.
summary: Suit Up Professional Preparation (S.U.P.P.) is a first-year leadership organization that aims to provide leadership and professional development opportunities for the first-year class.

name: Arab Student Organization
description: The Arab Student Organization (ASO) is a cultural non-profit organization focused on uniting the greater Arab community and those interested in Arab culture, tradition, and language. The ASO is a non-political and non-religious group with a sole focus on expanding and promoting Arab heritage. Moreover, it strives to foster cultural understanding within and propagate a positive image of Arabs to the Georgia Tech community through activities, events, and philanthropic projects. The ASO is open to any and all Georgia Tech students.
summary: The Arab Student Organization (ASO) is a cultural non-profit organization focused on uniting the Arab community at Georgia Tech and those interested in Arab culture, tradition, and language.

name: God's Influence Flowing Through Every Disciple
description: GIFTED is a thriving Christian organization at Georgia Tech, dedicated to fostering faith and community. We host weekly Bible studies that dive into Scripture through teaching, discussion, and reflection. Beyond that, our events calendar is packed with opportunities for fellowship and connection. GIFTED exists to be a refuge for encouragement, a source of spiritual guidance, and a space to celebrate God’s work in our lives. We also actively serve Georgia Tech and the wider Atlanta community through impactful ministries while building meaningful relationships on campus and beyond. Join us and grow in faith, purpose, and fellowship!
summary: GIFTED is a growing Christian Organization at Georgia Tech. We have many services such as a Gospel Choir, weekly Bible Studies, and Community Service. On top of that, we have church visits and various events for fellowship.

name: Treble Choir
description: Treble Choir is an all treble vocal ensemble. We sing a wide range of music and accept treble singers at all levels of singing experience. No auditions required! Register for the class on Buzzport.
summary: Treble Choir is an all treble vocal ensemble. We sing a wide range of music and accept treble singers at all levels of singing experience. No auditions required! Register for the class on Buzzport.

name: Glee Club
description:
The Georgia Tech Glee Club formed as an all-male group in 1906, making it the oldest student organization on campus. Composed of graduate and undergraduate students, the members of the group represent all of the Institute's colleges. In recent years, the Glee Club has performed at prestigious events both at Georgia Tech and throughout the Atlanta community and has traveled to locations such as New York City, Hawaii, and the UK, spreading what it means to be a Ramblin' Wreck and a helluva engineer!

 

Join us at the beginning of the semester on Monday nights from 6:30-7:45pm in West Village room 175 and come see for yourself what we're all about!
summary: The Georgia Tech Glee Club is a male vocal fach ensemble. It is the oldest student organization on campus, having completed World Tours and several studio albums.

name: Competitive Sports Office
description: This site will be primarily used by sport club officers for the purpose of communication between the Competitive Sports Office and officers of sport clubs.  Specifically, all required volunteer forms (safety officer, coach, CRC driver) are now to be completed online through this page.
summary: Competitive Sports provide students, faculty, and staff the opportunity for competition and fun in a variety of team and individual sport activities at various levels.

name: Black Graduate Students Association
description: Founded in 1988 and officially chartered in 1990, the Black Graduate Student Association at the Georgia Institute of Technology (GT BGSA) exists to provide an infrastructure of unity, support and academic guidance for the betterment of the Black graduate student body and community. GT BGSA accomplishes this mission through a determined executive board, standing committees, a talented membership, and partnerships with organizations like yours. Today, Georgia Tech is annually a top producer of black advanced degree graduates. Many of the over 300 black graduate students on campus are BGSA members, representing most of GT’s academic disciplines in doctoral and masters programs. Before these individuals move on to make contributions to organizations like yours, many are involved in the personal and professional development forums, community outreach, and cultural activities offered by GT BGSA. We invite you to learn more about GT BGSA and the benefits of collaboration. Your involvement is critical to securing the financial foundation that allows us to achieve our goals. We look forward to discussing creative ways for your organization to get involved with GT BGSA and make a difference.
summary: The Black Graduate Student Association at the Georgia Institute of Technology (GT BGSA) exists to provide an infrastructure of unity, support and academic guidance for the betterment of the Black graduate body and community

name: Turkish Student Organization
description:
TSO represents Turkish students and Turkish culture at Georgia Tech. We help new students adapt to life in USA and foster intercultural enrichment by offering a glimpse of our culture in various events and activities.

If you are an incoming student, you can fill out this form so we can get to know you: GT-TSO’ya Hoş Geldiniz! / Welcome to GT-TSO! – Fill out form
summary: TSO represents Turkish students and Turkish culture at Georgia Tech. We help new students adapt to life in USA and foster intercultural enrichment by offering a glimpse of our culture in various events and activities.

name: Infinite Harmony
description:
Infinite Harmony is a co-ed a cappella group here at Georgia Tech. We compete in the ICCAs, perform at gigs both on and off campus, and also have a retreat each semester.  We enjoy coming together to make great music and step away from the day-to-day stresses of class!

Learn more about us through our website, Facebook, YouTube, or just email us!
summary: Love to sing?  We're Infinite Harmony, Georgia Tech's premier co-ed a cappella group!  WE WILL BE HOLDING AUDITIONS FOR FALL 2025!  If you would like to audition with us, you can fill out our interest form on our website!

name: Nu Chapter of Pi Tau Sigma
shortName: Nu Chapter of Pi Tau Sigma
description: PI TAU SIGMA is the International Honor Society for Mechanical Engineers. Pi Tau Sigma members are chosen on a basis of sound engineering ability, scholarship service, leadership, and integrity. The Pi Tau Sigma mission is to strive to create better engineers through commitment to academic excellence and dedication to service. Pi Tau Sigma prides itself in the core values: Integrity (soundness of character and moral conduct), Service (serving the mechanical engineering profession and the community), and Leadership (fostering initiative through example in a professional manner). Pi Tau Sigma is a member of the Association of College Honor Societies. There are presently 182 collegiate chapters at universities in the United States, Puerto Rico, Mexico, Qatar, and United Arab Emirates.
summary: PI TAU SIGMA  is the International Honor Society for Mechanical Engineers. Pi Tau Sigma members are chosen on a basis of sound engineering ability, scholarship service, leadership, and integrity, and represent the top 25% of undergrads and 35% of grads.

name: VGDev
description:
For over 15 years, Georgia Tech’s award-winning video game development club has welcomed both beginners and seasoned developers. Whether you're new to game development or an experienced creator, you’ll find a fun and supportive environment here. Every semester, members of our club lead and support dev teams to make games which we will then showcase, from the school-at-large to major conventions.

We develop 6 games each semester with no joining restrictions, so you’ll have many options in terms of both complexity and genre. No worries if you miss earlier meetings, feel free to jump in any time, whether you’re into programming, art, music, or any other specialty.

Want to get involved? Join our Discord: VGDev's Discord
summary: No experience? No problem! We're an organization that makes video games; from the engine design all the way to logo graphics! All made by Tech students!

name: Pre-Student Osteopathic Medical Association
description: Pre-SOMA at Georgia Tech is a chapter of the undergraduate division of the Student Osteopathic Medical Association (SOMA). Our purpose is to inform prospective medical school applicants about the osteopathic medical profession, prepare them for entrance to osteopathic medical school, and to provide a support network for pre-health students at GT. By hosting a number of guest speakers, providing volunteering opportunities, and attending conferences, we strive to create a community in which access to health professionals and hands-on experience will help to shape the future of osteopathic medicine. Some of our favorite events include admission committee visits, an annual PCOM student panel, and more!
summary: Pre-SOMA at Georgia Tech is determined to inform prospective medical school applicants about the osteopathic medical profession, to prepare them for entrance into medical school, and to provide a support network for pre-health students on campus.

name: Wreck Racing
description:
Our Purpose:

Wreck Racing is an all-student racing team that builds cars for the Grassroots Motorsports $2000 Challenge (see www.2000challenge.com). With a tight budget, the $2000 Challenge favors those with more ingenuity and skill than money. As many as 100 different teams return each year to battle it out and see who can squeeze the most performance from a penny. Our current Challenge build features a 2005 Audi S4 engine and driveline under a 1986 Dodge Conquest body.

Our Members:

There are typically three types of people who will join Wreck Racing:

I. These are people who know very little about cars, but would like to know more. Whether it be driving, fixing, breaking, tuning, or even general maintenance these people are in the club to learn as much as they can about cars and engineering. Nowhere else at Georgia Tech can you find a club that will provide you with more hands-on experience with real cars. This allows students to finally put their classroom theory into real-life situations.

II. These people are generally experienced gearheads. They know a lot about cars and can tell you anything about cars ranging from normal maintenance to high-performance modifications. More than likely, these people drive a fast car (or more than one of them!) and are pretty good at it! They typically join the club because we can offer them one of the most unique and challenging automotive experiences they will ever have. Building a car for just over $2000 is no easy task. Building a car that can race hard for two days straight for $2000 takes a serious engineering effort.

III. The final type of person usually owns at least two cars (neither of which was purchased for more than $1000) and has performed at least one engine swap on them. These guys are total car freaks. When they are not trying to piece together a Wreck Racing car, you can probably find them in their own garage trying to piece together their own. Wreck Racing offers these people their car fix while stuck at school. So come on out and visit the Wreck Racing shop. No matter previous experience, members are able to work on a car that will provide them with welding, CAD design, no end of tuning, tweaking, cutting, painting, wiring, soldering, taping … well, you get the point. If you love cars and have not found your way to our garage, you don’t know what you are missing! New members are always accepted no matter their current skill level or major!
summary: Wreck Racing is an all-student racing team that builds cars for the Grassroots Motorsports $2000 Challenge (see www.2000challenge.com).

name: Dungeons & Dragons Club
description:
Hello! Welcome to Dungeons & Dragons Club! We help students get acquainted with the often-intimidating game of D&D and many other tabletop game systems! Whether you've only heard about the game or have played hundreds of times, this club will be fun, informative, and entertaining.

If you need to learn how to play, if you need dice, if you need books, if you need a dungeon master, if you want to learn to be a dungeon master, if you're a dungeon master looking for players, or if you're just looking to meet others passionate about the hobby, we invite you to come by our regular meetings, socials, or game nights or jump in our Discord Server where we offer a game listings channel and coordinate a club-wide public campaign. Look forward to seeing you there! For more information, please visit our Discord Server (https://discord.gg/YJzVJvH).
summary: Welcome to Dungeons & Dragons Club! We help students get acquainted with the often-intimidating game of D&D. No matter the skill level or amount of experience points, we have something for you! If interested, click here to delve into your first dungeon.

name: Engineers in Action
description:
Engineers in Action works to provide a sustainable solution to poverty in underserved communities located in South America and Africa by annually funding, designing, and implementing a suspended footbridge project in previously isolated populations. These pedestrian bridges provide year-round access to schools, medical facilities, freshwater access points, markets, job opportunities, and more. All skills and majors are welcome, as there are several tasks requiring broad skill sets and interests.

Our team of dedicated students has previously traveled to Bolivia in the summer of 2019 and was supposed to travel to Eswatini (located in southern Africa) in the summer of 2020, but those plans were canceled due to the Covid pandemic. EIA (on a National level) has constructed a total of 85 footbridges in 11 different countries, serving over 150,000 previously isolated individuals worldwide. These projects engage the local community in the construction, create a sustainable solution to poverty, and give students hands-on experience through extensive responsibilities in the design and construction process. Past EIA bridge builds have brought about a 12% increase in children enrolled in school, an 18% increase in healthcare treatment, a 59% increase in women in the labor market, and a 75% increase in farm profits.

In Summer 2021, 5 students from the organization traveled to Clendenin, West Virginia as part of the 2021 EIA In-Country program that was created because of COVID-19 restrictions. We spent 2 weeks in rural West Virginia constructing a 25-ft long vehicular bridge alongside professional mentors and community members.

Join the EIA family as we get hands on experience designing and constructing structures, build relationships with corporate sponsors who are always on the lookout for new talent, and make a huge impact on our local and international communities!

Check out our recent 2024 Mishka Mayu, Bolivia Pedestrian Bridge Project Highlights: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gFzw7vzf48w

Please reach out to georgiatecheia@gmail.com for more information and to be added to our Email list.
summary: We design and construct suspended footbridges in underserved isolated communities across the globe, allowing students to do all design and construction while also working closely with corporate sponsors who build relationships with the student members.

name: Industrial Designers Society of America
description: Georgia Tech's Industrial Designers Society of America (IDSA) student chapter represents the international professional organization at the educational-level. We strive to enhance the experience of industrial design students and people interested in industrial design alike though career-forward events. Building a strong design community is at our top priority. Come get excited about design!
summary: Georgia Tech's chapter of the international professional organization for Industrial Designers, IDSA. We strive to enhance the experience of industrial design students and people interested in industrial design alike by hosting career-focused events.

name: Student Competition Center Governing Board
description: The Student Competition Center (SCC) Governing Board is a collection of representatives from each student competition team within the Student Competition Center. The Board enables all of its constituent teams to address shared concerns in the areas of safety, operations, and finance with a unified voice. The board oversees finance, facilities, training, safety, outreach, and social events for the SCC.
summary: The Student Competition Center Governing Board is a collection of representatives from each student competition team within the Student Competition Center. The Board enables all of its constituent teams to address shared concerns and facility management.

name: Zeta Tau Alpha

name: Bhangra
description: We are GT's only premier competitive Bhangra team. We compete across the country, spreading our love and passion for this folk dance, while also graciously representing GT. As much as we love to win, we love getting to know our members even more. Every dancer on the team brings something unique to the group: both on the stage and off. This diversity not only allows us to dominate whatever dance floor we step onto, but also to come together and bond as a group. We all joined the team because we love to dance; but we stayed because we love the family. #bruahhhhh
summary: We are GT's only premier competitive Bhangra team. We compete across the country, spreading our love and passion for this folk dance, while also graciously representing GT. As much as we love to win, we love getting to know our members even more. Every..

name: College Democrats
description: College Democrats at Georgia Tech (CDGT) is dedicated to promoting a progressive message on Georgia Tech's campus. We have general meetings where we have discussions about current events, engage in activities, and hear from speakers. Our discussions with special guests allow CDGT members to get to know state and local officials and hear about their experience firsthand. In the past, we have hosted former gubernatorial candidate Jason Carter, Atlanta Mayor Kasim Reed, Representative and former GT quarterback Taylor Bennett, and many other officials. Other events have included but are not limited to: a rally against campus carry at the State Capitol, a debate with College Republicans, and a Planned Parenthood volunteer day.
summary: College Democrats of Georgia Tech (CDGT) is dedicated to promoting a progressive message on Georgia Tech's campus.

name: Men's Club Basketball
description: Men's Club basketball at Georgia Tech provides an exciting opportunity for players to foster their love of basketball through competitive contests against other universities, compete for regional and national championships, travel to new places, and make friends!
summary: Men's Club basketball at Georgia Tech provides an exciting opportunity for players to foster their love of the game, compete, travel across the country, and make new friends!

name: Student Alumni Association
description:
Our mission is to enhance the experience of each and every Tech student by providing opportunities that broaden the college experience, strengthen traditions, build lifelong loyalty to Georgia Tech, and prepare every student for success after college. We achieve this by leveraging our greatest asset, Tech’s alumni, and facilitating continuous interaction and sharing between alumni and students. Underlying all that we do is a belief in the importance of fostering lifelong participation and philanthropic support while maintaining Georgia Tech spirit and traditions.

 

To join SAA visit www.gtsaa.com/join
summary: Our mission is to enhance the experience of each and every Tech student by providing opportunities that broaden the college experience, strengthen traditions, build lifelong loyalty to Georgia Tech, and prepare every student for success after college.

name: Digital Equity and Empowerment Project at Georgia Tech
description: Project DEEP is a student-led organization at Georgia Tech that partners with the International Rescue Committee and other humanitarian initiatives to deliver tutoring, mentorship, and hands-on workshops in digital & AI literacy, STEM, and ethical technology. We work to bridge the digital divide and bring marginalized populations to the forefront of innovation, ethics, and advocacy—ensuring they have a voice in shaping inclusive, community-driven futures.
summary: Project DEEP is a student-led organization at Georgia Tech that partners with the International Rescue Committee to deliver tutoring, mentorship, and hands-on STEM workshops.

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America
description:
YDSA at Georgia Tech is the Tech branch of Young Democratic Socialists of America (YDSA), which is in turn the student and young adult arm of the Democratic Socialists of America (DSA), the largest socialist organization in the United States. YDSA GT is a leftist student organization which promotes social and progressive activism, socialist and leftist theory, and direct political action with a strong sense of community, diversity, and solidarity.

 

Our main goals include fighting for the immediate needs of those in the Georgia Tech and Atlanta community, while in the long-term focusing on educating and building our capacity to fight for more radical and structural changes.

 

We do not use our Engage (this page) to track membership, so if you would like to get involved, visit and fill out the form on our Linktree: linktr.ee/ydsagt
summary: YDSA at Georgia Tech is the Tech branch of Young Democratic Socialists of America, which is the student arm of the DSA, the largest socialist organization in the USA. We are a leftist student organization which promotes progressive activism and theory.

name: Influence29
description: Our mission is to impact and influence collegiate students spiritually by fostering an authentic (loving, accepting, and nonjudgmental) Christian community aimed at producing creative, uplifting, and real content and life-changing experiences.
summary: Our mission is to impact and influence collegiate students spiritually by fostering an authentic (loving, accepting, and nonjudgmental) Christian community aimed at producing creative, uplifting, and real content and life-changing experiences.

name: Office of Disability Services
description: The Office of Disability Services aims to improve the educational experience of students with disabilities through equitable access, accommodations, and the provision of programs and services.
summary: Office of Disability Services

name: Swing Dance Social Community
description:
Swing Dance Social Community (SDSC) was originally formed in 1999 to give students at Georgia Tech and people in the Atlanta Community a place to do what they love most – dance!

 

We offer a fun and inclusive environment for everyone of any background and ability level. With FREE weekly lessons and socials as well as monthly dances(sometimes with live bands!) we are proud to bring Social Dancing to Georgia Tech!

 

Looking to resume-build? Employers love to see social dancing on your resume too, because it shows you know how to talk to people. Leadership opportunities are available to students of all years. 

 

Become a member to get access to our member-only events and free entry to the monthly dances. To learn more about how to dance with us or get involved, visit our website https://sdsc.gtorg.gatech.edu/ or message us on Instagram @sdsc_at_gt!

Join the most efficient extracurricular activity, where you can Socialize, Exercise, and De-stress in one!
summary: Join us to learn styles of Social Dancing! We offer free weekly lessons, with exclusive one-on-one access to instructors as well as monthly dances. Join the most efficient extracurricular activity, where you combine Social, Exercise, and Destress in one!

name: IEEE PES
description: Founded in 1970, The IEEE Power and Energy Society is the oldest society of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), the world’s largest technical professional organization. IEEE PES at Georgia Tech is a student branch of the National IEEE PES. Join us to learn more about the power and energy area through technical talks, tours, and networking opportunities with other students, local PES chapter members and industry leaders.
summary: IEEE PES at Georgia Tech is a student branch of the National IEEE PES, where we host technical and social events to disseminate interests and opportunities in the power and energy industry.

name: Psychology Graduate Student Council
description: Psychology Graduate Student Council (PGSC) at Georgia Tech aims to foster a sense of community, information, and achievement within the School of Psychology.
summary: Psychology Graduate Student Council (PGSC) at Georgia Tech aims to foster a sense of community, information, and achievement within the School of Psychology.

name: Boxing Club
description:
Float like a butterfly, sting like a Yellow Jacket! The Georgia Tech Boxing Club trains to compete in amateur boxing tournaments across the country. We also provide beginner programs and fitness classes so that you can get in shape and learn the art of boxing without any pressure to compete. No experience necessary—all skill levels are welcome!

Practices are on Monday-Thursday from 7:15-8:30 PM and Saturdays from 11 AM - 12:15 PM

Practices are at Buckhead Fight Club, a world class boxing gym located about 15 minutes from campus. Get directions here. We carpool to practice; carpool information can be found in our Slack below.

Join our Slack for more information: http://gtboxing.slack.com

Also, visit our website for more information and FAQs: https://www.gtboxing.com/

The Spring 2024 Info Session is January 11th at 6:30pm. Our free week is January 16th - January 20th. Join the Slack for more details, the slideshow from the info session will also be available there.
summary: Float like a butterfly, sting like a Yellow Jacket! Whether you want to represent the White & Gold in national boxing tournaments, learn how to fight, or just have a good workout, Boxing Club has something for you. All experience levels are welcome!

name: Student Foundation
description:
The mission of the Georgia Tech Student Foundation is to enhance the experience of each and every Georgia Tech student by developing future leaders and loyal alumni who contribute to the Institute not only during their time as a student, but also in years to come. The Georgia Tech Student Foundation provides funding for student-centric initiatives, offers professional skill development through the management of its endowment, educates the student body on the spirit of philanthropy through donations to GT Roll Call, and develops strong leadership among its members. The committees offered are the Allocations Committee, Developments and Outreach Committee, Investments Committee, and the First-Year Leadership Initiative.

If you are interesting in joining the Georgia Tech Student Foundation, please fill out the committee interest form (below).
summary: “Give Back for Life" - The Georgia Tech Student Foundation is a completely student-run, philanthropic organization that strives to educate students about philanthropy, manage an endowment, and allocate funding to student organizations.

name: Catholic Student Organization
description:
The Georgia Tech Catholic Center's Mission is to provide a community for Georgia Tech students, faculty, and friends to encounter Christ through liturgy, faith formation, community, and service.

 

The Catholic Student Organization at Georgia Tech is the student body which works with the Georgia Tech Catholic Center located on campus next to Peter's Parking Deck. The Catholic Center offers Sunday and Weekday masses, confession, adoration, and a host of different events to engage the spiritual, intellectual, and fraternal needs of the GT community. There is a main chapel, adoration chapel, and plenty of communal space for students to do homework or just hang out in. We are open to ALL students (undergraduate and graduate), employees, faculty members, and GT alumni.

 

Sunday Mass Times:

9:30 am (Traditional)     11:30 am (Traditional)

5:00 pm (Praise & Worship)     9:00 pm (Praise & Worship)

Daily Mass at 5:30 pm Monday through Thursday, 12:30 pm Friday, and 7:30 am on Tuesday.

Communion Service at 5:30 pm on Wednesday.

Confession starts an hour and ten minutes before each mass until ten minutes before mass.

 

To find out more go to the Catholic Center's website!

Or check out our linktree: linktr.ee/gt_catholic

 

“Our belonging to Christ coincides with our belonging to the people of Christ, to the Church of God."

- Fr. Luigi Giussani
summary: The Georgia Tech Catholic Center's Mission is to provide a community for Georgia Tech students, faculty, and friends to encounter Christ through liturgy, faith formation, community, and service.

name: FLO Advisory Board
description:
The FLO Advisory Board works together to preserve the integrity of the First-year Leadership Organization (FLO) experience amongst member organizations.

 

Find Your First-Year Leadership Organization (FLO)!

Applications July 1, 2025!

A FLO (First-Year Leadership Organization) is an organization with Georgia Tech departmental connections that seeks to enhance the GT community through events, friendships, and service. FLOs are excellent opportunities for new students to build community and become engaged in leadership opportunities in their first year!  If you choose to join a FLO, you will not only have the opportunity to impact the entire campus community but most importantly, the new freshmen class! 

 

Currently, Georgia Tech has 8 FLOs! Click on each organization to learn more: 

Connect

FreShGA

First-Year Activities Board (FAB) 

First-Year Leadership Initiative (FLI) 

First-Year Texperience (FYT)

Seek Discomfort (SEED)

Suit Up Professional Preparation (SUPP)

Reimagine, Inspire, Serve, Empower (RISE)

 

2025-2026 Recruitment Update: 
First-Year Students are invited to apply to organizations of interest via the FLO Common Application. Applications open July 1, 2025 and close Tuesday, September 2, 2025.
summary: To preserve the integrity of the First-year Leadership Organization experience amongst member organizations.

name: American Medical Student Association
description:
The American Medical Student Association (AMSA) is a student organization committed to the concerns of students who aspire to become physicians. AMSA has a national membership of over 28,000 composed of premedical and medical students, interns, and medical residents. Membership is open to any student who is preparing to attend medical school. The Georgia Tech chapter of AMSA strives to provide a supportive environment for students pursuing health professions. We offer frequent guest speakers including campus pre-medical advisors, medical school admissions representatives, MCAT preparation firm representatives, current physicians, and more. AMSA also holds medically related volunteering opportunities such as MedShare, fundraising events for organizations around Atlanta, and multiple health awareness and professional networking events throughout the year. AMSA will also hold events pertaining to other health fields in order to expose our members to various post-graduate options.

 

Every year, the Georgia Tech chapter of the American Medical Student Association hosts Georgia Tech's Annual Pre-Health Conference. This conference attracts representatives from medical schools all over the United States who are looking to network with Atlanta's thriving pre-health community. We invite students to join these representatives and our local guests for a day of workshops, networking, and panel discussions focused on healthcare! At the end of the conference, we will also host a Health Fair for students to talk to representatives from various organizations!

 

Please visit our website for the registration forms and more information: www.gtamsa.weebly.com
summary: The American Medical Student Association (AMSA) is a student organization committed to supporting Pre-Health students through mentorship, advocacy, and membership engagement as they aspire to become future healthcare professionals.

name: Sigma Gamma Tau
description: Sigma Gamma Tau is the national Aerospace Engineering Honor Society. The goal of the Georgia Tech Chapter of SGT is to further the experience of aerospace engineers at Georgia Tech. AE students at the top of their class are invited to join SGT and partake in social, educational, and service activities. Examples of our activities include planning the AE Career Fair; running the AE Undergraduate Peer Mentoring and AE Undergraduate Teaching Assistant Programs, hosting academic, technical, and professional skill-building events; and organizing company networking events.
summary: Sigma Gamma Tau is the national Aerospace Engineering Honor Society. The goal of the Georgia Tech Chapter of SGT is to further the experience of aerospace engineers at Georgia Tech. AE students at the top of their class are invited to join semesterly.

name: Alpha Tau Omega
description: Founded on September 18th, 1888, our chapter is the oldest on Georgia Tech's campus, and we pride ourselves on our longstanding tradition of excellence. Scholarship, philanthropy, campus involvement, and intramurals are just a few of the areas in which we thrive. Having brothers from across the country, we possess a little bit of everything. ATΩ develops leaders in the community and instills brotherhood that will last a lifetime.
summary: Founded on September 18th, 1888, our chapter is the oldest on Georgia Tech's campus, and we pride ourselves on our longstanding tradition of excellence. Scholarship, philanthropy, campus involvement, and intramurals are just a few of the areas in which...

name: Net Impact - MBA
description: SBIC believes the business sector is critical in driving positive social and environmental change, but also that a triple bottom line perspective is best for business. Our mission is to provide knowledge, develop skills, host events, and facilitate connections for all our members in their pursuit of this vision. Many of these programs and events are coordinated in partnership with the Ray C. Anderson Center for Sustainable Business and the Institute for Leadership and Entrepreneurship (ILE).
summary: SBIC believes the business sector is critical in driving positive social and environmental change, but also that an inclusive and environmental perspective is best for business. Our mission is to provide knowledge, develop skills, and make connections.

name: Women's Club Basketball
description: The GT Women's Club Basketball team is a highly competitive club sport team on Georgia Tech's campus. We compete in regional and national tournaments, scrimmages, occasional one-off games, and host practices 3 times a week. Although not as intense as the GT's official D-1 team, we do strive to compete at a level higher than the average intermural team.
summary: The Women's Club Basketball team offers an outlet for players to foster their love of basketball. We value teamwork, dedication, and each other--which translate both on and off the court. This camaraderie is the foundation of our continued growth.

name: African Graduate Students Connect
description: African Graduate Students Connect (AGSC) exists to provide a unique social, professional and academic network to promote sociocultural enrichment, academic success, and Africa-related volunteering opportunities for graduate students of African descent or of the African Diaspora. This organization will provide periodic events or activities for African graduate students, with the intention of: fostering increased camaraderie and "a spirit of community" away from home; providing motivational, mentoring and morale support in times of need; facilitating the dissemination or sharing of pertinent and valuable academic, institutional and career/employment information; leveraging Georgia Tech African alumni to create professional networking opportunities; and, partnering collaboratively with other campus or external organizations to engage in STEM-related volunteering initiatives in Africa. This organization considers and values other black campus organizations as indispensable partners in its overall vision and agenda for organizational success. This organization also welcomes participation from and collaborations with non-African entities or persons who consider themselves to be "Friends of Africa."
summary: African Graduate Students Connect (AGSC) exists to provide a unique social, professional and academic network to promote sociocultural enrichment, academic success, and Africa-related volunteering opportunities for graduate students of African descent...

To actively recruit Hispanic/Latino and other underrepresented students into STEM fields at the undergraduate and graduate levels. 4.
