name: Sikh Students of Atlanta
description:
Mission Statement:

Connecting Sikh students across multiple Atlanta colleges (GT+Emory+GSU) with Sewa (selfless service), Keertan, and fun events!

 

Overview:

The Sikh Students of Atlanta's purpose is to provide a community for students to connect and engage with Sikh History & Punjabi (North Indian) culture. We are looking to engage in discussion about our values, share our knowledge amongst one another, celebrate our identity, build a sense of community, and volunteer in accordance with our culture's most important value: Sewa, selfless service. Additionally, we hope to battle ignorance and educate students on our campus about our identity.
summary: Connecting Sikh students across multiple Atlanta colleges (GT+Emory+GSU) with Sewa (selfless service), Keertan, and fun events! (Join our GroupMe here: https://groupme.com/join_group/66107799/8vJaOibe)

name: Chess Club
description:
The chess club will meet in person for the Fall 2025 semester on Fridays from 6:30pm - 8pm in Klaus 2443! (Check out our calendar here for more information -> https://sites.google.com/view/gtchess/calendar).

We have members of all levels from novice to 2500+ USCF, and anyone is welcome to participate. There are no dues for registered members. We host all sorts of events consisting of but not limited to casual free plays where players of all levels can find a game against people of similar strengths and meet other members with similar interests outside of chess, competitive tournaments where players can compete for prizes for their respective levels (meaning that almost anyone can win a prize), and lectures where members committed to getting better can learn chess content from a master level player. 

We coordinate meetings, events, and tournaments in our Discord server: https://discord.gg/BkUqAR5

If you don't have a discord, please sign up for our email list using the following link as another way of keeping up with the club's ongoing activities: https://forms.gle/NBZdVnPjoonzmMJA6

Calendar Link : https://sites.google.com/view/gtchess/calendar
summary: To provide an opportunity for Georgia Tech students of all skill levels to play chess in a friendly, casual environment as well as participate in workshops, tournaments, collegiate chess leagues, and other kinds of competitions and activities.

name: Theme Park Engineering and Design Club
description:
The Theme Park Engineering and Design Club provides a community for students who share an interest in themed entertainment. It promotes the idea that a student of any major, from engineering to design, can apply their skills to the themed entertainment industry and work for companies like Walt Disney Imagineering and Universal Creative. Our members all share a passion for theme parks and use this passion to develop the skills needed for a career in themed entertainment. 

                                                                                                                                                                        

If you are interested in theme parks and attractions, whether you've been to a park once or have visited every one from coast to coast, we welcome you into this club. As a member of GT TPED you will be able to take part in in-house design competitions, skills workshops, networking events with industry professionals, tours of themed attraction facilities, and social events to help meet other theme park enthusiasts. 

 

GT TPED meets every week with meetings being hybrid for the Fall semester. If you are interested in joining our club please join our Slack - the best way to stay up to date. If you have any additional questions feel free to email us at tped.gt@gmail.com. We cant wait for you to join us!
summary: TPED aims to promote student interest in themed entertainment and theme park careers by participating in design competitions, networking with professionals, touring facilities, and developing skills relevant to themed entertainment.

name: Juggling Club
description:
Juggling Club at Georgia Tech aims to create a community of jugglers on campus.  No previous skills are required to become a member - we will teach you how to juggle!

(Spring 2026) Please join our GroupMe for juggling announcements! We typically meet on Tech Green or Skiles Walkway. Practices are held on Mondays and Wednesdays at 5 pm, and Fridays at 2:30 pm.

GroupMe: groupme.com/join_group/50035039/H9ycnUls
summary: To provide an environment for people who enjoy juggling to meet and share knowledge about the hobby

name: 3484 Magazine
description: A student news magazine with a goal to engage Georgia Tech students in social topics and current events relevant to the Atlanta metropolitan area and tell unique stories about the community around Tech.
summary: To engage Georgia Tech students in social topics and current events relevant to the Atlanta metropolitan area and tell unique stories about the community around Tech.

name: Society of American Military Engineers
description: The Society of American Military Engineers (SAME) is a student-led chapter open to all GT students. Contrary to the name, you do not have to be an American, in the military, or an engineer to join. Instead, the focus of our club is to improve the professional skills of members and connect them with industry leaders, especially in the A/E/C industry. We help members polish their profile through resume reviews, talks from industry professionals, mock interviews, panel meetings, and other events. We aim to provide events every month, such as workshops, speakers, or site visits. Because we are supported by SAME Atlanta Post, we are able to host these events free of charge for students. An additional perk as a student member is free admission to SAME Atlanta Post events. With the Atlanta Post and SAME national, we have a strong network of professionals who are eager to see our members succeed. The SAME Chapter is an inclusive organization for anyone interested in the defense, engineering, and infrastructure industries, and we hope you become a part of it!
summary: The purpose of the SAME Student Chapter is to link students with professionals in federal and contracting sectors to help build the next generation of workers to support our nations infrastructure.

name: Tikkun Olam Makers
description:
Tikkun Olam Makers (TOM) is Georgia Tech's premier assistive technology organization. TOM empowers local communities of Makers (GT students with a passion for social impact, problem-solving, and design) to apply innovation and technology to solve overlooked challenges faced by Need-Knowers (individuals with a disability and/or who have an intimate knowledge and understanding of neglected needs that have no viable market solution). TOM is creating impact locally and globally by bringing together like-minded people around a shared vision of a more inclusive world.

TOM started with a simple observation that in every society, there is abundance and scarcity living
side-by-side. In other words, in every society, you have many talented people with a range of expertise alongside many people who have basic unmet needs (or “neglected challenges”). TOM’s process of innovation brings together the expertise and the needs, allowing a user-focused solution to be created. All TOM solutions are open-source and shared with the world through our online platform.

Learn more at https://tomglobal.org
summary: Tikkun Olam Makers is an assistive technology organization dedicated to improving people’s lives by making affordable, open-source technology accessible to all. Students work on projects that make a long-lasting impact in others lives!

name: The Association for Women in Mathematics Undergraduate Chapter
description:
The purpose of the AWM is to:

- Encourage women and girls to study and have active careers in the mathematical sciences.

- Promote equal opportunity and equal treatment of women and girls in the mathematical sciences.

- Support undergraduate women in the math department at Georgia Tech academically, socially, and professionally by developing programming that encourages women to become anchored in their field and thus increasing the number of women in the mathematical sciences at the highest levels.

- Events will focus on professional development, career development and community building.
summary: AWM is an organization with the goal of supporting and encouraging women and girls interested in or actively pursuing careers in math

name: Finance Society at Georgia Tech
description:
The Finance Society at Georgia Tech, a chapter of the Financial Management Association (FMA), is dedicated to empowering students interested in finance through hands-on learning, industry exposure, and career development. Our core pillars—Networking, Financial Literacy, and Professionalism—guide our wide array of programming.

We host career workshops, technical case study and workshop sessions, and guided recruitment prep to equip students with the tools needed for success in the competitive finance industry. Our Fintalks series keeps members informed with market updates and investment discussions. In addition, we welcome guest speakers from leading firms such as Bank of America and Goldman Sachs, offering direct insight into various financial career paths.
summary: The Finance Society at Georgia Tech believes in the value of networking, financial literacy, and professional developmment. We offer members valuable insight on various topics within the finance industry from professionals across the nation.

name: QBioS Student Association
summary: The organization will serve as a platform for students in the Quantitative Biosciences graduate program to organize social events, professional development workshops, Q&A with faculty or alumni and other events.

name: Material Advantage
description:
Material Advantage is an MSE student run organization that aims to host events for students to connect with others, and to provide a variety of resources for career development. 

 

We hold social events, help students find scholarships, and send students to conferences and competitions. We also host the MSE career fair and hold several career development workshops and industry networking events with industry professionals and alumni each semester.

 

Members from all majors are welcome, you do not need to be an MSE!

If you are interested in joining Material Advantage please join us on Engage and fill out this google form: https://forms.gle/Gq1JkiBLYCukdZvJ6
summary: As a chapter of the national organization, Material Advantage exists to enhance the professional development of students with opportunities to connect with peers, industry professionals, and faculty.

name: Society For Biomaterials Student Chapter
summary: The purpose of the Society For Biomaterials (SFB) at Georgia Tech is to encourage the development, dissemination, integration and utilization of knowledge in biomaterials primarily among students, the Georgia Tech community, and other members of SFB

name: Rescue Paws USA
description: Rescue Paws's goal is to partner with humane societies in the Atlanta area and host events with them, raising awareness for rescue animals and the benefits of adoption. We also want to raise money through events, donations, and fundraisers in order to make donations to the shelters. Our members will serve local humane societies by visiting and volunteering their time and care for the pets that need attention. We also as an organization hope to advocate for animal welfare and educate the public on how to properly care for animals. Oftentimes, college students will adopt or foster pets in their apartments, and it is crucial that they understand the best ways to take care of those animals! We hope to bring attention to the many animal shelters in the Atlanta area and educate the public on how they can properly care for those animals in need.
summary: Rescue Paws is a service-based organization at Georgia Tech with the goal of helping rescue animals and advocating for animal welfare. We hope to share the benefits of adoption, donate time and money to shelters, and educate about proper animal care.

name: Singapore Society
description:
Singapore Society (SingSoc) is a student organization at Georgia Tech dedicated to sharing and celebrating Singaporean culture. From recreating beloved local food to hosting social events and exploring traditions from our multicultural island, SingSoc offers a welcoming space to connect, learn, and build community. There is no restriction on who can join, and true to our multicultural roots, we welcome members of all nationalities to learn and experience more of what our island has to offer.

Message us at our IG to join our Telegram group!
summary: Singapore Society at Georgia Tech celebrates Singaporean culture, builds community, and shares the spirit, food, and traditions of Singapore with campus.

name: ConCave: Ph.D. Student Group in Design
description: To enhance the community of the Ph.D. students in design and related fields at Georgia Tech. To foster an exchange of ideas and research among Ph.D. students in design at different stages of their course of study. To create an intellectual environment for these exchanges to take place, by providing a venue for presentation and by encouraging a spirit of community and camaraderie. To promote the research ongoing within the Ph.D. program in the College of Design nationally and internationally.
summary: ConCave is a Ph.D. student-led organization in the College of Design. The Goal of the Group is to build a community revolving around design and research.

name: Buzz Buddies
description: The Buzz Buddies program serves as a mentorship collaborative between Georgia Tech and Atlanta Public Schools. Georgia Tech students will enhance educational opportunities for APS students through STEM initiatives and mentorship. Four pillars guide the program's purpose: mentorship, scholarship, confidence, and community.
summary: The Buzz Buddies program serves as a mentorship collaborative between Georgia Tech and Atlanta Public Schools. Georgia Tech students will enhance educational opportunities for APS students through STEM initiatives and mentorship.

name: Ski and Snowboard Club
description:
The purpose of this organization is to provide an accessible outlet for Georgia Tech students to ski and snowboard. Students would be able to attend recreational events and/or compete on the Competition Team as a Club Sport. Through the organization, students would be able to receive discounted/group pricing that would make skiing and snowboarding, generally considered an expensive sport, possible for university students. We want to lower the barrier of entry for newcomers and provide opportunities for our most advanced members, in order to create and foster a lasting snowsports community on campus. 

To join our Slack Announcements Channel, please click here!

If you are considering joining our club and being a part of the community, explore our website and social medias. Registration for Fall '26-Spring '27 is open at this link!
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide an accessible outlet and community for Georgia Tech students to ski and snowboard. Students would be able to attend recreational events and/or compete on the Competitive Team.

name: Origami Club
description:
Since Summer 2019, we promote the art of origami by holding meetings to introduce different folding methods and models, sparking public interest by organizing events, and discussing design and engineering applications of paper-folding.

We generally meet every Friday from 3:30-5:30pm to fold 1-2 beginner to advanced level models each in small groups. Beginners are welcome! No Dues! Paper is provided.

Announcements of club events and discussion can be found on our Discord.
summary: We aim to investigate the art of origami by researching design and engineering applications of paper-folding, holding regular meetings to introduce different folding methods to students, and sparking public interest in origami by organizing events.

name: STAR Servant Leadership Team
description:
STAR (Students' Temporary Assistance and Resources) is a network of programs dedicated to connecting students with everything they need to thrive and flourish during their time at Georgia Tech.

 

For more info about some of the programs we currently support, check out our website: http://star.studentlife.gatech.edu

 

The three goals of the STAR Servant Leadership Team are:
1. Raise awareness of STAR and the programs that comprise the STAR Network
2. Support existing programs through volunteering and student leadership opportunities
3. Create new programs in response to the identification of unmet needs

 

Click the "JOIN" button above to become part of the STAR Servant Leadership Team!
summary: STAR (Students' Temporary Assistance and Resources) is a network of programs dedicated to connecting students with everything they need to thrive and flourish during their time at Georgia Tech.

organizationType.name: Division of Student Engagement and Well-being

name: Quantum Computing Association
description:
Joining the QCA:

  1. Log into engage.gatech.edu
  2. Click the "join" button in the upper right corner of the QCA webpage (i.e. https://gatech.campuslabs.com/engage/organization/qca-gt).
  3. Join our Discord at https://bit.ly/qcadiscord. (highly encouraged)
  4. Attend our weekly meetings at 6:00-7:00 PM on Thursdays. On most dates, we meet in Mason 1133, except for August 28th, September 11th, and October 2nd, when we meet in Bunger-Henry 380.

 

The purpose of the QCA:

  1. Create a community of students interested in quantum computing by organizing casual social events such as dinners or recreational events
  2. Connect students with resources, people, and opportunities in quantum computing, both academic and professional 
  3. Cultivate students' skill, interest and knowledge of quantum computing by organizing workshops, seminars, discussions with professors, and quantum computing projects
summary: A student-led organization dedicated to fostering a community, educating students, and offering resources for students who are interested in quantum computing and quantum information at Georgia Tech

name: Night of Worship
description: A campus-wide night of worship for students, by students, uniting all denominations, ministries, and churches to give glory to God as one body. Night of Worship at Georgia Tech (GTNOW), is a group of Christian students from various campus ministries and churches in Atlanta coming together to produce worship nights on campus. The band consists of student vocalists and musicians and a fully student led executive board and marketing team. Our purpose is to unite Christians on campus through worshipping Jesus together, invite all people to embrace God's grace and worship Jesus with us, and glorify Jesus and praise Him through worship nights.
summary: A campus-wide night of worship for students, by students, uniting all denominations, ministries, and churches to give glory to God as one body.

name: German Club
description:
The purpose of German Club is 

  1. To provide a community for German speakers at Georgia Tech to practice speaking, writing, and listening of the language. 
  2. To Promote the German culture on campus, as well as collaborate with other on-campus organizations.
  3. To hold regular conversation opportunities where students can discuss and learn about relevant, on-going events in the world, while expanding their German communication skills.
  4. To invite German speakers from both academia and industry to show the professional importance and usefulness of German.
summary: Providing a community for those with an interest in German language and culture to increase their knowledge of German culture and practice their language skills.

name: SMILE
description:
Mission: 

SMILE, also known as Spreading Messages In Love & Encouragement, is a Georgia Tech student organization dedicated to building a happier campus community. Founded in the Spring of 2020, SMILE came about in the midst of the COVID-19 pandemic at a time when campus was most in need of uplifting. SMILE’s mission is to foster a more positive, connected culture in the greater Georgia Tech community. We accomplish this by organizing a wide range of initiatives and events that are reflective, encouraging, appreciative, and empathetic.

 

Get involved:

SMILE conducts membership recruitment at the beginning of each fall semester! Be sure to check our Instagram @smile.gatech for more updates on the recruitment process and how you can join. For any recruitment questions, please email SMILE (smile.gatech@gmail.com)!
summary: SMILE, also known as Spreading Messages In Love & Encouragement, is a Georgia Tech student organization dedicated to building a happier campus community through events and projects that spread positivity and increase connectedness on campus.

name: Tae Kwon Do
description: The purpose of TKD is to promote and provide Tae Kwon Do instruction at Georgia Tech, provide a safe environment for members of the Georgia Tech community to practice World Tae Kwon Do style martial arts, inspire a sense of community through members' common interest of Tae Kwon Do, and give students the opportunity to train for and participate in tournaments both local and national while representing Georgia Tech.
summary: Tech TKD aims to provide Tae Kwon Do instruction to members of the Georgia Tech community. We welcome new and experienced individuals to learn and train with us.

name: Bits of Good
description:
Bits of Good is the Georgia Tech chapter of Hack4Impact, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Bits of Good drives social impact by developing custom, pro bono software for local nonprofits. While we empower our nonprofit partners to work more efficiently and scale their impact, we also create opportunities for Georgia Tech students, both undergraduate and graduate, to spur meaningful change in the community around them.

 

We have helped 15+ nonprofits since 2017, ranging in focus from supporting incarcerated mothers and their newborn children to combatting food insecurity in the Atlanta area. Each nonprofit is assigned a project team that owns the entire development lifecycle, starting with ideation and ending with delivery. The project team has 4-6 software developers, an engineering manager, 1-2 product designers, and a product manager who follow agile development methodologies.

 

Every semester we also host two free bootcamps to give back to the Georgia Tech community. We have our developer bootcamp, offering an introduction to web development, and we have our designer bootcamp, providing the fundamentals behind UX/UI. These bootcamps should give students the crucial skills they need for both succeeding in Bits of Good and penetrating industry. No experience is needed to apply for either program!

 

Lastly, we have other opportunities in the fields of marketing, brand design, logistics, finance, and nonprofit relationship coordination. If you are passionate about social impact and want to use your unique skills to make a difference in our community, then you should apply to join Bits of Good.

 

Note - we do not recruit through Engage. Applications cycles for some roles are in the weeks leading up to a semester (over break) through the first week on campus. Applications cycles for other roles are at the end of the previous semester, meaning early November for the coming spring or early April for the coming fall. Apply through our website, and we highly recommend you follow us on Instagram @gtbitsofgood for updates on recruitment timelines and insights into org events.
summary: The purpose of Bits of Good is to create opportunities for Georgia Tech students to drive social impact in the community around them. We partner with nonprofits to develop custom web software to help them work more efficiently.

name: Biomedical Photonics Society
description:
Please come by homepage to be a member !

We are officially sponsored by Optica (formerly Optical Society of America) for external funds and vast resources.

Homepage : https://sites.gatech.edu/bips/
President : Hansol Yoon
Email : h.yoon@gatech.edu

--------------------------------------------------

     Biomedical Photonics Society (BiPS) is a student organization at Georgia Tech dedicated to Biomedical Photonics. We share a vision of facilitating future leaders in Biomedical Photonics community in both academia and industry.

     This student organization will foster an environment where all members can find unique opportunities to develop into future leaders in academics and industry in the rapidly growing field of biomedical photonics. Prepared plans of academic visits, industrial visits, and social events will enable for members to expand their professional networks and be aware of broad options that members can pursue to launch new paths after graduation, contributing to optical society and for future generations of scientists.

     We believe passion is highly contagious and this organization will be the space to share and facilitate positive environment as a united community of biomedical photonics.
summary: BiPS (Biomedical Photonics Society) is a student organization dedicated to Biomedical Photonics through Georgia Tech and share a vision of facilitating future leaders in Biomedical Photonics community in both academia and industry.

name: Planned Parenthood Generation Action at Georgia Tech
description: Planned Parenthood Generation Action at Georgia Tech believes in the fundamental right of every individual to manage their sexual health and wellbeing, reproduction, and access to comprehensive and affirming healthcare in settings that preserve and protect the essential privacy and rights of each individual, and advocates public policies that guarantee these rights. PPGen strives to provide educational programs and events that highlight these values and share them with the Georgia Tech community. We strive to do this specifically by creating an environment that is safe and healthy for all of our members.
summary: Spread awareness of what Planned Parenthood Southeast is doing in and around Atlanta and attempt to get Georgia Tech students involved in volunteer and leadership opportunities with Planned Parenthood.

name: Mixed Reality at GT
description:
The purpose of GTXR is to establish a community that fosters the development of mixed reality technologies. We want to prepare the next wave of XR developers to be leaders in this rapidly expanding field.

XR technology is seeing interest from industry giants across a variety of disciplines (entertainment, tech, art, commerce, etc.), and as being familiar with XR becomes increasingly important, we want to provide the GT community a way to get involved. By participating in meaningful projects, groundbreaking research, and events with industry leaders, our members will become leaders in XR regardless of prior experience.
summary: The purpose of GTXR is to establish a community that fosters the development of mixed reality technologies. We want to prepare the next wave of XR developers to be leaders in this rapidly expanding field.

name: Tzu Ching
description: Atlanta Tzu Ching is a Student Chapter of Tzu Chi Collegiate Association (TCCA), Which is a Sub-organization of Tzu Chi Foundation. Tzu Chi Foundation Has 432 Offices in 52 Countries and Has Over 2 Million Volunteers and 10 Million (Financial) Donors. Atlanta Tzu Ching Receives Resources and Strong Support from Tzu Chi Atlanta Branch (Local) and Tzu Chi USA (National). Atlanta Tzu Ching's spirit is to love care for all. Volunteering opportunities include environmental protection activities with Trees Atlanta, Disaster Relief Assessment and Distribution, and joint tutoring for elementary students with the Emory Tzu Ching Chapter.
summary: Atlanta Tzu Ching's spirit is to love and care for all.

name: Product@GT
description:
Product@GT aims to be a club through which members get immersed in the world of all things product. Our mission is to create product-minded thinkers and leaders who are well-versed in all aspects of creating successful products, ranging from engineering, design, to marketing.

Visit our website at productgt.notion.site/hello to learn more, and join our Slack channel for more regular updates at https://productgt.slack.com/signup (Sign ups limited to gatech email domains only)
summary: Product@GT is a club where members get immersed with all things product. We aim to create the next generation of product managers and product-minded thinkers at Georgia Tech.

name: Latin American Student Organization
description: The purpose of this organization is to unite students of Latino ethnic background and those with an interest in Hispanic/Latino culture by providing a sense of community through activities that invoke social, cultural, and educational involvement.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to unite students of Hispanic ethnic background and those with an interest in Hispanic/Latino culture by providing a sense of community through activities that invoke social, cultural, and educational involvement.

name: Seek Discomfort Club
description:
Seek Discomfort Club aims to empower its members to grow as people and leaders by seeking discomfort in their lives, all while providing a positive impact on their community. The club actively motivates its members to go outside of their comfort zone through a combination of social, physical, and personal challenges!

 

Please check out our new Instagram page @gtseek for more information!

Please join our GroupMe here: Seek Discomfort @ GT

Join our other socials and/or submit any of your interesting ideas here: seekdiscomfortgt
summary: Seek Discomfort Club aims to empower its members to grow as people and leaders by seeking discomfort in their daily lives, while providing a positive impact on their community.

name: Week of Welcome
description:
Week of Welcome is an opportunity for first-year students to get to know their new home – campus-life and city-life. Students will get to move in a week earlier than other students to start making Georgia Tech their home and create connections that will last throughout their time at Tech.

  • Connect with fellow Yellow Jackets through social and educational experiences.
  • Attend workshops based on areas you are interested in – from traditions to how to do laundry to how to study for college level tests to how to have the best resume possible.
  • Learn more about opportunities to be involved across all aspects of campus life.
  • Have fun before classes start.

Go to the Week of Welcome Website to learn more.
summary: Week of Welcome is an opportunity for first-year students to get to know their new home – campus-life and city-life.

organizationType.name: Division of Student Engagement and Well-being

name: RISE
description:
Applications are OPEN! Join a FLO by September 2nd

Fill out the common application on Engage here: Georgia Institute of Technology - Form: 2025-2026 First-Year Leadership Organization (FLO) Common Application

Who We Are

Welcome to RISE (Reimagine, Inspire, Serve Empower)! We are a first-year leadership organization for all incoming undergraduate students interested in building a sense of belonging for the Georgia Tech community using inclusive leadership principles. 

What We Do

RISE members are passionate about reimagining what belonging looks like on campus. They plan events that inspire connection and service projects that empower people of all genders within the Georgia Tech community, while making friends to last a lifetime.
summary: RISE (Reimagine, Inspire, Serve, Empower) is a First-Year Leadership Organization (FLO)

name: Connect
description:
Connect is a First-Year Leadership Organization designed to support student transitions into Georgia Tech. As a part of our close-knit community, Connectors will gain valuable leadership skills through event planning and team-building activities that facilitate strong connections. Our students will be given the space to exercise their creative ability and create unique opportunities for Connect and the first-year class. As a New Student and Transition Programs organization, Connect’s vision is to ensure that new students feel confident and possess the tools to succeed at Georgia Tech.  

Visit our website and learn about our advisors here: Connect First Year Leadership Organization

Applications to join Connect or one of the other 8 FLOs on campus will be available over the summer. Visit the First-Year Leadership Organization website for more information or send us an email at connect@gatech.edu.
summary: Connect is a First-Year Leadership Organization designed to support student transitions into the Georgia Tech community.

name: Office of International Education
description: The Office of International Education (OIE) provides leadership and advocacy for pursuing and supporting Georgia Tech’s international education goals through study abroad opportunities, global research and internship programs, international student and scholar mobility, on-campus internationalization, and intercultural engagement. In collaboration with our campus and community partners, OIE expands the Institute’s global footprint and ensures that we develop good global citizens.
summary: The Office of International Education (OIE) provides leadership and advocacy for pursuing and supporting Georgia Tech’s international education goals through student and scholar mobility, on-campus internationalization, and intercultural engagement.

name: Animation Club
description:
Animation Club is a community of those interested in animation and its affiliated processes (drawing, storyboarding, rigging, modeling, rendering, etc.) at Georgia Tech. Animation Club aims to provide members with a weekly meeting place to discuss animation, learn animation processes and techniques, and hear from guest speakers in the industry. The community-based nature of Animation Club fosters collaboration, peer critique, and support in anything related to animation and its affiliated processes within Georgia Tech policies based on members' desires.

Check Discord for Location

Mondays

7-8pm
summary: Animation Club is a community of those interested in animation and its affiliated processes (drawing, storyboarding, rigging, modeling, rendering, etc.) at Georgia Tech.

name: CHAARG
description:
The purpose of CHAARG is to help students on Georgia Tech's campus learn that living a healthy + happy lifestyle is something that is attainable and fun through the resources provided at Georgia Tech and the surrounding area. We host different workouts every week led by local studios, small groups focused on your personal goals, + socials to build community! CHAARG was founded to give college students the ability to be a part of something larger than themselves that will motivate + inspire them to become the best version of themselves through helping one another reach their health + fitness goals.

Join us for weekly workouts every Tuesday at 7pm!
summary: The purpose of CHAARG is to help students on Georgia Tech's campus learn that living a healthy + happy lifestyle is something that is attainable and fun through the resources provided at Georgia Tech and the surrounding area.

name: Saudi Student Association
description:
We aim to provide a welcoming environment for Saudi Students to connect with their heritage and network at Georgia Tech, and within the nation as a whole through academic and recreational events.

We welcome all students to come learn about our culture and and have a good time at our events.

linktr.ee/saudigatech
summary: A welcoming environment for Saudis, and all others to interact, network, and celebrate our culture.

name: RotorJackets
description:
RotorJackets educates Georgia Tech students about the technology surrounding Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), specifically those equipped with First-Person View (FPV) technology. We seek to offer students a hands-on learning experience by creating a safe environment for people to fly remote controlled (RC) vehicles on campus and providing training on how to responsibly build and operate UAVs. Finally, we maintain a Georgia Tech MultiGP drone racing chapter to host local, regional, and national races that count towards our school’s national ranking.
We primarily communicate and organize events through our Discord. Join via our website, rotorjackets.tech
summary: RotorJackets aims to educate, inspire, and organize students in safely operating Unmanned Aerial Vehicles, specifically those with First-Person View (FPV) technology. We do this by offering hands-on training, recreational flying, and racing events!

name: Campus Recreation Center
summary: Our mission is to be the leader in collegiate recreation by providing comprehensive and engaging programs and services that strengthen community wellbeing and enhance student success.

name: Book Jackets
description:
Book Jackets is a club for students who enjoy reading for fun! We hope to encourage students to read more, as it can be difficult to balance reading with other academic and non-academic responsibilities. We believe that reading is a fun and stress-relieving activity that can ultimately enhance concentration, focus, and open-mindedness, making it a positive addition to any student's schedule. Together, we can create an environment where students can discover new books and genres, talk openly about the impact that books have had on their lives, and meet other students that love to read!

 

Each semester, we will pick a diverse list of books to read as a club. The books will span a variety of genres so that all members have the opportunity to read books that interest them! Although we encourage members to explore new genres, there is no pressure to read every book on the list. We will decide on a time to hold meetings as a club each semester, and use those meetings to get to know each other and discuss the books as a group!

 

Join our Discord server using this link: https://discord.gg/gSSq5cD9qf
summary: Our purpose is to connect students on campus who share an interest in reading recreationally. Together, we can discover new books and genres, talk about the impact that books have had on our lives, and meet other students that love to read!

name: Filipino Student Association
description: The purpose of the Filipino Student Association at Georgia Tech is to promote Filipino Culture and the education of Filipino traditions among students. The club intends to build relationships between students through social events, meetings, and service activities. We aspire to organize, provide, and promote active participation in civic, social, and charitable activities within the Georgia Tech, the Asian American, and the Filipino community. We also desire to create and foster a positive, friendly, healthy, and safe social atmosphere for those interested in Filipino Culture, and to bring together students of diverse backgrounds and cultures.
summary: The purpose of the Filipino Student Association at Georgia Tech is to promote Filipino Culture and the education of Filipino traditions among students. The club intends to build relationships between students through social events, meetings, and service

name: Brain Exercise Initiative
description: We are an organization that uses simple math, writing and reading aloud exercises as an intervention to improve cognitive function in those with Alzheimer's Disease. We encourage intergenerational collaboration and community engagement.
summary: We are an organization that uses simple math, writing and reading aloud exercises as an intervention to improve cognitive function in those with Alzheimer's Disease. We encourage intergenerational collaboration.

name: Serve-Learn-Sustain
description:
Serve-Learn-Sustain is a campus-wide academic initiative working with all six colleges to offer students opportunities inside and outside the classroom to collaborate with diverse partners – across the community, non-profit, government, academic, and business sectors – on key sustainability challenges.  Through SLS, students use the knowledge and skills they are acquiring at GT to help “create sustainable communities.”

SLS actively participates in two professional networks: AASHE (Association for the Advancement of Sustainability in Higher Education) and the Global RCE Network (Regional Centres of Expertise on Education for Sustainable Development, affiliated with United Nations University).

 

Our Mission 

  • SLS educates students through classroom, co-curricular, and real-world learning, on campus and off, to contribute to the task of "creating sustainable communities"

  • SLS builds the capacity of faculty to (a) teach sustainable communities-related courses, and (b) collaborate with diverse partners on their research

  • SLS facilitates GT participation in partnerships that bring together key partners - from government, community, nonprofit, academic, and business sectors -  for joint learning, research, and action

 

Our Vision

  • Georgia Tech graduates are using their disciplinary expertise to help “create sustainable communities” where humans and nature flourish, now and in the future

  • Universities around the globe are asking, “What does Georgia Tech think?” about the role that a technological institute can play in creating sustainable communities

  • Students are choosing to come to Georgia Tech partly to participate in Serve-Learn-Sustain
summary: Serve-Learn-Sustain is a campus-wide academic initiative working with all six colleges to offer students opportunities inside and outside the classroom to collaborate with diverse partners –  non-profit, government, academic, and businesses.

name: Greek Emerging Leaders
description:
This is an 8-week cohort based program facilitated by Fraternity & Sorority Life staff. The program designed to give new members the tools to amplify their impact and leadership potential within their chapter, council, and greater community.

The program will be divided up into four quadrants of Leadership:  

1.      Exploring Leadership 

2.      Embracing Diversity and Community 

3.      Managing Complexities

4.      Living Your Values through Rituals and Service
summary: This is an 8-week cohort based program facilitated by Fraternity & Sorority Life staff. The program designed to give new members the tools to amplify their impact and leadership potential within their chapter, council, and greater community.

name: Greeks United for Inclusivity Diversity & Equity
summary: Greeks United for Inclusivity, Diversity, and Equity, is an inter-council Greek committee at Georgia Tech committed to creating a culture of diversity, equity, and inclusion within the Greek community.

name: Kendo Club at Georgia Tech
description:
Our organization exists to provide a safe, supportive, and instructive place for anyone and everyone who is interested in learning Kendo, Japanese swordsmanship. We welcome all levels, and have with highly ranked instructors.

Please fill out this form to join our Discord server and get updated about our events, practices, and more!

 

Our practice schedule can be found in our Instagram page www.instagram.com/kendogt.
summary: To provide a safe, supportive, and instructive place for anyone and everyone who is interested in learning Kendo, Japanese swordsmanship.

name: Trading at Georgia Tech
description:
Mission: Trading at GT is dedicated to fostering a deep understanding of the quantitative finance industry among Georgia Tech students and alumni. Our mission is to bridge the gap between academic finance theories and real-world financial market applications, preparing members for successful careers through practical experience and industry exposure.

Core Goals:

  1. Educational Enhancement: Provide comprehensive educational resources and workshops that demystify the complexities of quantitative finance.
  2. Career Preparation: Equip members with necessary tools and knowledge for excelling in finance-related interviews and job roles through tailored preparation materials and mentorship.
  3. Networking Opportunities: Offer platforms for members to connect with industry professionals and alumni, facilitating valuable networking and potential employment opportunities.
  4. Practical Experience: Engage members in industry-quality projects that simulate real-world financial environments and challenges.

Structure: Trading at GT is structured into four distinct sectors, each catering to specific interests and skills within the realm of finance:

  • Quant: The backbone of our technical efforts, this sector is dedicated to building high-performance trading infrastructure and strategies. Members conduct quantitative research to discover and exploit tradable alphas, using rigorous data analysis and financial modeling techniques.

  • Market Insights: This sector focuses on analyzing and interpreting market data to provide members with a clear understanding of current market trends and economic indicators. It serves as the foundation for informed decision-making in other sectors.

  • Education: Aimed at both beginners and advanced members, this sector provides a structured learning pathway through workshops, seminars, and courses. Topics range from basic financial literacy to advanced quantitative methods, ensuring all members have the opportunity to grow their knowledge and skills.

  • Discretionary Trading: Unlike the quantitatively driven sectors, Discretionary Trading explores broader aspects of trading including market speculation, sports betting, and poker. This sector engages in various research projects that apply financial and probabilistic principles to a wide range of markets, offering a diverse set of experiences to its members.

Together, these sectors ensure that Trading at GT not only enhances academic and practical knowledge but also provides a holistic view of the many career paths in finance, making it an invaluable resource for any Georgia Tech student or alumnus interested in the field of finance.

LinkedIn: LinkedIn

Discord: Discord

Website: Trading Club Website (includes information on how to join)
summary: Trading at GT is an organization that provides GT students and alumni with resources about the quantitative finance industry. We provide students with industry-quality projects, interview preparation resources, and exclusive employment opportunities.

name: SEED
description:
APPLY HERE by September 2nd @ 11:59 PM.

 

We live by two cardinal truths. First, life’s greatest moments and opportunities for growth lie outside of your comfort zone. Second, developing as a leader is much easier and more fun when empowered by a supportive community. SEE(k) D(iscomfort) First-Year Leadership Organization, or SEED, was created with these beliefs in mind. Our FLO serves to develop first-years into leaders by pushing them to intentionally seek discomfort while being surrounded and supported by their fellow members. Through weekly meetings, activities, and events, we target topics that contain real opportunities for leadership development and growth in a way that encourages members to dream bigger, push past their perceived limits, and unlock their full potential.

 

Many incoming first-years describe college as a time in which they want to make an impact on campus, do things they’ve never done before, and find what they’re truly passionate about. SEED structures its learning outcomes around these aspects so that members are getting what they want out of the first-year experiences and setting themselves up to be successful throughout the rest of college, and their lives.

 

Website: https://seedgatech.wixsite.com/home
summary: SEE(k) D(iscomfort), or SEED, is a First-Year Leadership Organization dedicated to helping members grow their leadership and personal potential through introducing unique, once-in-a-lifetime experiences that challenge their perceived limitations.

name: Culture of Respect
description:
As part of Georgia Tech’s continued commitment to addressing campus sexual violence, in 2020 we joined over 100 colleges and Universities in the Culture of Respect Collective, an initiative of the National Association of Student Personnel Administrators in Higher Education Administration (NASPA). The Collective is an ambitious two-year program bringing together institutions of higher education who are dedicated to ending campus sexual violence and guides them through a rigorous process of self-assessment and targeted organizational change. 

 

The program is grounded in an expert-developed public health framework, cross campus collaboration, and peer-led learning to make meaningful programmatic and policy changes. As part of our participation, we receive strategic support and technical assistance throughout the process, as well as detailed documentation of campus-initiated changes that support survivors, prevent sexual violence, and communicate that violence is unacceptable. 

 

A key component to the program’s success is it's emphasis on cross-campus collaboration. As a result, we formed a Campus Leadership Team (CLT) composed of leaders and partners from across campus to spearhead our efforts. The CLT has representatives from across our institution allowing us to think creatively and holistically about how to use our institutional resources effectively.  

 

In Summer of 2020, the CLT engaged in a robust self-assessment process where we did a thorough review of Georgia Tech’s policies, practices, and procedures. The co-leads (VOICE Health Educator Deontez Wimbley and VOICE Advocates Jennifer Gagen and Amanda Planchard) submitted the results of the self-assessment to NASPA in Fall of 2020, and as a result we received evidence-based recommendations for Georgia Tech to implement. Based on those recommendations, the campus Leadership Team is creating an action plan for our institution and providing support for operationalizing specific initiatives of the plan. Implementing those initiatives will be an ongoing process over the course of two years. Members of the Campus Leadership Team attend monthly meetings and additional subcommittee meetings. 

 

For more information or to get involved with VOICE, email us at voice@gatech.edu
summary: This is an ambitious two-year program bringing together institutions of higher education who are dedicated to ending campus sexual violence, guiding them through a rigorous process of self-assessment and targeted organizational change.

description:
Cricket Club at Georgia Tech is dedicated to providing an organized and welcoming space for students to enjoy the sport of cricket—whether you're completely new to the game, have played casually before, or come with experience from academies and competitive teams. We are involved in both recreational cricket and competitive club cricket, regularly playing in tournaments against other schools. All updates for practices, scrimmages, and memberships are found on our WhatsApp join here: https://chat.whatsapp.com/F1FO05vUAIlEODiBd8MDBr?mode=ems_copy_h_t

We host on-campus practices, scrimmages, and tournaments using hard tennis balls, and we also have a traveling leather ball team that practices off-campus and competes at a higher level. Beyond playing, we build community by organizing watch parties for major cricket events and fostering a space for everyone to share their passion for the game.

No matter your skill level, you’ll find a place in our club—and we especially encourage those who have never played before to come give it a try!

description:

  2. Provide education and environmentally responsible alternatives to the materials used within the School of Architecture and School of Industrial Design maker spaces (studios, woodshops, workshops, Digital Fabrication Laboratory).
  3. Develop educational resources and events for students within the College of Design on green design certifications, green design tools, eco-conscious material resources, and industry leading environmental trends.
  4. Connect to the community outside of Georgia Tech to teach and share green design ideas, materials, and knowledge through interactive design activities and talks.

Foster environmental, physical, and mental wellness within the College of Design design ecosystem.
summary: ECO unites students in the College of Design to advance sustainable design. We promote eco-friendly practices, including sustainable methods, improved recycling, reduced material waste, and collaboration with experts to enhance sustainability efforts.

We are most known for our monthly Sandwich Runs, where we make, package, and deliver meals to the nearby homeless and food-insecure population. We also host Winter Clothing Drives, Spring Toiletry Drives, and various social and fundraising events. We were recognized for our service efforts at the MLK Student Celebration in 2024.

We will host events throughout the semester to facilitate conversational practice, such as on-campus events with snacks and games and off-campus trips to local Korean restaurants, karaoke, etc.
summary: Korean Conversation Club at GT is a language exchange club where GT students studying Korean and Korean international students at the Language Institute can get together at events to practice their Korean and English conversational skills.

name: ElectrifyGT
description: ElectrifyGT is a student-led sustainability consulting club and campaign focused on accelerating campus electrification at Georgia Tech. We develop economical electrification and decarbonization strategies by engaging key stakeholders, conducting research, and mobilizing student support to aid in a responsible transition. Our mission is grounded in the belief that electrification is the fastest and most effective way to reduce greenhouse gas emissions on campus, while also delivering cost savings and public health benefits that are critical to Georgia Tech’s short- and long-term planning and the environment. Past projects include collaboration with campus administration on initiatives such as electrifying landscaping equipment and analyzing the GTPD fleet, which contributed to the introduction of Mach-E electric vehicles for GTPD use.
summary: ElectrifyGT is a student-led advocacy organization advancing the cause of campus electrification at Georgia Tech.

name: Ramblin' Puppy Raisers at Georgia Tech
description: Ramblin' Puppy Raisers promotes awareness of service dogs and their work and training. We educate students about ways to be involved and volunteer in the service dog community, particularly the process of becoming a puppy raiser or puppy sitter. We connect and support students who volunteer to become puppy raisers or puppy sitters in partnership with service dog organizations.
summary: Ramblin’ Puppy Raisers promotes awareness of service dogs and educates, support, and connects students who become involved in the service dog raising communities as volunteer puppy raisers or sitters in partnership with service dog organizations.

name: Omicron Delta Epsilon
description: Omicron Delta Epsilon (ODE) is the international honor society in economics, dedicated to recognizing and promoting academic excellence and professional development among students passionate about the field. Our chapter provides a welcoming and intellectually enriching community where members can connect with like-minded peers, faculty, and alumni through a range of events. We host faculty panels, graduate school and career preparation sessions, and collaborative programs with the Economics Club. Membership in ODE is not only a mark of academic distinction, it’s an opportunity to engage in thought-provoking conversations, explore real-world economic issues, and build meaningful connections that extend beyond the classroom. Whether you're considering a career in economics or simply enjoy learning more about the subject, ODE is a great way to grow and get involved.
summary: Omicron Delta Epsilon at Georgia Tech is the international honor society for economics, dedicated to recognizing academic excellence and fostering a vibrant community of students through events, networking, and professional development opportunities.

name: The Women's Network
description: The Women's Network (TWN) is the largest collegiate women's networking organization in the United States. Our mission is to connect collegiate and recently graduated women and non-binary people to each other and to industry leaders, while celebrating ambition. TWN has a presence on over 100 college campuses in the United States and Canada, and, at present, over 40,000 members in our community. Our network is a great way to form authentic connections, develop leadership skills, gain insight into different careers, and meet other ambitious women on your campus and across the world.
summary: The purpose of The Women's Network is to foster a noncompetitive, uplifting environment for ambitious women to network, cultivate personal success, and discuss the challenges women face in professional settings.

name: Culinary Health and Education For Students
description: CHEFS is an organization dedicated to teaching students cooking skills, financially responsible food shopping, and nutrition fundamentals. CHEFS aims to create a community where students can learn and share culinary knowledge in a fun and social environment.
summary: CHEFS is dedicated to helping students learn basic cooking skills, terms, and techniques in a safe environment.

name: Employee Engagement
description: GTHR Employee Engagement’s department provides resources that cover the spectrum of our five dimensions of well-being: Social, Emotional, Physical, Professional, and Spiritual. Employees can choose from a myriad of topics in our monthly Be Well series or peruse the many resources offered through the University System of Georgia (USG). The USG has also partnered with Kepro to provide a comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP) to Georgia Tech employees and their families. Not only does the EAP provide counseling services, employees can also find an abundance of work-life resources and training opportunities for employees and people leaders. Currently, only a small portion of our employees are taking advantage of this invaluable resource, so we encourage you to promote this serviceto your employees.
summary: GTHR Employee Engagement’s department provides resources that cover the spectrum of our five dimensions of well-being: Social, Emotional, Physical, Professional, and Spiritual.

name: March of Dimes at Georgia Tech
description:
March of Dimes at GT is a chapter of the national nonprofit March of Dimes (www.marchofdimes.org) which combats the morbidities and mortalities of mothers and infants. Georgia is historically known for inadequate care of mothers and infants compared to the United States averages, and Atlanta is even worse in terms of premature birth, health disparities, and maternal morbidities. Through March of Dimes, our organization utilizes direct outreach, advocacy through legislation and education, and fundraising to fight these inequities and support mothers and infants within Atlanta. 

Follow our instagram: @gtmarchofdimes (we are more likely to respond here rather than our gmail)

And join our Groupmeif interested:Groupme
summary: March of Dimes at Georgia Tech works directly with Atlanta mothers, the legislation, and fundraising to combat maternal and infant morbidities

name: Jadoo at Georgia Tech
summary: The purpose of Jadoo is to: Promote South Asian culture and inclusivity at Georgia Tech through the promotion of various dance styles; Perform and compete regionally and around the country; Learn, acclimate, and master various dance styles.

name: Brazilian Jiu Jitsu at Georgia Tech
description:
Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu Club is a student-run organization dedicated to fostering a supportive and inclusive environment for students to learn and practice Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu.

We hold practices three days a week, welcoming members of all experience levels—from beginners to advanced. In addition to regular training sessions, the club hosts events throughout the semester, including UFC watch parties, tournaments, and outreach events, promoting community and supporting members in competition.

Whether you're looking to improve your fitness, learn self-defense, or join a community passionate about Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu, BJJ Club is the perfect place to get involved!

Primary Contact: Discord

Location: CRC Studio A

First Practice: Friday 8/29, 5:00 - 7:00 PM

Fall 2025 Practice Schedule:

Thur: 5:00 - 7:00 PM

Fri: 5:00 - 7:00 PM

Sat 3:30 - 5:30 PM
summary: Brazilian Jiu Jitsu at Georgia Tech facilitates a safe and encouraging environment for beginners and experienced practitioners to develop their skills in Brazilian Jiu Jitsu.

name: Women @ College of Computing
description:
Welcoming, Supporting, and Celebrating Women in Computing at Georgia Tech

Since 2004, W@CC has been committed to building a community for women and non-binary students of the College of Computing, regardless of their major or background. 

Our goal as a club is to provide a space for members to share their experiences, meet other like-minded students, and grow professionally. We meet weekly on Thursdays at 6:30pm within the College of Computing Building. Meetings range from professional development workshops with our company sponsors, to member socials (think boba, pottery, tote bags!) and outings around Atlanta. A few of our events this past year have included workshops with companies like Goldman Sachs, Bloomberg and Cloudflare, a Women's History Month crafting picnic, and an outing to the Atlanta Botanical Gardens :)

 

Check us out on Instagram (@waccgt) and join our slack to be updated on our events, and reach out with any questions!
summary: Women @ College of Computing encourages women in computer science to network with their peers while providing a forum for women in the field and giving others who are interested in promoting women in computer science an outlet to do so.

name: Organization for Social Activism at GT
description: The Organization for Social Activism at GT is a student activist organization created to foster collaboration, conversation, and compassion in college spaces. We serve to educate our peers and draw bridges between students and institutional allies to pursue social justice more cohesively, holistically, and effectively. OSA provides time, space, and resources for students to learn and talk about the issues plaguing ourselves and the world around us. We host open mics to build a community of compassion, topical forums to converse and create crucial allies for collective action, as well as collaborative events/RSO mixers, volunteering opportunities, fundraising, and curate informative, non-partisan posts to educate peers with inclusivity in mind.
summary: OSA at GT is an organization created to foster collaboration, conversation, and compassion in the realm of student activism. We serve to educate our peers and draw bridges between students and institutional allies to pursue social justice effectively.

name: Calisthenics at Georgia Tech
description:
Join our Groupme channel to see all announcements and club communications: Here

Your can view our calendar: Here

The Calisthenics at GT objective is to build a community around functional strength and longevity with the revival of street workout culture.
summary: Calisthenics at GT is for students interested in bodyweight/gymnastics style workouts. We primarily focus on strength based movements such as muscle-up, front lever, planche, handstands, etc.

name: Alpha Lambda Mu
description: Our mission is to connect, build, and sustain a lifelong brotherhood as the first Muslim-interest fraternity at Georgia Institute of Technology through excellence in character, excellence in service, excellence in intellectual standard, excellence in spirituality, and excellence in creativity amongst all members, chapters, conglomerates, and associates.
summary: Alpha Lambda Mu's purpose is to connect, build, and sustain a lifelong brotherhood as the first Muslim-interest fraternity in America.

name: Orthodox Christian Campus Ministries
description:
The vision of OCCM is to:

  1. Inspire and instill in students the commitment to live an Orthodox Christian way of life daily.

  2. Be an integral part of students’ spiritual formation by nurturing them spiritually, scholastically, socially, and even emotionally.
summary: OCCM connects college students in faith, practice, community, and values with the ancient faith of the Oriental Orthodox Christian Church during their collegiate journey through community life, prayer, service to others, and study of the Faith.

name: School of Computational Science and Engineering Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of this Association includes representing the graduate student body of the School of Computational Science and Engineering in all matters concerning academics, welfare, administration, social activities, and other matters specific to graduate students, working with the School of Computational Science and Engineering in promoting greater recognition of graduate student education; promoting closer graduate student-faculty-administration relations; and stimulating interest in and appreciation of graduate education both on and off campus.
summary: The purpose of this Association includes representing the graduate student body of the School of Computational Science and Engineering in all matters concerning academics, welfare, administration, social activities, and other matters specific to grads.

name: Survivor Club at Georgia Tech
description: Survivor Club organizes semester long games of Survivor that mimics the CBS reality tv show of the same name, Survivor. Every semester, 16-20 students are cast to compete for the title of Sole Survivor in physical, mental, social, and strategic competitions first in teams, then individually. Survivor Club welcomes those interested in planning challenges, filming and editing, those looking to make friends or try something new, those looking for a unique extra-curricular, and all interested in the show! In addition to running the game, we often host watch parties for the CBS show live on Wednesdays.
summary: Survivor Club organizes and recreates a semester-long game of the CBS reality TV show Survivor, where GT students compete against each other in physical and mental challenges and strategize to avoid getting voted out and become the sole survivor.

name: 5 Rivers Fishing Club
description:
The Georgia Tech 5 Rivers Fishing Club is a member of Trout Unlimited's 5 Rivers program, which is a network of college clubs focused on fly fishing and the conservation of our waterways. Our club provides a place for all fishermen regardless of experience, providing instruction for those of all skill levels. The club hosts many instructional events led by industry professionals that teach everything from casting a fly rod to on-the-water events where members will be able to catch their first fish or fine-tune their skills!

In addition, the club fosters conservation initiatives that benefit our rivers and streams, as well as the communities that revolve around them. We partner with local Trout Unlimited chapters as well as other nonprofits like Chatahoochee Riverkeeper to help keep our waterways healthy. Through both these missions, we strive to share our passion for fly fishing with all Georgia Tech students in a fun and sustainable way.
summary: The purpose of 5 Rivers Fishing Club is to promote fly fishing as a form of outdoor recreation by bringing together students, faculty, and staff. The club is open to everyone, and often hosts instructional events to help new anglers learn the sport.

name: Young Women for America at Georgia Tech
description:
The purpose of YWA at Georgia Tech is to connect like-minded young women to magnify their voices, lead a movement, and impact culture by promoting biblical values and conservative principles on campus and in the community.

 

We aim to do this through prayer, fellowship, education, and action. YWA at Georgia Tech is dedicated to impacting culture for Christ, focusing on seven core issues: the family, the sanctity of human life, religious liberty, education, sexual exploitation, national sovereignty, and support for Israel.
summary: YWA at GT connects like-minded young women to magnify their voices, lead a movement, and impact culture by promoting biblical values and conservative principles on campus and in the community.

name: Women in Nuclear
description: The goal of WiN at Tech is to foster a culture of inclusion and belonging that encourages a diverse group of students to pursue professions and trades in the nuclear sector, inspire advocacy and public support for the advancement of nuclear energy and technology, and create professional development and networking opportunities for career advancement for students interested in the nuclear industry. WiN is open to anyone and is not exclusive to only women.
summary: WiN is a professional organization of individuals dedicated to advocacy for nuclear energy, technology, and inclusion in the nuclear industry.

name: Black Women Support Group
description:
The purpose of BWSG is: 

  1. To provide a safe space for black women to discuss issues impacting them at a predominantly white institution. 
  2. To encourage the personal, academic, and professional development of black women 
  3. To collaborate with the Georgia Tech community, the Greater Atlanta area, and Georgia Tech black alumnae.
summary: BWSG provides a safe space for black women to discuss issues impacting them at a predominantly white institution, through social, academic, and professional development.

name: Public Policy Undergraduate Student Association
description: PPUSA aims to create a sense of community among current and former students/alumni and provide development opportunities, both academic and professional, for students interested in public policy.
summary: PPUSA aims to create a sense of community among current and former students/alumni and provide development opportunities, both academic and professional, for students interested in public policy.

name: Creatives @ GT
description: The purpose of Creatives at GT is to build a community of creatives at Georgia Tech that gives students a platform where they can express themselves through art, expose them to new avenues of creativity, and inspire them to continuously cultivate their artistic passions. We strive to promote collaboration and inclusivity among people and various art forms.
summary: We are a community of creatives at Georgia Tech that gives students a platform where they can express themselves through art, expose them to new avenues of creativity, and inspire them to continuously cultivate their artistic passions.

name: Disc Golf Club at Georgia Tech
description: Disc Golf Club is a competitive collegiate disc golf team on Tech's campus that is active within the college disc golf scene.
summary: Disc Golf Club is a competitive collegiate disc golf team on Tech's campus that is active within the college disc golf scene.

name: Oral Health Outreach at Georgia Tech
description: Oral health has major effects on quality of life but is often overlooked. Our volunteer mission is to improve oral health for Atlanta's communities in need and for individuals on GT's campus. We will fundraise for & package dental hygiene kits to be distributed consistently with GT’s many volunteer organizations, along with a short note including addresses of free/low-cost dental clinics in Atlanta. We further plan to partner with SGA to increase student access to dental hygiene products in the dorms, and we will take part in other dental-related service in the Atlanta area.
summary: Oral Health Outreach at Georgia Tech's mission is to improve oral health for Atlanta's communities in need and for individuals on GT's campus.

name: K-Buzz
description: As Georgia Tech's official Korean Pop culture club, K-Buzz provides a place to talk about Korean Entertainment, learn about Korean Culture, and promote these things on campus. We're all about fun, music, games, creativity, and finding new friends who share the same interests. From PPT nights to watch parties, discussing a comeback or finding a concert buddy, we strive to create a space where Tech students can feel free to enjoy what they love!
summary: K-Buzz provides a place to talk about Korean Entertainment, specifically K-Pop and K-Dramas, teaches our members more about Korean Culture, and promotes Korean Culture and Entertainment on Campus.

name: MCAT Club
description:
Help students familiarize themselves and prepare for the Medical College Admissions Test (MCAT)

Join the GroupMe here: Link!
summary:
Helping students familiarize themselves and prepare for the Medical College Admissions Test (MCAT).

Join the GroupMe here: https://groupme.com/join_group/92451158/mJTg81mi

name: Madhatter Knits at Georgia Tech
description: Madhatter Knits at Georgia Tech will teach volunteers how to knit hats and supply knit hats for premature babies in neonatal intensive care units (NICUs) all over the state. We will engage our community in different service events and unite towards the goal of raising prematurity awareness through fundraising, socials, and more.
summary: Madhatter Knits at Georgia Tech will teach volunteers how to knit hats and supply knit hats for premature babies in neonatal intensive care units (NICUs) all over the state.

name: Thomistic Institute @ Georgia Tech
description: The Thomistic Institute exists to promote the thought of St. Thomas Aquinas in our contemporary world by strengthening the intellectual formation of students at universities and in the public square. We focus on reading primary and secondary texts situated within the Thomistic Tradition and bringing in speakers to engage in dialectic.
summary: The Thomistic Institute exists to promote the thought of St. Thomas Aquinas in our contemporary world by strengthening the intellectual formation of students at universities and in the public square.

name: Intellectual Property Student Organization
description: The purpose of this organization is to provide opportunities for Georgia Tech students to learn more about the field of Intellectual Property. Beyond simply raising awareness, the organization seeks to provide networking and mentorship opportunities by connecting graduate and undergraduate students with IP-related leaders in the community.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide opportunities for Georgia Tech students to learn more about the field of Intellectual Property.

name: Black Civil and Environmental Engineers at Georgia Tech
summary: Provide resources for black students, specifically civil and environmental engineering, that’ll allow them to prosper mentally, academically, and career-wise.

name: Delta Epsilon Psi
description: We the brothers of Delta Epsilon Psi vow to be a fraternity whose primary purpose is to instill brotherhood, discipline, and commitment within its members through various social and service driven endeavors.
summary: We the brothers of Delta Epsilon Psi vow to be a fraternity whose primary purpose is to instill brotherhood, discipline, and commitment within its members through various social and service driven endeavors.

name: IfYou'reReadingThis
description:
The purpose of IfYoureReadingThis is to build a campus-wide and inter-campus support network for those who struggle with mental illness. We collect letters from faulty, students, and alumni about their mental health journeys so that others can read them and feel less alone.

Our chapter at Georgia Tech not only aims to foster this campus-wide support network, but provide students with tangible means of talking about their mental health, attending events centered around mental health awareness, and advocating for their needs.

Apply to be a Campus Ambassador for us here!

Check out our Linktree!
summary: The purpose of IfYoureReadingThis is to build an institute-wide support network for those who struggle with mental illness.

name: Eritrean-Ethiopian Student Association
description: Our organization seeks to foster a welcoming community that highlights and celebrates Ethiopian and Eritrean culture. We host meetings and events with various fun activities and traditional elements so we can increase representation and enlarge our community at Georgia Tech.
summary: Our organization seeks to increase representation, while celebrating and showcasing Ethiopian & Eritrean culture. We will have meetings with various fun activities to increase our engagement and expand our community at Georgia Tech.

name: Yugioh @ GT
description:
Provide a space for people to play, trade, and learn the Yu-Gi-Oh! TCG. We support all levels of play, from new players to seasoned veterans. Joining the discord will provide the most up-to-date news for meetings, tournaments, and more!

Join us at 

https://discord.gg/H8wckNCgnW
summary: Provide a space for people to play, trade, and learn the Yu-Gi-Oh! TCG.

name: Buzzin' for Babies
description:
A handful of Atlanta-area hospitals maintain Neonatal Intensive Care Units, which are hospital wards for premature babies. Georgia Tech student volunteers can help hospital staff in these NICUs and in other similar hospital wards (such as Mother/Baby units) with simple--but vital!--tasks like feeding, diaper changing, etc. Buzzin' for Babies exists to facilitate this volunteerism by streamlining how GT students will onboard to volunteer with area facilities to help meet hospital needs by committing to volunteer a few hours each month. Buzzin' for Babies also exists to bring together like-minded members of the Georgia Tech community to foster camaraderie among GT students and staff committed to community service.

Applications to join the organization may be submitted by requesting to join the Engage page of B4B or by emailing ppieterick3@gatech.edu
summary: Buzzin' for Babies exists to facilitate volunteerism with Southern Regional Medical Center, a non-profit hospital near ATL airport, that seeks help in its Neonatal Intensive Care Unit, a hospital ward for premature babies, and its Mother/Baby Unit.

name: Woodruff School Graduate Women
description: The purpose of Woodruff School Graduate Women (WSGW) is to serve the academic, social, and career needs of women graduate students in the Woodruff School of Mechanical Engineering at Georgia Tech. We are already an active organization in the School of Mechanical Engineering. We plan social events for our members, organize mentorship lunches with faculty, assist with graduate student recruitment, and partner with other Woodruff School student organizations to host additional events.
summary: The purpose of Woodruff School Graduate Women (WSGW) is to serve the academic, social, and career needs of women graduate students in the Woodruff School of Mechanical Engineering at Georgia Tech.

name: Students Observing and Researching Meteorology Club
description:
At StORM Club, we are committed to the following mission:

1 - Promote meteorological awareness in the local community. The weather affects everyone, so it's important to increase community awareness and understanding of the role it plays in our daily lives.
