2 - Help jumpstart and develop the networks of meteorology students and enthusiasts by hosting networking events, field trips, and by connecting them with professionals in the field through interactive experiences.
3 - Create opportunities for faculty, graduate students, students, and hobbyists alike to learn more about and showcase their meteorological interests with custom presentation days, fun Kahoot quizzes, live significant event tracking meet-ups, and so much more!
4 - Provide a safe, fun, and positive environment for all club members to feel comfortable and thrive in.

Georgia Tech's StORM CLUB is a proud chapter of the American Meteorological Society.
summary: StORM Club is Georgia Tech’s meteorology hub. We promote community awareness and provide students with fun, productive opportunities to network and jumpstart their atmospheric careers.

name: Aero Makers at Georgia Tech
description: The purpose of AMGT is to foster an inclusive and friendly environment between the mentors, users, and larger Aerospace community through making and engineering with events, equipment, and a broad campus reach.
summary: The purpose of AMGT is to foster an inclusive and friendly environment between the mentors, users, and larger Aerospace community!

name: WebDev @ GT
description: In WebDev @ GT, we aim to foster students' learning of web development technologies and techniques. We have project teams that create interactive web applications; some past examples include a web-based video conference platform, a 3D map of Georgia Tech's campus, and an application that leverages GPT API to play Dungeons and Dragons. We also have a bootcamp program that enables students to learn web development from scratch. We start by teaching basic web development technologies like HTML and CSS, then moved onto more advanced technologies like React, Node.js, and MongoDB.
summary: In WebDev @ GT, we aim to foster students' learning of web development technologies and techniques.

name: Medical Brigades @ GT
description:
Medical Brigades @ GT plans, funds, and goes on international medical trips to countries across the world, such as Ghana, Nicaragua, Guatemala, Panama, Honduras, or Greece, to support underserved communities with lacking healthcare systems. When not going on these brigades, we host a variety of local volunteering and advocacy opportunities to support communities and healthcare systems locally.

Join our GroupMe for more updates:

GroupMe link
summary: Medical Brigades @ GT plans, funds, and goes on international medical trips to countries across the world such as Ghana, Nicaragua, Guatemala, Panama, Honduras, or Greece to support underserved communities with lacking healthcare systems.

name: Bioinformatics at Georgia Tech
description:
Through Bioinformatics at GT, we strive to connect students interested in the intersection between computer science and biology to industry and research, while also pursuing projects relevant to the spaces of bioinformatics and computational biology.

For the most recent information and meeting times, check out our website: https://gtbioinformatics.framer.website/
summary: Through Bioinformatics at GT, we strive to connect students interested in the intersection between computer science and biology to industry and research, while also pursuing projects relevant to the spaces of bioinformatics and computational biology.

name: HOSA Future Health Professionals at Georgia Tech
description: To carry out the mission of the International HOSA organization on Georgia Tech's campus to achieve a seamless transition from education to a health profession by helping every member to develop leadership skills, practice technical health skills, and refine team skills.
summary: We carry out the mission of the International HOSA organization on campus to achieve a seamless transition from education to a health profession by helping every member to develop leadership skills, practice technical skills, and refine team skills.

name: Micro-Physiological Systems Society
description: We aim to setup a platform to showcase research done at Georgia Tech in the space of micro-physiological systems, discuss exciting new developments within the field, and provide networking opportunities for students and related external organizations.
summary: We aim to setup a platform to showcase research done at Georgia Tech in the space of micro-physiological systems, discuss exciting new developments within the field, and provide networking opportunities for students and related external organizations.

name: Research Insights Club
description:
Research Insights Club (RIC) is a student organization dedicated to teaching researchers valuable skills including writing, figure making, project management, mentorship, and much more. We strive towards building a vibrant community of researchers from all fields who collaborate to share knowledge and help each other on their scientific endeavors.

Our efforts are divided amongst three committees: Resource Development, Communications, and Events. Members will join one committee of their interest where they will support the clubs' initiatives and gain marketable skills that many have added to their resumes. Members are eligible to join multiple committees if they wish.

Whether you're totally new to research or have been involved before, Research Insights Club is a great way to help you and the GT community navigate the research maze.
summary: Our organization's purpose is to disseminate detailed and hard-to-obtain knowledge to Georgia Tech students to help them navigate and thrive in the complex field of academic research.

name: Real Estate at Georgia Tech
description:
The Georgia Tech Real Estate Club provides opportunities for Georgia Tech students to learn and engage with Atlanta area Real Estate projects, professionals, and firms through networking events, workshops, site tours, and industry presentations to further their career prospects. We focus on all disciplines of commercial real estate including development, construction, acquisitions, asset management, capital markets, lending, brokerage, architecture, and more.

Please visit www.reatgt.org to learn more about our organization and upcoming events.
summary: The Georgia Tech Real Estate Club provides opportunities for students to learn and engage with Atlanta area Real Estate projects, professionals, and firms through workshops, site tours, and industry presentations to further their career prospects.

name: Naturally Me
description: Naturally Me aims to allow students to express their passion for natural hair and establish a continuum of exploring more about what it means or how it looks to wear it as a crown with such pride. This continuum of learning will: emphasize the importance of the history behind natural hair, change the stigma of natural hair, and help others create a personalized relationship with their natural hair. Naturally Me serves as an opportunity to create an environment that allows students to take part in endless discussions/activities such as workshops, partnerships or collaborations, and panels. Additionally, it will create an inclusive, communal space that encourages both men and women who are willing to educate themselves about the maintenance of natural hair as well as individuals who would be willing to learn about our culture if interested.
summary: Naturally Me aims to allow students to express their passion for natural hair and establish a continuum of exploring more about what it means or how it looks to wear it as a crown with such pride.

name: Birdwatchers
description:
Birdwatchers is an organization devoted to birding, science, conservation, and community. Birdwatchers aims to increase appreciation for birds on campus, connect students with other birdwatchers and ornithologists, and provide opportunities for the greater enjoyment of birds.

 

There are many different ways you can choose to participate in the club, formally and informally:

- attend general meetings, every Wednesday 6:30-8pm
- join the  Discord
- go birding on campus and submit your checklists to the campus eBird hotspot
- join our iNat project and submit your bird observations
- report dead birds on campus to dBird.org
- follow us on Instagram to see event highlights
summary: Birdwatchers aims to increase appreciation for birds on campus, connect students with other birdwatchers and ornithologists, and provide opportunities for the greater enjoyment of birds.

name: Ethics Bowl at Georgia Tech
description:
Ethics Bowl at Georgia Tech aims to:

a. Educate members on ethical theories, philosophical analysis, and other skills relevant to philosophy and ethics.
b. Facilitate competition by members in the APPE Regional and National Ethics Bowl, the National Bioethics Bowl, and other Collegiate Ethics Bowl Tournaments. 
 
Find more information at our Website or email us at ethicsbowlgt@gmail.com!
summary:
Ethics Bowl at Georgia Tech aims to:

a. Educate members on ethical theories, philosophical analysis, and other skills relevant to philosophy and ethics.

b. Facilitate competition by members in Collegiate Ethics Bowl Tournaments.

name: Wrestling Club @GT
description:
Wrestling Club aims to:

1. Give a space for students to come learn and practice wrestling skills and techniques.

2. To hold regular practices so that students can develop as wrestlers.

3. To give students a chance to compete in live wrestling, in practice and at events.

 

Our Fall 2025 Practice Schedule is as follows:

Monday 6:00-8:00 PM in the CRC Studio A

Wednesday 6:00-8:00 PM in the CRC Auxiliary Gym

Sunday 6:00-8:00 PM in the CRC Studio A
summary: Wrestling Club aims to give a space for students to come learn and practice wrestling skills and techniques.

name: Squash Club @ Georgia Tech
description: We're a club dedicated to providing a friendly environment for people to connect with others to play or learn squash. Anyone can join, including complete beginners who don't know how to play to well-tenured squash goers. Every semester, we plan to host a tournament with prizes for the top winners and social events that build a community and allow others to connect. We plan to pass bills that would allocate funds towards purchasing top-of-the-line equipment that Squash Club members can exclusively use. We also look towards possibly passing a bill that could be used to renovate one of the racketball rooms in the CRC and transform it into a squash court. As a whole, we are dedicated to building a community for developing your squash skills, creating new friends, and enjoying the game.
summary: We're a club dedicated to providing a friendly environment for people to connect with others to play or learn squash.

name: Orthodox Christian Fellowship
description: OCF exists to foster the community of Orthodox Christian students through community life, prayer, service to others and study of the Faith.
summary: OCF exists to support Orthodox Christian students through community life, prayer, service to others and study of the Faith.

name: Ghanaian Student Association @ Georgia Tech
description: The purpose of the GSA@GT is to bring together all GT students of Ghanaian descent together. In addition, the organization will develop programs to assist its members to realize their full professional potential at Georgia Tech; promote within, and provide access to members the opportunities to network and create a community for current and future Ghanaian students at Georgia Tech; educate the Georgia Tech community on Ghanaian culture and customs; and finally to serve as the beginning of a strong alumni for Ghanaian students at GT.
summary: Ghanaian Student Association @ Georgia Tech exists to bring together all GT students of Ghanaian descent together.

name: WreckCon
description: WreckCon Organization plans and runs the event WreckCon every year. This is a convention that celebrates nerdier interests in the student body and Georgia Tech campus culture. The organization also hosts supplementary events throughout the year.
summary: Wreck Convention (WreckCon) at Georgia Tech plans and runs the event WreckCon every year. This is a convention that celebrates nerdier interests in the student body. The organization also hosts supplementary events throughout the year.

name: Emergency Medical Services at Tech
description:
EMS at Tech seeks to expand knowledge of and access to emergency medical services among the campus community. This is done through outreach initiatives and training events for the general public, as well as educational opportunities and clinical experience for organization members.

Applications for membership should be submitted through our application form: https://sites.gatech.edu/emsat/get-involved/
summary: EMS at Tech seeks to expand knowledge of and access to emergency medical services among the campus community.

name: Supercomputing @ GT
description:
Supercomputing @ GT is a student-run organization under the Georgia Tech College of Computing. Our mission is to make supercomputing/HPC accessible to students and to create a community around it. We welcome beginners and strive to provide space for students to gain insight into the field. Members get exposed to Georgia Tech supercomputing resources, research, and opportunities. Our most popular event tends to be our Supercomputing Faculty Panel which features professors and researchers at Georgia Tech! We also feature workshops, talks, and guest speaker events from industry and national labs.

We meet Tuesdays @ 5pm. Location and details can be seen on our Discord or Instagram page (on our Linktree). Please fill out the Interest Form on our Linktree to get added to our club newsletter!
summary: Supercomputing @ GT's purpose is to make supercomputing/HPC accessible to students, expose Georgia Tech supercomputing resources & research, educate beginners, and create an active community of HPC (high-performance computing) enthusiasts.

name: AfroDance Team
description: The AfroDance Team is a dance group that celebrates the rich and diverse cultures of Africa through dance. Our ensembles blends traditional and contemporary dance styles influenced by various African countries, such as Nigeria, South Africa, Ghana, Congo, and Tanzania. We offer dynamic performances for events and festivals, as well as interactive workshops and practices for all skill levels. We aim to foster a greater appreciation for the diverse African cultures and an environment that encourages an expression of creativity through dance.
summary: The AfroDance Team aims to foster a greater appreciation for the diverse African cultures and foster an environment that encourages an expression of creativity through dance.

name: Writing Club @GT
description: Within this organization, we believe that each student has a story to share, which can make them feel seen and validated. In addition, it presents opportunities for others to become inspired and have a new perspective. Therefore, we wish to foster creativity and openness throughout campus. Lastly, we will provide resources for those who want help in improving their writing skills, both for personal and school/business. We hope to keep the art of writing alive, especially in a place where an activity like this can be often overshadowed.
summary: Writing Club @GT intends to create a safe space for people to express themselves with creatively written forms of communication, including but not limited to, poetry, stories, songs, and scripts .

name: John Lewis Student Leadership Pathways
description:
The John Lewis Student Leadership Pathways (JLSLP) equips participants with the skills they need to make meaningful changes within their community. In addition, the Pathways aim to inspire students to steadily increase their leadership capacities using the Aspen Leadership Development Index as the framework by which students approach leadership.

 

Students pursuing this leadership path will be able to enhance their leadership skills with an emphasis on social change. It is the mission of the JLSLP to prepare participants to be change agents in their community.

 

All Georgia Tech students are eligible to apply for the JLSLP at any time within their college career! Students earn pathway levels of learning by attending workshops, retreats, service projects, and programs offered by JLSLP and other Georgia Tech departments. Student progress is managed through the Engage website. Students who participate in the program will be able to review their progress throughout the program at any time. Students will have the opportunity to Engage in the pathways throughout their college experience. Our program is offered to all students undergrad and graduate students! 

 

Mission: The John Lewis Student Leadership Pathways are dedicated to cultivating visionary and ethical leaders who are empowered to inspire positive change in their communities and beyond. Through comprehensive programming, experiential learning and mentorship students will gain a greater understanding of their strengths and personal values. The program is designed to shape individuals into influential leaders who champion innovation, foster collaboration, and drive sustainable solutions for a better future.

 

Join the John Lewis Student Leadership Pathways by requesting to join this page!!

ATTENTION CAMPUS PARTNERS:

To streamline the process and to aid with event tracking, we have updated Engage to include supplemental questions for all campus partners to complete to ensure each program aligns with our leadership framework, the Aspen Index. Now, when you tag events to count in the pathways, you will answer a questionnaire selecting the leadership category and capacities and listing your program's learning outcomes. We will then review your submission and determine if the event counts towards the Pathways.
summary: Join the John Lewis Student Leadership Pathways by requesting to join this page!!

organizationType.name: Division of Student Engagement and Well-being

name: H. Milton Stewart School of Industrial and Systems Engineering (ISyE)
description: The Stewart School of ISyE offers undergraduates a variety of ways to become involved in research activities. Our program is designed to allow undergraduate students from different disciplines and programs to explore their passions, deepen their knowledge, and contribute to the advancement of the applications or methodologies in variety of research thrusts impacting many of the world's most pressing challenges.
summary: The Stewart School of ISyE offers undergraduates a variety of ways to become involved in research activities. Our program is designed to allow students to contribute to the advancement of impacting many of the world's most pressing challenges.

name: Office of Sustainability
description: The Office of Sustainability is an Office of Infrastructure and Sustainability at Georgia Tech. Our mission is to serve as a collaborative partner working toward sustainable and equitable solutions for our Georgia Tech community.
summary: The Office of Sustainability is an Office of Infrastructure and Sustainability at Georgia Tech. Our mission is to serve as a collaborative partner working toward sustainable and equitable solutions for our Georgia Tech community.

name: Atlanta University Center Transfer Student Association
description:
The purpose of the AUC TSA is to:

Cultivate an inclusive and hospitable community for both incoming and existing transfer students from the Atlanta University Center (AUC) at the Georgia Institute of Technology. We aim to actively promote student engagement while fostering a collaborative environment beneficial for establishing academic, professional, and social networks and relationships.

Coordinate and facilitate outreach initiatives within the AUC and its esteemed institutions, including Clark Atlanta University, Spelman College, and Morehouse College. These initiatives are designed to ignite curiosity, unveil available resources and opportunities, and forge meaningful connections with AUC Dual Degree Engineering Students and prospective students to the Georgia Institute of Technology.

Establish a distinguished alumni network of accomplished graduates who have completed the Dual Degree Engineering Program. We aim to develop a network of willing to mentor and encouraging alumni to inspire upcoming generations of participants in the AUC Georgia Institute of Technology graduates, perpetuating a legacy of guidance and excellence.
summary: The purpose of the AUC TSA is to cultivate an inclusive and hospitable community for both incoming and existing transfer students from the Atlanta University Center (AUC) at the Georgia Institute of Technology.

name: Young Americans for Liberty at Georgia Tech
description: The Georgia Tech chapter of Young Americans for Liberty. We are a youth organization that champions individual liberty, limited government, and personal responsibility. We strive to create an atmosphere of open dialogue about the political and economic state of the country and advocate for policies that protect individual rights on campus. We aim to foster civil discussions to empower future leaders who challenge the status quo and advocate for lasting change.
summary: Young Americans for Liberty at Georgia Tech aims to create a campus community that champions individual liberty, limited government, and personal responsibility.

name: Project Sunshine at Georgia Tech
description: Project Sunshine brings volunteer-led programming to pediatric patients and their families in a variety of medical settings. We recognize the tremendous weight of managing a child’s illness or injury, whether it requires short-term or long-term care. Every child with a medical challenge is unique, and our programs seek to adapt to the individual needs of each child. The organization's mission is to deliver the healing power of play to children with medical needs through trained and dedicated volunteers.
summary: Project Sunshine brings volunteer-led programming to pediatric patients and their families in a variety of medical settings. The organization's mission is to deliver the healing power of play to children with medical needs.

name: SiliconJackets
description:
The main mission of SiliconJackets is to create an environment which makes the design of silicon hardware accessible to students. Our 4 main goals are:

1. Give students the opportunity and resources to enhance their education through the design, verification, test, and fabrication of an ASIC

2. Complement ECE course offerings by filling in the gaps between courses training and industry practices

3. Make students more marketable to companies and connect students with better industry opportunities​

4. Establish Georgia Tech as a hub for semiconductor innovation

If you're interested in joining us, please join our server here:
summary: The main mission of SiliconJackets is to create an environment which makes the design of silicon hardware accessible to students.

name: American Concrete Institute at Georgia Tech
description: The purpose of the Student Chapter is to provide opportunities for the professional development of its members related to the design and construction of concrete structures and activities in the concrete industry. The Student Chapter will work closely with the Georgia Chapter of ACI to provide interaction with engineering professionals from the local community.
summary: The purpose of the Student Chapter is to provide opportunities for the professional development of its members related to the design and construction of concrete structures and activities in the concrete industry.

name: Black Students in Industrial Design
description: Black Students in Design @ GT (BID) is a student led organization that aims to strengthen the community of black students within the College of Design and the broader creative community at GT. Bi-weekly meetings are held and are filled with fun activities, technical workshops, guest speakers, collaborations with other organizations, and much more!
summary: Black Students in Design @ GT (BID) is a student led organization that aims to strengthen the community of Black students within the College of Design.

name: School of Cybersecurity and Privacy Graduate Student Association
description: The School of Cybersecurity & Privacy Graduate Student Association (SCP GSA) is a student-run organization whose purpose is to represent graduate students of SCP in all matters concerning academics, welfare, administration, social activities, and other matters specific to graduate students, working with the School of Cybersecurity & Privacy in promoting greater recognition of graduate student education; promoting closer graduate student-faculty-administration relations; and stimulating interest in and appreciation of graduate education both on and off-campus.
summary: The School of Cybersecurity & Privacy Graduate Student Association (SCP GSA) is a student-run organization whose purpose is to represent graduate students of SCP in all matters concerning graduate students working with the SCP.

name: Voters of Tomorrow at Georgia Tech
description: VOT@GT is a non-partisan, pro-democracy organization that seeks to educate, mobilize, and represent our generation of voters on the Georgia Tech campus. We encourage students to vote and mobilize in their respective communities. We aim to inform students about opportunities for political activism while engaging with the student community on campus. We work with non-partisan organizations outside of Tech that share similar values as well as on-campus organizations that seek to spark political activism. As an organization, we want to serve as a resource for students on campus.
summary: VOT@GT is a student-led, non-partisan organization focused on engaging Georgia Tech students in politics, encouraging voter registration, and informing them about political events & various forms of activism.

name: Hacky Sack at Georgia Tech
description: The purpose of Hacky Sack at Georgia Tech is to, A) Introduce and teach Georgia Tech students about hacky sacks and hacky sacking. B) Offer students a hands-on learning experience by providing training on how to responsibly build and handle hacky sacks in a safe and controlled manner. C) Maintain a recreational community of hacky sackers.
summary: The purpose of Hacky Sack at Georgia Tech is to introduce and teach Georgia Tech students about hacky sacks and hacky sacking.

name: Dragonflyers
description: Welcome to Dragonflyers! We're a small club who enjoys learning and performing with the Chinese Yoyo (aka Diabolo). Whether you're a freshman coming in wanting to try something new or an master's student who wants to pick up a new skill, everyone is welcome from all skill levels. Come join us if you want learn or perform at some of the largest Student Org events around and off-campus!
summary: The Dragonflyers is the Chinese Yoyo/Diabolo club here at GT. We learn, practice, and perform all around campus. Whether you're brand new, did a little in the past, or have already performed at competitions, we would love to have you!

name: Biomedical Engineering Transfer Students
description: Biomedical Engineering Transfer Students (BMETs) is a student organization that is designed to harness incoming transfer BME students with the proper campus resources and community to ease their transition into the campus culture of Georgia Tech.
summary: Biomedical Engineering Transfer Students (BMETs) is a student organization that is designed to harness incoming transfer BME students with the proper campus resources and community to ease their transition into the campus culture of Georgia Tech.

name: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People @ GT
description: The principal objectives of the Association shall be to ensure the political, educational, social and economic equality of all citizens; to achieve equality of rights and eliminate race prejudice among the citizens of the United States; to remove all barriers of racial discrimination through democratic processes; to seek enactment and enforcement of federal, state and local laws securing civil rights; to inform the public of the adverse effects of racial discrimination and to seek its elimination; to educate persons as to their constitutional rights and to take all lawful action to secure the exercise thereof, and to take any other lawful action in furtherance of these objectives, consistent with the NAACP’s.
summary: The principal objectives of the Association shall be to ensure the political, educational, social, and economic equality of all citizens.

name: The Blueprint
description: The Blueprint, published annually, serves as the official yearbook of the Georgia Institute of Technology and documents student life at the Institute.
summary: The Blueprint, published annually, serves as the official yearbook of the Georgia Institute of Technology and documents student life at the Institute.

name: Warhammer Club @ GT
description:
Warhammer Club @ GT is a club centered around building a Warhammer/wargaming community here at GT. We are involved in all facets of the Warhammer hobby including painting, gaming, and narrative play. We are welcoming of everyone and especially of those who are new and would like to check the hobby out! If you are interested feel free to join our discord at 

https://discord.gg/ZFtRW83P
summary: Warhammer club is building a community around the tabletop game franchise Warhammer and other wargames.

name: The American Lung Cancer Screening Initiative at Georgia Tech
description: The mission statement of GT ALCSI shall be to promote awareness for lung cancer screening for individuals at high-risk of developing lung cancer at Georgia Tech and the larger local community through educational efforts, community outreach, and health literacy engagement. Activities may include advocating for Congress members to support a lung cancer bill, reforming lung cancer screening curriculum at local medical schools, presenting to various stakeholders to build awareness, and working with local health departments to bring actionable change to the local communities.
summary: The mission statement of GT ALCSI shall be to promote awareness for lung cancer screening for individuals at high-risk of developing lung cancer at Georgia Tech and the larger local community.

name: Guard Club @ GT
description: Guard Club @ GT's purpose is to facilitate the teaching of the art of Colorguard, which consists of choreographed dancing through the use of spinning equipment like flags, wooden rifles, and sabres, to the general campus community in a more accessible manner.
summary: Guard Club @ GT's purpose is to support the teaching of, and help individuals build upon, the art of Colorguard in the Georgia Tech community.

name: Solar Decathlon at Georgia Tech
description: The purpose of the organization is to create a platform through which Georgia Tech students of all majors and ages can compete in the US Department of Energy's Solar Decathlon competition on an annual basis. The Solar Decathlon competition challenges student teams to design and build energy-efficient, solar-powered houses that are cost-effective, energy-efficient, and aesthetically pleasing. Each year, they host a design challenge in which a team creates a design for a hypothetical construction project of their choosing from the available categories (residential, commercial, office, etc.). Every other year, they host a build challenge in which a team makes a 2-year commitment to design and build a sustainable home. The competition inherently emphasizes interdisciplinary teams from Schools all across our Institute, and radical collaboration leads to successful projects.
summary: The purpose of the organization is to create a platform through which Georgia Tech students of all majors and ages can compete in the US Department of Energy's Solar Decathlon competition on an annual basis.

name: National Residence Hall Honorary
description: The National Residence Hall Honorary is a national organization devoted to honoring those who embody its pillars of Service and Recognition and furthering the pursuit of those pillars across the nation.​ Its membership includes leaders who’ve worked to make a difference at Tech through Service and Recognition and is capped at 1% of the on-campus population.​We promote service through on and off-campus community volunteering. For instance, at Georgia Tech, we work closely with RHA, dedicating our time and service to assist them in any way possible. In doing so we inspire personal growth and development. We promote recognition through submissions of Of The Months (OTMs) which are used acknowledge outstanding members of the housing community on regional and national levels.
summary: The National Residence Hall Honorary is a national organization devoted to honoring those who embody its pillars of Service and Recognition and furthering the pursuit of those pillars across the nation.​

name: Russian Speaking Association
description: GT's hub for all things Russian. Whether you're taking a RUSS class, are interested in learning more about Eastern European culture, or just want to eat some authentic bliny and pelmeni, RSA is here to connect you with native and heritage speakers.
summary: Russian Speaking Association aims to share the culture of Russian-speaking countries.

name: Remote Area Medical at Georgia Tech
description: Remote Area Medical (RAM) provides free mobile medical and dental services to remote communities that lack access to a wide variety of health services. RAM at Georgia Tech will coordinate trips to RAM pop-up clinics throughout the Southeast in locations such as Louisville, Cleveland, and Knoxville. Members can expect to offer chair side assistance for providers, register patients, take vitals, serve as translators, and help facilitate the overall flow of the clinic. The RAM chapter at Georgia Tech will also fundraise in hopes of hosting our own clinic in underserved Georgia communities.
summary: Remote Area Medical (RAM) is a mobile pop-up clinic that provides free medial, dental, and vision services to rural communities in need.

name: Special Pops at Georgia Tech
description: Special Pops at Georgia Tech is a chapter of the Special Pops Tennis Program Inc. Special Pops is an adaptive tennis program with a mission to enrich the lives of children and adults (athletes) with intellectual disabilities through the lifetime sport of tennis. We hope to help bridge the gap between Georgia Tech students and neurodiverse populations in the metro-Atlanta area.
summary: Through Special Pops at Georgia Tech, I hope to offer a service of teaching tennis to neurologically diverse populations around the metro-Atlanta area.

name: Supersonics Club at Georgia Tech
description: The Supersonics Club at Georgia Tech purpose is to bring the design, analysis, optimization, building, and testing high-speed aircraft systems into the hands of Georgia Tech students. We have plans to bring different vehicle configurations to their physical form. Our first goal is to break the Guinness World Record for the fastest remote-control jet-powered plane, currently set at 462 mph. This program will continually iterate the Record Breaker vehicles to try and break our own record for years to come. From here, we will work to become the first student-led collegiate team to build an aircraft capable of breaking the sound barrier, by any means necessary. Best of luck, and fly fast!
summary: GTSC's purpose is to bring the design, analysis, optimization, building, and testing of high-speed aircraft systems into the hands of Georgia Tech students.

name: MedTech Hack
description: MedTech Hack is a medical device hackathon set up through 3 months of prototype planning, and training workshops, culminating in a 36 hour build event. The organization aims to provide opportunities for students to take classroom theory to real-world application and create impactful medical devices. Any Georgia Tech student can sign up for free, and teams comprised of 4-5 members will prototype their medical device idea based on a specific track of their choice.
summary: MedTech Hack is a medical device hackathon set up through 3 months of prototype planning, and training workshops, culminating in a 36 hour build event.

name: Drone, Radio control, and Experimental Aircraft Club at Georgia Tech
description:
The purpose of DRXC is to promote radio-controlled multirotor and fixed-wing design, building, and flight as a hobby among students at Georgia Tech and to support radio-controlled aircraft competition teams, such as the Design-Build Vertical Flight Team, started in 2023. DRXC is a chartered chapter of the Academy of Model Aviation, allowing us to maintain good standing with the FAA and other organizations that regulate drone use/ flight cost-effectively.

DRXC hosts the Design, Build, Vertical Flight (DBVF) Competition team. This competition challenges teams to design and build a VTOL aircraft capable of completing a specific mission. This year, the mission is designed to simulate the delivery of supplies to support forest firefighting. We are excited to compete this year after getting third place in our first year participating in the competition.
summary: The purpose of DRXC is to promote radio-controlled multirotor and fixed-wing design, building, and flight as a hobby among Georgia Tech students and to support radio-controlled aircraft competition teams.

name: ColorStack at Georgia Tech
description:
ColorStack @ Georgia Tech is open to all GT students pursuing careers in tech. Our chapter is centered on supporting Black and Latinx students and allies through community, mentorship, and career development.

What we do:

  • Community + belonging: consistent meetups that help students stay connected and supported
  • Skill-building: workshops on technical prep, resumes, LinkedIn, and interviewing
  • Mentorship: peer and professional guidance from students and industry mentors
  • Career access: networking with employers, referrals, and internship/full-time readiness
  • Opportunity exposure: hackathons and conference support to grow skills and connections

Our goal: increase the number of Black, Latinx and allies who launch rewarding technical careers while welcoming allies and building a stronger tech pipeline at Georgia Tech.
summary: Open to all GT students. We build community, mentorship, and career readiness centered on supporting Black and Latinx students and allies pursuing tech careers.

name: International Students and Professionals in Aerospace @ Georgia Tech
description: International Students and Professionals in Aerospace (ISPA) is a student organization that aims to share knowledge on opportunities and advocate for solutions to issues faced by international students in the aerospace field at Georgia Tech.
summary: ISPA@GT shares knowledge on opportunities and obstacles for foreign nationals in the aerospace industry and advocates for issues faced by international students studying aerospace engineering in the United States.

name: GreyHat
description:
The purpose of this organization is to spread awareness of security practices as well as create an engaging community of students interested in security. As part of this, we will prepare for and compete in security competitions.

Join the Discord server for up-to-date meeting and event information: https://discord.gg/wQWyKpGNpC
summary: The purpose of this organization is to spread awareness of security practices as well as create an engaging community of students interested in security. As part of this, we will prepare for and play in security competitions.

name: Campus Cats
description: Campus Cats cares for the stray and feral cats that live on and around the Georgia Tech campus. This includes feeding, assisting in TNR (trap, neuter, release), vaccinations, other veterinary care, and tracking known campus cats.
summary: Campus Cats cares for the stray and feral cats that live on and around the Georgia Tech campus.

name: Science & Technology Policy Connections at Georgia Tech
description:
Through S&T Policy Connections at Georgia Tech, students have the opportunity to craft innovative policy solutions to prominent science & technology issues and present these policy solutions to state and federal policymakers.

The goals of S&T Policy Connections are to (1) unite students, policymakers, and S&T experts, (2) increase knowledge and awareness of complex S&T issues, (3) support policymakers to craft solutions around S&T issues, (4) promote ideological diversity, interdisciplinary discussion, and informed decision-making around S&T policy, and (5) invite youth to contribute to S&T policy.
summary: Through S&TPC, students have the opportunity to craft innovative policy solutions to prominent science & technology issues and present these policy solutions to state and federal policymakers.

name: A-Town Showdown
description: A-Town Showdown hosts an Indian dance competition for both the fusion and garba-raas dance circuit.
summary: A-Town Showdown hosts an Indian dance competition for both the fusion and garba-raas dance circuit.

name: Waterski and Wakeboard Club at Georgia Tech
description: Our goal is to grow the sports of waterski and wakeboard through practice and tournament participation. All skill levels will be welcome, as the purpose of this organization will always be to encourage participation in the sport.
summary: We aim to grow the sports of waterski and wakeboard through practice and tournament participation. All skill levels will be welcome, as the purpose of this organization will always be to encourage participation in the sport.

name: Sigma Upsilon Chapter of Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc.
description: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. is an organization dedicated to the advancement of its members as well as the community. With a motto of Greater Service, Greater Progress, it is our organization's firm belief that giving back to the community is the best way to see societal progression. Being a member of this organization opens one up to advancement in social, personal, and professional skills.
summary: As organization that focuses on Scholarship, Sisterhood, and Service, we firmly believe the best way to advance ourselves is to advance our communities. Being a member of this organization opens one up to social, personal, and professional advancement.

name: Institute for Electronics and Nanotechnology
description: As one of 10 interdisciplinary research institutes at Georgia Tech IEN facilitates interdisciplinary team forming and research, operates state-of-the-art core facilities, connects with external partners, and runs outreach and workforce development programs.
summary: The Institute for Electronics and Nanotechnology (IEN) supports the campus-wide electronics and nanotechnology community at Georgia Tech by connecting researchers across academic disciplines.

name: Georgia Tech Athletics Association
description: With 400-plus student-athletes across 17 varsity sports, Georgia Tech competes at the highest level of intercollegiate athletics as a member of NCAA Division I and the Atlantic Coast Conference (ACC), while also developing young people who will change the world. To stay up to date on the Yellow Jackets, go to ramblinwreck.com
summary: Georgia Tech Athletics Association

name: Equity and Compliance Programs
description: Equity and Compliance Programs is here to educate, identify, and illuminate systemic and institutional barriers to equity and inclusion at Georgia Tech; while creating a culture beyond compliance for ADA and Title IX.
summary: Equity and Compliance Programs

name: Center for Education Integrating Science, Mathematics, and Computing
description: The Center for Education Integrating Science, Mathematics, and Computing (CEISMC) is Georgia Tech's K-12 STEM Education department. We have many ways for undergraduate and graduate student organizations to get involved with K-12 outreach during the school year and in the summer. Visit https://ceismc.gatech.edu/ to learn more about our work.
summary: GT CEISMC K-12 STEM Outreach Opportunities

name: Lambda Chi Alpha
description: The purpose of Lambda Chi Alpha is to create a brotherhood that serves to develop young men in such a way that they enter their post-college years equipped with the relational and professional skills to lead fulfilling lives in all areas and to leave a positive impact on their families and communities. The social events, leadership roles, philanthropy efforts, and other experiences that members share during their active years work to form brothers into ideal men of character; selfless, loyal, and devoted to honorable pursuits throughout their lives.
summary: The purpose of Lambda Chi Alpha is to create a brotherhood that serves to develop young men in such a way that they enter their post-college years equipped with the relational and professional skills to lead fulfilling lives in all areas.

name: Center for Student Engagement
description:
With a holistic view of student development, the Center for Student Engagement invests in the empowerment and support of registered student organizations which catalyze belonging and an engaged campus community. The Center for Student Engagement aims to support a campus community that provides all Georgia Tech students safe and meaningful engagement opportunities.

All RSOs are supported and managed through the Center for Student Engagement by the Student Organizations Team, which serves to facilitate the registration, oversight, and support of all RSOs.

The Center for Student Engagement is comprised of 5 areas: Civic Engagement, Fraternity & Sorority Life, Student Media, the John Lewis Student Leadership Pathways, and Student Organizations.
summary: The Center for Student Engagement aims to support a campus community that provides all students safe and meaningful engagement opportunities. The Center staff is committed to helping students find where they can grow and thrive on campus.

organizationType.name: Division of Student Engagement and Well-being

name: Black, Latino, & Indigenous in Electrical & Computer Engineering
description:
The purpose of BLIECE is to create a community within the School of Electrical and Computer Engineering at Georgia Tech where minorities may seek friendship, support, advice, and mentorship in regard to their academic, professional, and personal lives. Additionally, BLIECE seeks to increase the number of underrepresented students in ECE. If you are a current GT ECE student, please join our groupme: Link. 

If you are looking to sponsor or support BLIECE in anyway please email us at bliece-president@ece.gatech.edu
summary: The purpose of BLIECE is to create a community within the School of Electrical and Computer Engineering at Georgia Tech where minorities may seek friendship, support, advice, and mentorship in regard to their academic, professional, and personal lives.

name: Women Lead Right at Georgia Tech
description: Women Lead Right at Georgia Tech aims to engage and amplify the civic and political impact of women on Georgia Tech’s campus; engage, educate, train, and lead Georgia Tech’s women in grassroots advocacy; and offer a safe environment to discuss the concerns affecting women today.
summary: The purpose of Women Lead Right at Georgia Tech is to engage and amplify the civic and political impact of women on Georgia Tech’s campus.

name: Yellow Jacket Aviation
description:
We are building an aircraft that will be built and flown by GT Students! We don't just need engineers, please sign up if you would be interested in any of these teams!

 

Finance - research grants, get in touch with Alumni and secure funding for this project. You will also then need to manage the organisation's finances.

 

PR and Design - Build up the club's social media presence and design things such as the aircraft livery.

 

GT and Ramblin Wreck Liaison - we need people to stay in touch with GT to integrate the aircraft into GT culture.

 

FAA and TSA Liaison - we need a team to work on the legal aspects of this project and communicate with the FAA and TSA.

 

Logistics - an aircraft is big, we need to transport it, we need to store it, we need to store parts, do we need a hangar? These are things the Logistics team will need to manage.

 

Engineers - we will need a group of Engineers to research and build the actual aircraft. YOU DO NOT NEED TO BE AN AE MAJOR for this. But some practical skills and experience are preferred.
summary: Come help build the Ramblin' Wreck of the Sky!

name: Vibha at GT
description: To promote public education and support our community, both locally and internationally.
summary: To promote public education and support our community, both locally and internationally.

name: Shop With a Jacket
description: Our purpose is to enhance the accessibility of resources for underprivileged Atlanta elementary school students, enabling them to purchase holiday gifts for themselves and loved ones. We hope to do this by partnering with elementary schools throughout Atlanta-Fulton County to organize an annual SWAJ Event Day. On Event Day, GT students and participating children will spend a day together shopping, wrapping gifts, and enjoying fun holiday activities. With money raised by GT students and the Atlanta community, each child can spend a designated amount of money on items that they need and want. Our hope is for Event Day to be a positive, annual event where people from across the community can come together to celebrate and learn from one another! This is also an effort to increase GT's impact on K-12 schools in the area.
summary: SWAJ aims to enhance the accessibility of resources for underprivileged Atlanta elementary school students, enabling them to purchase holiday gifts for themselves and loved ones. On SWAJ event day, GT students will go shopping and do holiday activities.

name: RoboRambler
description:
Students can engage in a new type of robotics combat game (First-Person Shooting robotics), learn full-stack development ranging from computer vision to circuit design and to machine design, and gain experience in extensive 3D printing research and practices.

Please fill out the interest form if you would lilke to join us: 
https://forms.office.com/Pages/DesignPageV2.aspx?subpage=design&FormId=u5ghSHuuJUuLem1_Mvqgg8Gi4N-NYNFCt-1p-VhoyuZUNzZCMERCUUJPMjcxWTc3UVpEQlhSUDQ2Wi4u&Token=f7eea2a228724f329fa17716f70e37b1
summary: RoboRambler is the RoboMaster team at Georgia Tech, competing in the RoboMaster North America competition. Join us to experience an amazing combination of robotics and first-person shooting games!

name: Hooks & Needles Fiber Arts Club @ GT
description:
To provide a space for students who enjoy knitting and/or crocheting to connect with others who share that passion and to teach students looking to learn fiber arts techniques.

Join us on discord: https://discord.gg/BHuFNTvMbJ
summary: To provide a space for students who enjoy knitting and/or crocheting to connect with others who share that passion and to teach students looking to learn fiber arts techniques.

name: Craft Community
description: The goal of the organization is to create a space for crafters to build community and improve on skills. We want to have weekly/bi-weekly meetings for crafters to work on their individual projects in a shared space. We also want to host monthly workshops to build/strengthen skills. This club is open to all crafters or individuals who are interested in crafting. We also want to host supply swaps, a showcase at the end of each semester, and potentially integrate a community service perspective. The best way to stay in touch with us is joining our GroupMe.
summary: The goal of the organization is to create a space for crafters to build community and improve on skills. We want to have weekly/bi-weekly meetings for crafters to work on their individual projects in a shared space.

name: Herpetology @ GaTech
description:
Teach individuals about reptile and amphibians while at the same time fostering a community for herpers and those interested in the natural sciences. 


Join the discord here! (We don't use engage much)

https://discord.gg/GA5yPMc6nV
summary: Herpetology @ GaTech teaches individuals about reptile and amphibians while at the same time fostering a community for herpers and those interested in the natural sciences.

name: Ivan Allen College of Liberal Arts
description: Georgia Tech’s Ivan Allen College provides innovative, human-centered perspectives at the intersection of humanities, social sciences, arts, and STEM. We develop leaders prepared to advance technology and improve the human condition. Each semester, the College hosts a variety of events, socials, workshops, and more for our students to network and form community outside of the classroom.
summary: Georgia Tech’s Ivan Allen College provides innovative, human-centered perspectives at the intersection of humanities, social sciences, arts, and STEM. We develop leaders prepared to advance technology and improve the human condition.

name: Women in Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The purpose of WISyE is to advance and support women’s experience, learning, and networking in Industrial and Systems Engineering at Georgia Tech.
summary: The purpose of WISyE is to advance and support women’s experience, learning, and networking in Industrial and Systems Engineering at Georgia Tech.

name: Go Club @ GT
description:
Welcome to Go Club @ GT! Our goal is to...

◐ Introduce the game of Go (additionally known by the names Wéiqí, Baduk) to interested members of the Georgia Tech community.

◐ Create a community for players of Go to meet, learn, compete, and connect through weekly meetings, teaching sessions, competitions, outreach events, and more.

◐ Connect to the greater Atlanta community through partnership with groups like the Atlanta Go Club.
summary: Go Club @ GT is passionate about introducing the game of Go (additionally known by the names Wéiqí, Baduk) to interested members of the Georgia Tech community!

name: Billiard Club at GT
description:
If interested in joining the club, just come to the GT Tech Rec at 6 PM on Thursdays. We meet at that time every week for an hour or two and play pool, so if you come, you will be able to join the club. You can also join by requesting on this engage page. Login to your GT account, then simply request to join. 

 

Join our slack for more regular communications about weekly meetings, events, and more.

Join Slack
summary:
The purpose of Billiard Club at GT is to enjoy playing pool and all related games.

name: The Nourish Project @ GT
description: We are an organization that seeks to promote longevity, safe aging, and social connectedness among the elderly. We will go to senior living centers in the local area and give seminars on nutrition, exercise, technology skills, and social connection!
summary: We are an organization that seeks to promote longevity, safe aging, and social connectedness among the elderly.

name: Student Organization for Latinos in Computing
description: Creating community, advisory, and support for latino students in computing. Some of our goals include: creating a space for collaboration, sharing, and discussion; fostering career opportunities for our members; and providing mentorship for incoming latino students.
summary: Create community, opportunities, and support for latino students in computing.

name: The Daniel Guggenheim School of Aerospace Engineering Student Ambassadors
description: The purpose of this organization will be to support the School of Aerospace engineering in programming efforts for current students and in recruitment efforts for prospective GT students. This will be achieved by giving facility tours and participating in visit days and events.
summary: The purpose of this organization will be to support the School of Aerospace engineering in programming efforts for current students and in recruitment efforts for prospective GT students.

name: Partners in Health Engage at Georgia Tech
description: Partners In Health strives to provide equitable healthcare access to marginalized communities worldwide, addressing not only immediate medical needs but also underlying social determinants of health. Through partnerships and advocacy, they work towards a world where quality healthcare is a fundamental human right, irrespective of one's economic status or geographic location. The purpose of the chapter at GT would follow similar ideals in which we would raise awareness and contribute to the campaign of the PIH.
summary: Partners In Health strives to provide equitable healthcare access to marginalized communities worldwide, addressing not only immediate medical needs but also underlying social determinants of health.

name: Doctors Without Borders Student Chapter
description:
We seek to raise awareness and knowledge about Doctors Without Borders work in the field and about medical humanitarian issues. As well as to create a community of pre-health students through planning social, volunteering, informational, and fundraising events. 

More info and member join form on our linktree! https://linktr.ee/GT.FriendsofMSF
summary: To raise awareness and knowledge about Doctors Without Borders work in the field and about medical humanitarian issues. As well as to create a community of pre-health students through planning social, volunteering, informational, and fundraising events.

name: M28 Church
description: M28 Church at Georgia Tech exists to create an avenue for discipleship through the local church and to allow M28's college ministry to better serve Georgia Tech's campus.
summary: M28 Church at Georgia Tech exists to create an avenue for discipleship through the local church and to allow M28's college ministry to better serve Georgia Tech's campus.

name: Jackets for Justice
description: Jackets for Justice is aimed towards students with an interest in the law, especially those contemplating law school. Envisioned as a society, we plan to organize talks with legal professionals, including lawyers and judges, and arrange visits to courthouses, corporate offices, and law schools. Additionally, we would like to offer workshops led by seasoned professionals on topics such as law school applications, the LSAT, essays, and extracurricular activities. Our core mission is to foster a community of like-minded individuals!
summary: Jackets for Justice is aimed towards students with an interest in the law, especially those contemplating law school.

name: Women’s Recruitment Team at Georgia Tech
description: Assist the Office of Undergraduate Admission in recruitment initiatives to increase women enrollment at Georgia Tech and create a strong and diverse community of like-minded women within the team itself.
summary: Assist the Office of Undergraduate Admission in recruitment initiatives to raise the ratio of women at Georgia Tech and create a strong and diverse community of like-minded women within the team itself.

name: Photography @ GT
description: Offer students an inclusive environment catering to all levels of photography enthusiasts. We provide a platform for members to express their artistic visions through photography and offer a multifaceted approach to exploring the world of photography.
summary: We offer students an inclusive environment catering to all levels of photography enthusiasts. Provide a platform for members to express their artistic visions through photography.

name: Black in Biomedical Engineering at Georgia Tech
description: Aims to foster a supportive community by providing networking opportunities, academic support, and career development resources. The club advocates for diversity and inclusion in the field, addressing challenges and celebrating the achievements of Black individuals in biomedical engineering. Through cultural events, educational outreach, and collaboration with other organizations, the club strives to create a more inclusive and empowered environment for Black students pursuing careers in the biomedical field.
summary: BiBME aims to foster a supportive community by providing networking opportunities, academic support, and career development resources.

name: The Black Student Computing Organization
description: The Black Student Computing Organization, affectionately known as BSCO, is a community for students of African-Descent, pursuing Computer Science, Computational Media, Computer Engineering, and additional computing concentrations. Rooted in the pillars of Academic Support, Corporate Enrichment, and Community Security. The students of BSCO aim to equip, embolden, and uphold each other as they journey through the computing experience at Georgia Tech.
summary: The Black Student Computing Organization, affectionately known as BSCO, is a community for students of African-Descent, pursuing Computer Science, Computational Media, Computer Engineering, and additional computing concentrations.

name: Girls Into VC at Georgia Tech
description: Girls Into VC is the first organization dedicated to bringing girls into venture capital. We’re committed to bridging the gender gap by creating a robust pipeline of skilled, passionate young women equipped and ready to revolutionize the industry. Through targeted education, resources, and mentorship, we empower the next generation of women leaders in venture capital. Inform. Innovate. Invest.
summary: Empowering the next generation of female Yellow Jackets to become venture capitalists & championing women-led startups.

name: Flash Mob @ GT
description:
Flash Mob @ GT brings spontaneity and happiness to Georgia Tech's campus through brief, intermittent choreographed dance routines with people of all skill levels.

We use Slack, so please email elind7@gatech.edu to get added to the slack!
summary: Flash Mob @ GT brings spontaneity and happiness to Georgia Tech's campus through brief, intermittent choreographed dance routines with people of all skill levels.

name: Black Students in Psychology at GT
description: To increase the number of culturally responsible black psychologists who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.
summary: To increase the number of culturally responsible black psychologists who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.

name: Thai Student Association at Georgia Tech
description:
The purpose of ThaiSA is to:

  1. Provide support, community, and friendship to Thai and Thai American students adapting to the Georgia Tech environment.
  2. Assist in the cultural transition of Thai and Thai American students to Western culture,
  3. Create a robust network of Thai communities of students
  4. Cultivate a sense of belonging and community among Thai and Thai American students at Georgia Tech.
  5. Foster connections and collaboration among Thai and Thai American descent enrolled at Georgia Tech.
  6. Promote Thai culture both within the Georgia Tech community and beyond, raising awareness about its richness and diversity.
  7. Engage with the Thai community in Thailand to facilitateand foster cultural exchange between the two communities.
  8. Become a beacon for the Thai community in Atlanta.
summary: Thai SA works to cultivate a sense of belonging and community among Thai and Thai American students at Georgia Tech.

name: Undergraduate Business Analytics Club
description:
UBAC’s purpose is to provide business analytics education and networking opportunities for undergraduate students across Georgia Tech.
