name: AI@GT
description:
Artificial Intelligence at Georgia Tech is Georgia Tech's premier student organization focused on artificial intelligence (AI). Our mission is to promote awareness, understanding, and engagement with AI among students at Georgia Tech through events, research projects, hackathons, and more.

Our Linktree: https://linktr.ee/aigatech
summary: Artificial Intelligence at Georgia Tech is Georgia Tech's premier student organization focused on artificial intelligence (AI).

name: Growing Ramos Association Cultivating Inclusion and Academic Support at Georgia Tech
description: GRACIAS is a nonprofit organization devoted to providing resources and mentorship to individuals of the Latino/a/x and Hispanic communities in Metro Atlanta, empowering them to pursue higher education. We provide both in-person and virtual resources to all individuals interested and spend a large amount of our time doing outreach events. We are determined to do what we can to help these communities cross the many barriers that exist in pursuing higher education.
summary: GRACIAS is a nonprofit organization devoted to providing resources and mentorship to individuals of the Latino/a/x and Hispanic communities in Metro Atlanta, empowering them to pursue higher education.

name: College of Computing Association for Graduate Women and Gender Diversity
description:
Getting Involved: Join this organization on GT Engage to hear about our events, and fill out this interest form to be added to our Slack and mailing list!

Mission: The purpose of this Association includes representing the graduate students of the College of Computing who identify as women and other gender minorities, and their allies, in all matters concerning academics, welfare, social activities, and other matters specific to graduate women and gender minorities. The Association will be working with the College of Computing (CoC) in promoting greater recognition of issues specific to graduate women and gender minorities; promoting a sense of community; and stimulating interest in and appreciation of graduate women and gender minorities both on and off campus.
summary: The purpose of this Association includes representing the graduate students of the College of Computing who identify as women and other gender minorities, and their allies, in all matters.

name: Hispanic Recruitment Team
description: HRT provides current Georgia Tech students the ability to assist with Hispanic recruitment efforts. Promotes retention for incoming admitted Hispanic students and provides peer-guidance for prospective Hispanic students and families.
summary: HRT provides current Georgia Tech students the opportunity to assist with recruiting hispanic students to come to tech, while also creating a central location for the bonding of hispanic students at Tech.

name: Atlanta Christian Collegiate Fellowship
description: The ACCF focuses on campus gospel, spiritual Growth, community Service, cross-campus fellowship, and intra-state and cross-state Fellowship.
summary: ACCF focuses on: Campus Gospel, Spiritual Growth, Community Service, Cross-campus Fellowship, Intra-state and cross-state Fellowship

name: Sending Smiles
description: Sending Smiles (www.sendingsmiles.love) connects people undergoing cancer treatments to their communities through heartfelt, handwritten letters of support. We aim to remind these strong individuals that their community stands behind them, and we believe in the power of a handwritten letter, and the smile it can bring to one's face.
summary: Sending Smiles (www.sendingsmiles.love) connects people undergoing cancer treatments to their communities through heartfelt, handwritten letters of support.

name: The Black Student Recruitment Team
description: The purpose of the Black Student Recruitment Team is to provide current Georgia Tech students information to assist with recruitment efforts, promote retention for incoming admitted students, provide peer-guidance for prospective students and families, provide information to the Office of Undergraduate Admissions.
summary: The purpose of the Black Student Recruitment Team is to provide current Georgia Tech students information to assist with recruitment efforts.

name: Buzz Committee
description: The Buzz Committee promotes Georgia Tech spirit by providing a creative space for Buzz's biggest fans to support Buzz. Students will utilize a broad range of skills to help with skit ideation, prop construction, media creation, and much more. The committee communicates with Buzz to have a real influence on Buzz's interactions across campus. The group unites the countless Georgia Tech students who have fallen in love with Buzz's intoxicating spirit and enjoy having an impact on how Buzz interacts with his community.
summary: The Buzz Committee promotes Georgia Tech spirit by providing a creative space for Buzz's biggest fans to support Buzz through skit ideation, prop construction, media creation, and much more!

name: IT Support
summary: IT Support portal

name: First-Year Texperience
description:
The First-Year Texperience (FYT) is a First-Year Leadership Organization (FLO) at Georgia Tech built around one core value: connection. We believe your first year should be about more than classes—it’s about finding your people, learning the traditions of campus, and discovering Atlanta, the city we now call home.

 

Each week, FYT creates space for first-year students to unwind, relax, and have fun together. Our programming is designed around your interests, whether that means exploring local gems like the Fox Theatre, getting creative with cake decorating and pottery painting, solving escape rooms, or hosting on-campus events to bring the community together.

 

From small moments of laughter to big memories with your cohort, FYT is here to make your first year at Georgia Tech unforgettable.
summary: First-Year Texperience

name: Residence Life / Housing
description:
Our department is a large and complex organization supporting over 8,700 on-campus residents among 49 traditional halls, suites and apartments, some of which were constructed for the 1996 Summer Olympics. Our team of 200+ professional staff and 300+ student staff from six departments support residents directly and indirectly with their unique talents. We are housed within the Student Engagement and Well-Being division that includes many student service offices.

Mission

Housing and Residence Life offers quality, safe, well-maintained residential facilities. We encourage, promote, and facilitate customer focused programs and services that attract students to our facilities and to Georgia Tech. We provide physical, programmatic, and technological systems to enhance the quality of life on campus, to support the academic needs of resident students and to complement the academic goals of the Institute.
summary: Housing and Residence Life encompasses all our residence halls, their staffs, and more than 270 student employees. You can find events here for the First Year Wellness Experience and lots more too!

organizationType.name: Division of Student Engagement and Well-being

name: Arts, Belonging and Community
description: The units comprising the new division of Arts, Belonging, and Community (ABC) share a mission of actively engaging students through a wide array of strategies – arts, activities, intercultural dialogue, social events, student organizations and others. The unifying goal is to promote and sustain a sense of belonging and community for all students across all identities, in particular those students who come from historically marginalized or underserved communities.
summary: Arts, Belonging, and Community

organizationType.name: Division of Student Engagement and Well-being

name: GT Pre-Health Advising
description:
As part of the Office of Undergraduate Education (OUE) and Academic Success and Advising (ASA) unit, Pre-Health advising supports all undergraduate, graduate, and alumni pursuing careers in the health professions. GT Pre-Health advising collaborates with student organizations and departments that have a pre-health focus to support the diverse pre-health community at Georgia Tech.

Home website: https://prehealth.gatech.edu/
summary: GT Pre-Health Advising

name: National Society of Black Women in Medicine at Georgia Tech
description: The National Society of Black Women in Medicine is a multi-disciplined organization that works to increase the recruitment and retention of Black women pursuing broad careers in medicine, while simultaneously promoting the advancement of those already established in these fields.
summary: The National Society of Black Women in Medicine is a multi-disciplined organization that works to increase the recruitment and retention of Black women pursuing broad careers in medicine.

name: Biology Student Advisory Committee
description:
The purpose of BSAC is: 

  1. Peer Advising and Mentoring – to promote a general education about the Biology degree and related opportunities at Georgia Tech to current, incoming, and prospective students, including but not limited to: Biology Peer Advisors, FASET Advisors, Biology Mentors and Peers (BioMAPs) 
  1. Networking and Professional Development– to host events and guest speakers that enable a forum of exchange among members of the Biology community, and to support undergraduate Biology students in their career goals through events such as BioDiscover and the Spring Social. 
  1. Prospective Student Outreach and Recruitment – to support high school students who are interested in the Biology major at Georgia Tech, including Biology Student Ambassadors 
  1. Advisory Feedback to the School – to provide insights to school leadership such as feedback on instructional faculty candidates and programmatic concerns to students
summary: The Biology Student Advisory Committee provides peer advising, mentoring, networking, and student feedback to promote a general education about the Biology degree and related opportunities at Georgia Tech.

name: Non-Traditional Student Organization
description:
The purpose of Non-Traditional Student Organization is to:

a. To create a welcoming and inclusive space where non-traditional students can connect, share their unique experiences, and feel a sense of belonging within the larger university community.

b. To promote academic success and personal development by providing resources, peer support, and opportunities for collaboration, helping members navigate their unique educational journeys.

c. To offer networking opportunities, social events, and professional development activities tailored to the needs of non-traditional students, enabling them to build meaningful connections and thrive both on and off campus.
summary: The purpose of Non-Traditional Student Organization is to create a welcoming and inclusive space where non-traditional students can connect, share their unique experiences, and feel a sense of belonging within the larger university community.

name: Project Management at Georgia Tech
description:
Our purpose is to provide a platform for students to develop project management skills through workshops, certifications, and real-world projects. We aim to foster collaborations, leadership, networking and communication skills. This will as a result prepare members for successful project management roles across various industries.

Spring 2026 Meetings will happen every other Tuesday, 6:00 PM - 7:00 PM 

380 Classroom Bunger Henry

Meeting Dates:

Feb 3, 10, 24

March 10, 24

April 7, 21
summary: Our purpose is to provide a platform for students to develop project management skills through workshops, certifications, and real-world projects.

name: Parents and Family Programs
summary: Parents and Family Programs

name: Brawl Stars @ GT
description: Brawl Stars @ GT provides it's members a space to play Brawl Stars together with other GT students! Here, you can meet new people, make new friends, and play Brawl Stars together! We also help members complete in-game events, provide opportunities to compete in intra-club competitions, compete in ranked play, in national tournaments, and against other universities. Players of all skill levels are welcome to join - we can't wait to Brawl together! Join the #1 Brawl Stars community on campus!
summary: The #1 Brawl Stars Community at Georgia Tech University! The best place on campus to play Brawl Stars, compete in a variety of events (even against other universities) and meet new people - come Brawl with us!

socialMedia.ExternalWebsite: Join our official club Discord server! https://discord.gg/dhWFCM5UXT

name: Friends of Hospice Care @ GT
description: The purpose of this organization is to provide companionship to individuals in the elderly community who are in hospice care facilities. We plan to reach out to several different hospice care organizations in hopes of collaborating with them and providing volunteering opportunities for the students who join this club. Opportunities will include bereavement care for families who've lost a loved one, 1 on 1 companionship with a patient in a hospice care facility, as well as volunteering in events for hospice care facilities to foster engagement within the community (such as reading hours for individuals with dementia!), to let them know they're not alone.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide companionship to individuals in the elderly community who are in hospice care facilities.

name: iOS Club @ GT
description: iOS Club is an organization which aims to provide a community for passionate iOS developers developing apps together. We provide flexible ways for students to build their iOS development skills, whether they’re just getting started or already have apps published on the App Store. Our diverse alumni network also serves as an opportunity for students to meet top engineers in the industry.
summary: iOS Club is an organization which aims to provide a community for passionate iOS developers developing apps together.

name: Every Nation Campus
description: Every Nation Campus exists to honor God by helping students grow in their love for God, their love for people, and their confidence in sharing their faith.
summary: Every Nation Campus exists to honor God by helping students grow in their love for God, their love for people, and their confidence in sharing their faith.

name: Boricuas ORganized for Impact
description: BORI at Georgia Tech is dedicated to fostering a close-knit, supportive community for Puerto Rican students, promoting their cultural identity, and enhancing their personal and professional development. We strive to celebrate Puerto Rican heritage through events, raise awareness across campus and Atlanta, and connect with the Puerto Rican diaspora. Our mission extends beyond Georgia Tech as we build relationships with organizations in Puerto Rico, engaging in fundraising, volunteer efforts, and outreach. Additionally, we inspire students in Puerto Rico to pursue academic excellence and explore opportunities at Georgia Tech.
summary: BORI at Georgia Tech is dedicated to fostering a close-knit, supportive community for Puerto Rican students, promoting their cultural identity, and enhancing their personal and professional development.

name: Black Queer Experience at Georgia Tech
description:
The purpose of Blaque is to:

    Foster a community and a support system for Black Queer students at Georgia Tech

    Provide educational & career resources to Georgia Tech's Black Queer community

    Advocate & outreach to the broader Georgia Tech community around Black Queer issues
summary: The purpose of Blaque is to foster a community and a support system for Black Queer students at Georgia Tech.

name: Ukrainian Club
description: This club is for Ukrainian students of Georgia Tech, so that they can connect with their origin while being far away from home, speak in their native language, talk about Ukrainian culture, etc. As for non-Ukrainian students it is a perfect opportunity to get to know Ukrainian culture and history more, learn basic Ukrainian, make more friends and have fun time.
summary:
For all Ukrainians of Georgia Tech: Разом нас багато, нас не подолати!
For all non-Ukrainians of Georgia Tech: We would love for you to join us!

name: The Oasis
description: The Oasis at Georgia Tech strives to make the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ available to our community to bring them into their inheritance. It teaches the tenets of the gospel, enhance moral training through the teaching of the Bible, satisfy the spiritual thirst in our community, as well as provide solace for the troubled, practice our religious convictions, and give dignity to the human race. A Vision to take the divine presence of God to the peoples and the nations of the world demonstrating the character of His Spirit with a Mission to raise a generation of men and women who will come into their inheritance to fulfill God's dream.
summary: The Oasis at Georgia Tech strives to make the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ available to our community to bring them into their inheritance.

name: Experiential and Engaged Learning
description:
The Office of Experiential and Engaged Learning (E2L) is the consolidation of Georgia Tech’s signature experiential, or “learning by doing”, programming within the Office of Undergraduate Education and Student Success (OUESS).. E2L leads the way in designing and delivering high-impact experiential learning that transforms the lives of our students. E2L also supports campus-wide first-year and transfer-student seminars designed around the distinctive Georgia Tech educational experience and serves as a central hub supporting learning communities that have grown up over the past two decades at Georgia Tech.

Units within E2L include:

Undergraduate Research

Student Innovation Program

Undergraduate Transition Seminars (GT 1000 & GT 2000)

Co-ops and Internships

Ignite Summer Launch Program

Community-Based Learning
summary: The Office of Experiential and Engaged Learning (E2L) leads the way in designing and delivering high-impact experiential learning that transforms the lives of Georgia Tech students.

name: Georgia Tech Police Department
description: GTPD's mission is to ensure the safety and well-being of the Georgia Tech community through educational programming, safety initiatives, and proactive law enforcement. We are committed to fostering trust, enhancing community partnerships, and delivering effective crime prevention and response strategies that promote a secure and informed environment for all.
summary: Georgia Tech Police Department

name: Academic Success and Advising
description: The Office of Undergraduate Education’s (OUE) Academic Success and Advising (ASA) unit creates and implements a comprehensive vision around academic success at Georgia Tech. ASA brings together OUE’s programs and student services that focus on advising, tutoring, pre-graduate/pre-health advising, and first-generation and limited income student resources. ASA also provides leadership for a wide range of targeted initiatives and special projects that impact retention, persistence, and timely degree completion.
summary: Academic Success and Advising (ASA) creates and implements a comprehensive vision around academic success at Georgia Tech.

name: Career Center
description:
The Georgia Tech Career Center is a unit of the Office of Undergraduate Education (OUE) that provides quality career education for every student. Our model encompasses three crucial components:

1) Career Preparation includes career exploration, programming, advising, and securing a career path.

2) Career Experience manifests primarily as co-op, internships, and related experiential education.

3) Career Connections are facilitated through career fairs, interviews, networking events, and related programs.

Our team partners across campus, and around the globe, with students, employers, faculty, and alumni to support students’ career aspirations.
summary: The Georgia Tech Career Center provides quality career education for every student.

name: AI Safety Initiative at Georgia Tech
description:
Experts broadly expect significant progress in AI during our lifetimes, potentially to the point of surpassing human-level intelligence. Worryingly, technological advancement for AI systems has outpaced our ability to explain their behavior, control their objective, and to build robust safeguards against failures. As such, managing risks from advanced artificial intelligence is one of the most important problems of our time. We are a community of technical and policy researchers at Georgia Tech aimed at reducing these risks and steering the trajectory of AI development for the better.  Our composition is roughly 50% undergrads, 45% grads/PhDs, and 5% working professionals.

We provide various opportunities to upskill and contribute to different problems in the field.

  • AI safety fellowship: An accelerated introductory course to get up to speed on core motivations and literature in AI Safety.
  • Rapid Upskilling Cohorts: Accountability groups to gain the technical skills needed for AI Safety research - course uses PyTorch
  • Research capstone projects: For motivated students who complete the AI safety fellowship, this is a chance to publish AI safety research with other fellowship alumni, mentorship, and funding support.  See our list of publications here. 

We are also constantly delivering impactful projects within the Georgia Tech community and beyond. Some of our efforts include:

  • Presented a demonstration on red-teaming language models at a congressional exhibit hosted by the Center for AI Policy in Washington DC.
  • Helping Liberia draft regulations to Prevent AI-enabled Catastrophic Biorisk Events.
  • Organized the first AI Safety Forum where we hosted over 60 students, researchers, industry professionals, and members of the public.
  • Contributed to the National AI Action Plan RFI. This government effort eventually matured into “America’s AI Action Plan” which incorporated a few points we made.

Visit https://www.aisi.dev/ for more information.  Our main communications platform for internal and external opportunities, all future events, programs, and research is our discord server.  Also click here sign up for our mailing list to stay in the loop with our organization and ongoing endeavors!

We do not manage members on engage because we want to invest in our most promising members by providing exclusive opportunities and resources.  This page is sparsely monitored.
summary: AISI is an academic community working to ensure AI is developed to the benefit of humanity's future.

name: Medical Mentors Network
description: A mentor-mentee networking organization intended for pre-medicine students (pre-MD, pre-PA, pre-dental, etc.). The organization intends to connect its members with medical opportunities through networking with members of their anticipated profession with the goal of providing opportunities such as volunteering, clinical, and shadowing through making connections with other members.
summary: A mentor-mentee networking organization intended for pre-medicine students (pre-MD, pre-PA, pre-dental, etc.).

name: Grey Matters
description: The primary purpose is to publish a high-quality neuroscience journal that is entirely produced by undergraduate students, encompassing writing, editing, illustration, and design. In the near future, we plan to organize annual neuroscience-related events to connect community members, as well as host educational activities at local high schools.
summary: The primary purpose is to publish a high-quality neuroscience journal that is entirely produced by undergraduate students, encompassing writing, editing, illustration, and design.

name: Structural Nucleic Acid Anticancer Research Society Atlanta Branch at Georgia Tech
description: Engage and empower students in crystal-growing, crystallography, and structural biology research. Provide students with the opportunity to learn about wet-lab and dry-lab research as they relate to therapeutic drug design.
summary: We engage and empower students in crystal-growing, crystallography, and structural biology research. Provide students with the opportunity to learn about and execute wet-lab and dry-lab research as they relate to therapeutic drug design.

name: SynapseX
description: SynapseX aims to advance the development of brain-computer interface(BCI) technologies by aiming to create a functional BCI prototype that translates neural signals into binary code. Through interdisciplinary collaboration across neuroscience, engineering, and computing, the organization maximizes innovation in all fields of BCI like neural signal processing, device engineering, and software development. SynapseX also prioritizes mentorship and learning, inviting students from all backgrounds to contribute to the development of cutting-edge BCI technology.
summary: SynapseX aims to advance the development of brain-computer interface(BCI) technologies by aiming to create a functional BCI prototype that translates neural signals into binary code.

name: Day Trading Club at GT
description:
The purpose of this organization is to educate members on basic technical analysis, market performance, and microtrends information/techniques to equip students with the necessary skills to succeed in second, minute, or hour trades.

We specifically trade nasdaq futures, but different assets/trading styles are welcome!

Disclaimer: The Day Trading Club at Georgia Tech is an educational organization that aims to provide resources and foster discussions about financial markets. The content, activities, and advice provided by this organization and its members are for informational purposes only and should not be construed as professional financial advice. Members are encouraged to consult with a licensed financial advisor before making any investment decisions. The club, its officers, and its members are not liable for any financial losses incurred by participants.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to educate members on basic technical analysis, market performance, and microtrends information/techniques to equip students with the necessary skills to succeed in second, minute, or hour trades.

name: Chorale at Georgia Tech
description:
Chorale is a non-auditioned singing organization that welcomes all voices and experiences. The ensemble explores a variety of music that expands knowledge of musical genres, history, culture, and vocal technique. While previous choir experience is helpful, it is not a prerequisite. Through rehearsal and performance, students will gain a deeper understanding of how music reflects and shapes the human experience.

 

The purpose of Chorale is to:

- Develop and understand the necessary skills utilized in the performance of choral literature
- Encourage vocal technique through structured aural and harmonic theory practices
- Experience a variety of repertoire through refined rehearsal, sectionals and performance
- Display important membership qualities that consistently uplift and promote unity within the organization
- Strive for the highest standards of ensemble singing

 

We accomplish this by:
- Studying, rehearsing, and performing music both on and off campus
- Developing the musical abilities of our members
- Aiding in presenting to the public a complimentary image of the Institute

 

You can find more information about us on our website, as well as our Instagram.
summary: Chorale is a non-auditioned singing organization that welcomes all voices and experiences. The ensemble explores a variety of music that expands knowledge of musical genres, history, culture, and vocal technique.

name: Jackets Against Poverty
description: We aim to alleviate poverty around ATL by facilitating partnerships with local non profits to organize volunteer opportunities, fundraisers, and food drives at GT. Our volunteer opportunities are focused on outreach into the community, specifically on-site visits to local homeless encampments under the supervision of an experienced social worker at Intown Cares Atlanta to hand out supplies, clean up trash, build shelters, and more. Another aspect of outreach is afterschool volunteering with under resourced children with Restoration Atlanta. We also provide off-site opportunities such as fundraising to build and sustain a shower bus that will be taken to local encampments to improve hygiene and slow spread of disease. Students will be directly involved in the construction, maintenance, and fundraising for this bus. Finally, our partnership with Intown Cares also extends into supporting their food pantry by organizing food drives around Tech!
summary: We aim to alleviate poverty around ATL by facilitating partnerships with local non profits to organize volunteer opportunities, fundraisers, and food drives at GT.

name: Robotics Outreach Club at Georgia Tech
description: The Robotics Outreach Club at Georgia Tech is an organization that encourages and facilitates long-term volunteering with and mentoring local robotics teams, such as those that participate in the First Robotics Tech Challenge (FTC) and other similar competitions. FTC is an annual competitive robotics league in which middle and high school teams must design, build, and program a robot for a yearly challenge. This process involves intensive engineering and computer science knowledge and applications. This club aims to pair local teams with GT mentors and facilitate volunteering at nearby competitions.
summary: The Robotics Outreach Club at Georgia Tech is an organization that encourages and facilitates long-term volunteering with and mentoring local robotics teams.

name: Anesthesiologist Assistant Association @ GT
description:
The purpose of the AAA is to:

1. Provide a community for students sharing the same career aspirations: to become an anesthesiologist assistant.

 

2. Create networking opportunities for students to connect with current AA students, AA school admissions officers, and current and retired anesthesiologist assistants.

 

3. Educate members of the process to becoming an AA, including schooling and daily responsibilities within the profession.

 

4. Provide volunteering, fundraising, and clinical opportunities so that students can learn from current professionals while obtaining volunteer hours and experience for AA school. 

 

5. Spread awareness to students on campus about the purpose and career of an anesthesiologist assistant.
summary: We aim to help bring awareness to the anesthesiologist assistant profession and help Georgia Tech students aspiring to become AAs build well rounded applications through assisting in getting shadowing hours, writing a personal statement, etc.

name: Women in Construction Chapter @ GT
description: The Women in Construction Chapter serves to support and empower women working or interested in the construction industry, providing a platform for networking, mentorship, and professional development. Its purpose includes promoting gender diversity, advocating for equal opportunities, and helping women overcome industry-specific challenges. Additionally, such chapters work to create awareness and visibility for women’s contributions in construction, foster a supportive community, and encourage younger generations—including students in construction, architecture, and engineering disciplines—to pursue careers in this field.
summary: The Women in Construction Chapter serves to support and empower women working or interested in the construction industry, providing a platform for networking, mentorship, and professional development.

name: Seven Generations
description: Seven Generations is dedicated to fostering a vibrant community that embraces Indigenous culture, traditions, and advocacy across campus. Our purpose is to create a space where students can learn from and engage with Indigenous knowledge and perspectives, particularly the philosophy of making decisions that consider the well-being and impact on the next seven generations. We aim to empower students to become thoughtful leaders, advocates, and allies, ensuring that the actions we take today contribute to a sustainable and inclusive future for all.
summary: Seven Generations is dedicated to fostering a vibrant community that embraces Indigenous culture, traditions, and advocacy across campus. Our purpose is to create a space where students can learn from and engage with Indigenous knowledge and perspectives

name: Hearts & Healing Fund
description: Organize and conduct fundraising events aimed at providing financial support for free medical camps and clinics in both local and global communities with limited access to healthcare. Facilitate the delivery of essential medical services through these fundraising efforts, including but not limited to blood testing, PAP smears, breast ultrasounds (women's health clinics), ECGs, and blood pressure measurements. Promote awareness and accessibility of healthcare services in underserved areas by partnering with medical professionals, organizations, and community leaders to address critical health needs and improve overall health outcomes.
summary: We aim to organize and conduct fundraising events aimed at providing financial support for free medical camps and clinics in both local and global communities with limited access to healthcare.

name: Pre-Veterinary Medical Association at Georgia Tech
description: We want to promote and stimulate interest in the field of veterinary medicine and provide its members with community service opportunities supporting animal welfare organizations. We want to provide its members with a forum for educational and informational guest speakers experienced in the field of veterinary medicine and animal health. We want to provide a network of academic and professional support to connect students interested in similar goals in the field of animal health and sciences.
summary: We want to promote and stimulate interest in the field of veterinary medicine and provide its members with community service opportunities supporting animal welfare organizations.

name: Black students in Material Science and Engineering
description: The purpose of this organization is to create a supportive and empowering community for Black students within the Material Science and Engineering (MSE) at Georgia Tech. Recognizing the unique challenges faced by students from underrepresented backgrounds, we aim to provide a space where students can connect with peers who share similar experiences, foster professional and academic development through networking opportunities, and engage in outreach initiatives to inspire and mentor prospective MSE students from diverse backgrounds. Through collaboration, resource-sharing, and mentorship, our organization seeks to build a stronger, more inclusive MSE community where Black students can thrive, grow, and contribute meaningfully to the field.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to create a supportive and empowering community for Black students within the school of Material Science and Engineering (MSE) at Georgia Tech.

name: Create for Kids at Georgia Tech
description: Welcome to Create For Kids, where we use the power of art to bring smiles to hospitalized children! We know that being in a hospital can be tough for kids and their families, so we provide various creative activities to make their stay a little brighter. We believe that art is vital to the healing process, and we strive to create a nurturing environment that promotes creativity and smiles. Our volunteers are passionate about their work and are dedicated to impacting children's lives by creating original art pieces and compiling hospital kits to be delivered to nearby pediatric wings.
summary: Create For Kids is a nonprofit organization focused on fostering creativity and inspiring children in hospital settings.

name: Swifties at GT
description: Bringing together Taylor Swift fans and the GT community as a whole through Taylor Swift themed events and meetings! Creating a community and giving students an opportunity to make new friends. All levels and types of Swifties are invited! Follow us on Instagram at @swiftiesatgt!
summary: We aim to bring together Taylor Swift fans and the GT community as a whole through Taylor Swift themed events and meetings.

name: Propulsive Landers @ GT
summary: Welcome to our page! We're Georgia Tech's 3rd rocketry team with the mission to become the first student organization in the world to achieve vertical take-off and landing of a hybrid rocket. Join us on our mission to create something extraordinary.

name: Audio Engineering Society @ GT
description:
AES @ GT aims to:

- Further the knowledge of interested students in the field of Audio Engineering-

- Hold workshops to further the Audio Engineering knowledge among Music Tech and non Music Tech students @ Georgia Tech

- Arrange attendance to the Audio Engineering Society conference every fall

 

Join our Discord for more info!
summary: ● Further the knowledge of interested students in the field of Audio Engineering ● Hold workshops to further the Audio Engineering knowledge among Music Tech and non Music Tech students @ Georgia Tech

name: Muslim Tech Collaborative at Georgia Tech
description: The purpose of this organization is to empower students on campus by embracing technology and engineering through professional development, education, and projects. For professional development, we facilitate industry connections between students and professionals. For education, we teach students about topics not previously covered in classroom which are useful to know going into the tech industry. For projects, we partner with local mosques and humanitarian non-profits to develop projects for them, free of charge, in hopes of doing students doing good work while gaining valuable skills.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to empower students on campus by embracing technology and engineering through professional development, education, and projects.

name: Supply Chain and Logistics Organization
description:
1. To foster the development of supply chain knowledge, networking opportunities, and professional growth for Georgia Tech students through a variety of resources to build a strong community of future supply chain professionals.

2. To engage with supply chain professionals to help students network within the industry, find career opportunities and holistically understand Supply Chain early on in their college career, bridging the gap between classroom learning and industry practices.

3. To connect with Georgia Tech Supply Chain and Logistics Institute to allow for better communication with students to pick their concentration and facilitate discussion and promotion of events around supply chain.

 

Our primary forms of programming include monthly general body meetings, affordable certifications, industry workshops, site visits, the Supply Chain Day career fair, and pro bono consulting opportunities.
summary: To foster the development of supply chain knowledge, networking opportunities, and professional growth for Georgia Tech students through a variety of resources to build a strong community of future supply chain professionals.

name: National Marrow Donor Program @ Georgia Tech
description: Engage students in the NMDP® mission to save lives by: 1. Providing meaningful, unique opportunities for students to learn about blood cancers and other life-threatening diseases treated by a blood stem cell transplant. 2. Increasing awareness of the need for young, committed, racially and ethnically diverse registry members who can save a life. 3. Holding campus-wide education/awareness events, donor registry and fundraising events and activities to give the entire student body the opportunity to join in this social movement and global effort. Be the official chapter partnering with the NMDP representative to educate and engage the university or college community in this unique opportunity to save lives.
summary: Engage students in the NMDP® mission to save lives by providing meaningful, unique opportunities for students to join the blood stem cell registry and learn about blood cancers and other life-threatening diseases treated by a blood stem cell transplant.

name: Project Yellow
description: Project Yellow aims to empower Georgia Tech students to provide mental health resources and education to children and elementary schoolers near Georgia Tech. We plan to do this by presenting engaging lesson plans at schools, fundraising, and sending donations to underprivileged children around Atlanta. Project Yellow also aims to foster a safe and welcoming community for students at Georgia Tech to learn about how to take care of their mental health and create meaningful connections with their peers.
summary: Project Yellow aims to provide mental health resources and education to children and elementary schoolers near Georgia Tech.

name: Karting Club at Georgia Tech
description: Our organization's purpose is to bring together racing fans of all kind and race go-karts against each other at local Atlanta motorsports parks. This could be at indoor, outdoor, or professional tracks near Georgia Tech campus. We will also share racing news in the community such as Formula 1 watch parties while hosting racing party events such as simulator competitions on F124 game.
summary: Our organization's purpose is to bring together racing fans of all kind and race go-karts against each other at local Atlanta motorsports parks.

name: Romanian Student Association
description: The Romanian Student Association (RSA) at Georgia Tech is a cultural/social organization that appreciates and shares Romanian culture. RSA is open to all Georgia Tech students and is a space where we value our culture and are excited to share it with the whole Georgia Tech student body. Our goal is to elevate the general understanding of Romanian culture, history, language, food, and education within the school and the local community.
summary: The Romanian Student Association (RSA) at Georgia Tech is a cultural/social organization that appreciates and shares Romanian culture.

name: The Leukemia and Lymphoma Society at GT
description: To provide a means for students at Georgia Tech to meet and fulfill the mission of our national affiliate, The Leukemia & Lymphoma Society (LLS) which is “to cure leukemia, lymphoma, Hodgkin’s disease and myeloma, and improve the quality of life of patients and their families.” We achieve this by facilitating events centered around fundraising, networking with healthcare professionals, educational workshops, and community outreach.
summary: We provide a means for students at Georgia Tech to meet and fulfill the mission of LLS. a. The official mission of our national affiliate, The Leukemia & Lymphoma Society (LLS) is “to cure leukemia, lymphoma, Hodgkin’s disease and myeloma

name: Phi Delta Epsilon Georgia Zeta Chapter
description:
Mission Statement: Phi Delta Epsilon Medical Fraternity (PhiDE) creates physicians of integrity with a life-long commitment to our guiding principles.

Vision Statement: PhiDE was founded to support the guiding principles of philanthropy, deity, and education. We accomplish this through fellowship, mentoring, leadership development, scientific education, teaching and practicing ethics in medicine, medical research, medical teaching, and nonprofit community service.

As the only international professional medical Fraternity, PhiDE brings these values and teachings to premedical students, medical students, and physicians around the world.
summary: Phi Delta Epsilon Medical Fraternity creates physicians of integrity with a life-long commitment to our guiding principles.

name: Ethiopian and Eritrean Graduate Student Association at Georgia Institute of Technology
description: The purpose of our organization is to connect and provide a support system to new and existing graduate students with Ethiopian and Eritrean ethnic backgrounds at Georgia Tech and others interested in the culture by organizing cultural, educational, and social events
summary: The purpose of our organization is to connect and provide a support system to new and existing graduate students with Ethiopian and Eritrean ethnic backgrounds at Georgia Tech.

name: Sarah Leah Rachel Rivkah
description: The purpose of this organization is to provide a fun and safe space for Jewish women on campus to plan events and facilitate Jewish discussions.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide a fun and safe space for Jewish women on campus to plan events and facilitate Jewish discussions.

name: Cake4Kids at GT
description: As an affiliate of the local Atlanta chapter of Cake4Kids we will serve as a means to push the mission of the national Cake4Kids mission: We bake and deliver free birthday cakes for underserved children who might not otherwise receive one. Our priority will be to fundraise for the organization and connect with local shelters and social services to provide our services to more kids across metro Atlanta.
summary: As an affiliate of the local Atlanta chapter of Cake4Kids we will serve as a means to push the mission of the national Cake4Kids mission.

name: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc. Georgia Institute of Technology Chapter
description: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc. is committed to uphold and enhance the interests and aspirations of Latino Unity for the achievement of social justice for all Latinos. We uphold our values of service, respect, unity, brotherhood, culture, and pride.
summary: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc. is committed to uphold and enhance the interests and aspirations of Latino Unity for the achievement of social justice for all Latinos.

name: Georgia Tech Library
description: The Georgia Tech Library defines excellence in the creation, preservation, curation, and connection of scholarship. Through this work we create new paths of learning, knowledge, information, and resources to develop leaders who advance technology and improve the human condition.
summary: Georgia Tech Library

name: Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Inc.
description: The Gamma Omega chapter of Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Inc. is dedicated to establishing a sisterhood based on unity, love, and respect through fostering social and cultural events, academic excellence, and community service. As the first and largest Latina sorority in the nation, we provide a supportive and inclusive environment that empowers members to grow as leaders and advocates for positive change. Through cultural awareness, philanthropic efforts, and sisterhood, we aim to uplift our communities and create a lasting impact both on campus and beyond.
summary: The Gamma Omega chapter of Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Inc. is dedicated to establishing a sisterhood based on unity, love, and respect through fostering social and cultural events, academic excellence, and community service.

name: Campus Services Sustainability
description: Discover how Campus Services is making sustainability a priority through engaging events and initiatives. From waste reduction workshops to energy-saving challenges, community clean-up days to garden workshops, our events are designed to educate, inspire, and empower students, staff, and faculty to take action. Join us in creating a more sustainable campus and making a positive impact!
summary: Campus Services Sustainability

name: HHC Housing
description: Sign up for HHC housing events and general body student meetings through this portal. This will help us accurately account for attendance, distribute food and supplies, and gather data on the most successful events. Can't wait to see you!
summary: HHC Housing

organizationType.name: Division of Student Engagement and Well-being

name: Sewa at GT
description: Sewa at GT is a student-driven, service-oriented organization dedicated to fostering a culture of selfless service while leveraging Georgia Tech’s innovative and problem-solving mindset to create meaningful impact. Rooted in the principles of Sewa (selfless service), our mission is to enhance the well-being of the Georgia Tech community, uplift the greater Atlanta area, and contribute to global humanitarian efforts. Our initiatives focus on improving campus life by addressing student wellness, sustainability, and social equity, ensuring that service is integrated into the fabric of everyday student life. Beyond campus, we strive to make a difference in the local community by partnering with nonprofits, shelters, and educational institutions to support underserved populations, promote environmental conservation, and provide essential resources. Our global outreach extends to responding to natural disasters, humanitarian crises, and development challenges, utilizing Georgia Tech’s technical expertise and innovative capabilities to create sustainable solutions. Through volunteer-driven initiatives, fundraising efforts, and technology-driven service projects, Sewa at GT empowers students to become compassionate leaders and changemakers. By bridging the gap between service and innovation, we aim to cultivate a community where selfless action drives transformation, both locally and globally.
summary: Sewa at GT is a student-driven, service-oriented organization dedicated to fostering a culture of selfless service while leveraging Georgia Tech’s innovative and problem-solving mindset to create meaningful impact.

name: American Sign Language Club at Georgia Tech
description: ASL Club aims to share resources for people to learn American Sign Language, forge connections to the Deaf community in the greater Atlanta area, and foster an environment more inclusive to deaf and hard of hearing people.
summary: ASL Club aims to share resources for people to learn American Sign Language, forge connections to the Deaf community in the greater Atlanta area, and foster an environment more inclusive to deaf and hard of hearing people.

name: Student Services Engagement
description: Student services engagement brings energy to campus life through fun and engaging events like Grad Fest celebrations, Coke-sponsored socials, and interactive Book Swaps. These events offer students a chance to connect with valuable campus resources while enjoying memorable experiences with their peers. Whether celebrating milestones, swapping stories over books, or sipping a refreshing Coke, there’s always something exciting happening to help students thrive and feel at home on campus.
summary: Student Services Engagement

name: Tech Dining
description: Georgia Tech Dining provides a diverse and convenient dining experience for students, faculty, and staff across campus. With a variety of dining halls, retail locations, and grab-and-go options, Tech Dining offers fresh, high-quality meals tailored to meet a range of dietary preferences, including vegetarian, vegan, and allergen-friendly choices. The program emphasizes sustainability, sourcing local ingredients whenever possible, and reducing food waste through initiatives like reusable containers and composting programs. In addition to everyday dining, Tech Dining features themed meals, special events, and flexible meal plans to accommodate the needs of the Georgia Tech community. Whether enjoying a meal at one of the campus dining halls, picking up a quick bite from a food truck, or ordering catering from popular retail locations, Tech Dining ensures a welcoming and satisfying experience for all.
summary: Tech Dining

name: Undergraduate Law Review at Georgia Tech
description:
Gather a group of Georgia Tech students that are interested in the legal world, law, writing, and editing.

Work through an editing and writing process to produce student written articles and publish them.

Hold events to recognize the achievements of the editors and members, as well as events to further the knowledge of all members, whether that be about law school, legal processes, legal writing, and the complexities of that environment.
summary: Undergraduate Law Review at Georgia Tech aims to gather a group of Georgia Tech students that are interested in the legal world, law, writing, and editing.

name: Technology Consulting Group @ GT
description: We want to connect students with early-stage startups and established tech companies to solve real-world challenges at the intersection of technology and business. Our team will provide strategic consulting, product insights, and technical solutions to help startups refine their vision, optimize their technology, and scale effectively. For Startups & Tech Companies – We will offer data-driven analysis, user research, and technical expertise to enhance product-market fit, streamline operations, and accelerate growth. For Students – We will provide hands-on experience working with real companies, developing skills in consulting, technology strategy, and venture-building while expanding their professional network. By bridging the gap between tech-savvy students and high-potential startups/established tech companies, we aim to foster innovation, drive impact, and shape the future of entrepreneurship.
summary: We want to connect students with early-stage startups and established tech companies to solve real-world challenges at the intersection of technology and business.

name: Black Girls Code
description: The purpose of Black Girls Code at Georgia Tech is to provide a safe and fruitful learning environment that offers the skills, support, and resources black girls need to excel in the Tech/STEM world. You can contact us @ blackgirlscodegt@gmail.com
summary: The purpose of Black Girls Code at Georgia Tech is to provide a safe and fruitful learning environment that offers the skills, support, and resources black girls need to excel in the Tech/STEM world.

name: Motorcycle Club at Georgia Tech
description: Our mission is to promote safe riding practices, foster community among motorcycle enthusiasts, and explore the joy of two-wheeled adventure. We aim to provide educational resources on motorcycle safety and maintenance, organize group rides and events, and create a welcoming environment for riders of all experience levels.
summary: Our mission is to promote safe riding practices, foster community among motorcycle enthusiasts, and explore the joy of two-wheeled adventure.

name: GT Brothers Connection
description: GT Brothers Connection (GTBC) promotes cultural and professional development, holistic wellness, networking, mentorship, leadership, and scholarly programs in support of African heritage male faculty and staff.
summary: GT Brothers Connection

name: Women in PreMed @ Georgia Tech
description: This organization aims to provide women, an underrepresented group in the medical field, opportunities for professional development, guidance on pursuing a career in medicine, and chances to give back to the community.
summary: This organization is meant to provide women, an underrepresented group in the medical field, opportunities for professional development, guidance on pursuing a career in medicine, and chances to give back to the community.

name: Teddy Bear Hospital
description:
Teddy Bear Hospital (TBH) aims to provide children with positive health care experiences, promote health and well-being, encourage higher education, and increase health literacy in the community. It also provides undergraduate students the opportunity to volunteer at local elementary schools to promote these missions during TBH sessions. At TBH sessions, children participate in highly interactive medical stations with their teddy bears as patients to learn about topics and career pathways in medicine in a comfortable environment. Undergraduate students gain public speaking skills and the skills necessary to work with children as they lead these TBH sessions successfully. 

To learn more about TBH:

Join our Discord: https://discord.gg/A2mebD3SEW

Follow us on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/teddybearhospitalgt/
summary: TBH is a non-profit organization that aims to reduce medical anxiety in children and encourage higher education while providing undergraduate students the opportunity to practice the skills necessary for successfully working with children.

name: Fighting Game Community at Georgia Tech
description: FGC@GT seeks to unite fighting game players and fans in an inclusive community which encourages each other to improve through a casual and competitive environment.
summary: FGC@GT seeks to unite fighting game players and fans in an inclusive community which encourages each other to improve through a casual and competitive environment.

name: Beach Volleyball Club at Georgia Tech
description: The purpose of the Beach Volleyball Club is to provide any student making the team the opportunity to play competitive beach volleyball for Georgia Tech against other colleges
summary: The Beach Volleyball Club at Georgia Tech is a coed team dedicated to training, teamwork, and high-level competition. We welcome committed players who want to participate in structured practices, and compete in tournaments against other colleges.

name: The Association of Women in Mathematics Graduate Chapter
description: We are a graduate chapter of the (national) Association for Women in Math. Our goal is to support female graduate students in their academic careers and to promote mathematics.
summary: We are a graduate chapter of the (national) Association for Women in Math. Our goal is to support female graduate students in their academic careers and to promote math in general.

name: Period Project @ GT
description: At The Period Project, we’re improving the lives of menstruators one period at a time. By increasing local, regional, and national awareness of period poverty and delivering menstrual health products to those in need, we’re replacing poverty with community and shame with dignity. We are part of a worldwide movement of similarly focused nonprofit organizations accelerating the momentum by incubating ideas and exchanging mutual aid for the common – and greater – good.
summary: At The Period Project, we’re improving the lives of menstruators one period at a time.

name: The HealthLink Initiative at Georgia Tech
description: The Health Link Initiative is dedicated to connecting uninsured/underinsured communities of Georgia with accessible healthcare services. Through the development of a free clinic database and in-person health education events, we plan on working to connect those that are in vulnerable communities of Atlanta with accessible and affordable healthcare services.
summary: The Health Link Initiative is dedicated to connecting uninsured, undocumented, and underserved communities of Georgia with accessible healthcare services.

name: Belonging and Student Support
description:
Reporting administratively to the Associate Vice President for Arts, Belonging & Community within the Student Engagement & Well-Being cabinet area, this department expands and enhances Georgia Tech’s ability to:

1. promote belonging for all Yellow Jackets,

2. facilitate constructive dialogue and promote intercultural competencies,

3. foster students’ holistic development, and

4. support their academic success.

 

Belonging & Student Support serves and collaborates with all students and student communities, while sustaining and expanding capacity to serve any student who benefits from co-curricular support to facilitate their pathway to degree. Staff routinely review student data on educational outcomes, health and wellness, belonging, and engagement to identify programmatic and service priorities that can make a positive difference in students’ experiences while at Georgia Tech.

 

We strongly affirm Georgia Tech’s commitment to ensuring students are our top priority, demonstrating that we thrive on diversity, safeguard freedom of inquiry and expression, and cultivate wellbeing for all students – no matter their background, identity or viewpoint. All Yellow Jacket students are welcome at our activities, events, programs, and services - each designed to meet students’ various learning styles, unique needs, and differing life experiences.
summary: Belonging and Student Support

name: Central Asian Student Association
description:
Central Asian Student Association (CASA) is the home for students from Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, and Turkmenistan at Georgia Tech. We are a community built on the legendary hospitality of the Silk Road, dedicated to helping students bridge the gap between their heritage and life in Atlanta.

Our Mission:

  • Community: Providing a supportive "home away from home" for incoming students as they navigate the Georgia Tech grind.

  • Celebration: Hosting vibrant cultural events like Nauryz, Kurban Ayt, and International Women’s Day.

  • Exchange: Sharing the rich traditions, flavors, and history of Central Asia with the broader Tech community.

Whether you’re looking for a seat at our table for a Plov dinner or just a place to relax with a cup of Chay, CASA is open to all.
summary: The goal of CASA is to create representation of students from Central Asian Countries (Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, and Turkmenistan).

name: Novel Engineering for Alternative Lift Aerospace
description: Neal Aerospace Instagram
summary: Neal Aerospace aims to research and develop small scale alternative propulsion devices.

name: Students Providing Aid
description: Our purpose is to unite the student body to help us prepare and provide first-aid kits to children in developing nations
summary: Our purpose is to unite the student body to help us prepare and provide first-aid kits to children in developing nations

name: ENgineering Emergency Medical Systems
description: ENEMS (Engineering and EMS) is an interdisciplinary club that brings together students from diverse backgrounds to tackle challenges in emergency medical services (EMS) through engineering innovation. By combining expertise from biomedical engineering, computer science, and other fields, members work collaboratively to brainstorm, design, and prototype technologies that improve emergency response and patient care. Through collaborations with EMS agencies, hospitals, and medical professionals, members can gain experience in medical device innovation while engaging in research, hands-on prototyping, and real-world testing.
summary: ENEMS (Engineering and EMS) is an interdisciplinary club that brings together students from diverse backgrounds to tackle challenges in emergency medical services (EMS) through engineering innovation.

name: Rise and Run
description: Rise and Run aims to provide an inclusive and supportive community of neurodiverse runners.
summary: Rise and Run aims to provide an inclusive and supportive community of neurodiverse runners.

name: Coffee Club @ GT
description: We want to create a community of coffee enthusiasts at Georgia Tech who learn, taste, and appreciate coffee together in a welcoming environment for all experience levels. Membership is open to all Georgia Tech students regardless of coffee knowledge or experience. Our focus is on creating an inclusive space for both coffee experts and curious beginners to learn together. Our goals are to build coffee appreciation skills among members, connect with the local coffee community, provide a relaxing social outlet for students, and create opportunities for hands-on learning.
summary: We want to create a community of coffee enthusiasts at Georgia Tech who learn, taste, and appreciate coffee together in a welcoming environment for all experience levels.

name: Black Student-Athletes Association at Georgia Tech
description: Being a black student-athlete at a predominantly white institution presents unique challenges, combining the demands of athletics with the experiences of being a minority on Georgia Tech’s campus. BSAA creates a dedicated space for black student-athletes to connect, share experiences, and find community. Many student-athletes struggle to engage with campus activities, events, and organizations due to their rigorous schedules. This organization seeks to bridge this gap by fostering a supportive network of black student-athletes while also creating opportunities for greater connection with the entire broader Georgia Tech community.
summary: This organization seeks to bridge this gap by fostering a supportive network of black student-athletes while also creating opportunities for greater connection with the entire broader Georgia Tech community.

name: The Neurodiversity Initiative at Georgia Tech
description: The purpose of the Neurodiversity Initiative is to: a. Volunteer with and provide resources for families of children with special needs in underserved populations in the Atlanta Community, specifically focusing on impoverished populations b. Provide a space for GT Neurodiverse students to engage with, connect, and network with each other c. Organize campus-wide and smaller, regular events that aim to improve the condition of those struggling families with children with special needs d. Fundraising
summary: The purpose of the Neurodiversity Initiative is to Volunteer with and provide resources for families of children with special needs in underserved populations in the Atlanta Community, specifically focusing on underserved populations

name: Hobby Club@GT
description:
Hobby Club's main goal is to provide students an avenue to try new hobbies with as few barriers to entry as is possible. Some hobbies may be inherently difficult for individual students to access for various reasons (cost, lack of expertise, time constraints, etc), and hobby club aims to lower these limits. Hobby club also aims to provide a safe space for members to confidently try at new activities with no worries about whether or not they know or are good at the activity. When possible, hobby club will also provide materials for members to independently continue hobbies at their own perogative or put members in contact with other groups (such as other campus clubs) that may specialize in their hobby of interest.

Join our Discord Server here:
https://discord.gg/4VRRXcAv9E
summary: Hobby Club's main goal is to provide students an avenue to try new hobbies with as few barriers to entry as is possible.

name: Epsilon Eta
description: Epsilon Eta is a gender-inclusive environmental fraternity dedicated to fostering a community of passionate environmental changemakers. Our mission is to raise environmental awareness and empower future leaders pursuing careers in sustainability, conservation, and related fields. Through hands-on initiatives, professional development, and a tight-knit network of like-minded individuals, we strive to make a tangible impact on the planet while supporting each other’s academic and career goals. Whether you’re an aspiring environmental scientist, policy advocate, engineer, or innovator, Epsilon Eta is the place to connect, grow, and take action for a greener future!
summary: Epsilon Eta is a gender-inclusive environmental fraternity dedicated to fostering a community of passionate environmental changemakers.

name: Little Smiles
description: We aim to educate elementary students about oral health through engaging, child-friendly activities in order to reduce future psychological barriers to dental healthcare.
summary: We aim to educate elementary students about oral health through engaging, child-friendly activities in order to reduce future psychological barriers to dental healthcare.

name: Agora
description: Agora seeks to glorify God as an ecumenical forum and journal of Christian thought. By creating and curating pieces that reflect the vibrant beauty of God’s nature, we offer a collective Christian perspective to the campus dialogue.
summary: Agora seeks to glorify God as an ecumenical forum and journal of Christian thought. By creating and curating pieces that reflect the vibrant beauty of God’s nature, we offer a collective Christian perspective to the campus dialogue.

name: Armwrestling @ GT
description: The purpose of the organization is to provide a place where people who are interested in armwrestling to practice and train with other such hobbyists. The organization will also teach technique and safety practices for newcomers into the sport.
summary: The purpose of the organization is to provide a place where people who are interested in armwrestling to practice and train with other such hobbyists. The organization will also teach technique and safety practices for newcomers into the sport.

name: Muslims in Healthcare
description: Muslims in Healthcare (MIH) is a professional network designed to empower Muslim students who aim to work in health-related careers. MIH will work to connect Muslim students to healthcare professionals, help members achieve their health-career aspirations through meetings and events, engage in community service events related to health-related issues, and hear from health professionals regarding Islam in the workspace. MIH strives to create a welcoming and inclusive environment where Muslim students can thrive and build their professional skills and their connection with Islam while balancing the load of a pre-healthcare college experience.
summary: Muslims in Healthcare (MIH) is a professional network designed to empower Muslim students who aim to work in health-related careers.

name: Public Policy Graduate Student Association at Georgia Tech
description:
PPGSA’s purpose is to:

- Connect the School of Public Policy (SPP) graduate student body population.

- Help represent the graduate student body’s diverse needs and desires to the administration.

- Help communicate administrative decisions to the graduate student body.

- Organize methods of connecting graduate students to possible career and research opportunities.

- Create and foster a positive, friendly, healthy, and safe social atmosphere for students.

name: Students Supporting Israel
description:
Students Supporting Israel (SSI) is a student-led organization dedicated to educating and empowering students of all backgrounds through meaningful conversations about Israeli history, culture, and current events.

SSI provides a welcoming space for learning, dialogue, and engagement by hosting educational programs, cultural events, speaker series, and interactive discussions. Our mission is to promote critical thinking, mutual understanding, and respectful discourse on campus.

We encourage students to ask questions, explore diverse perspectives, and engage thoughtfully with complex global issues, regardless of prior knowledge or background.
summary: The goal of SSI is to educate students of all backgrounds on Israeli history, culture, and current events.

name: NextStep
description:
The purpose of NextStep is to:

a. Reduce food insecurity in Atlanta through community engagement, food drives, and donations.

b. Educate the Georgia Tech community about systemic causes of hunger and food deserts.

c. Foster student leadership through volunteerism and partnerships with local organizations.
summary: Through several initiatives that are not limited to fundraising, hands on events, and more, we hope to make an impact as the premier student-led non-profit focused on alleviating food insecurity through student intiative.

name: Heart of Passion at Georgia Tech
description: Heart of Passion at Georgia Tech aims to provide emotional support for young adult cancer survivors by facilitating connections with peers and student volunteers. Through special events and engaging activities, the organization strives to develop a widespread emotional support network of peers, both with and without cancer, rooted in lasting memories and fun.
summary: Heart of Passion at Georgia Tech aims to provide emotional support for young adult cancer survivors by facilitating connections with peers and student volunteers.

name: Malayalees @ GT
description:
1.To unite students of Malayalee heritage

2. Foster an environment of cultural exchange

3. Celebrate the unique traditions of Kerala

4. We shall promote cultural awareness, provide a support system through social, educational, and cultural activities, and contribute positively to the GT’s diverse community
summary: Malayalees @ GT aims to unite students of Malayalee heritage.

name: PoliScope
description:
In an era of rapid policy change and polarization, students need spaces where ideas are examined critically, openly, and without partisan bias. PoliScope was established to fill this gap. PoliScope is Georgia Tech’s nonpartisan home for students who want to make sense of the various issues and developments shaping their lives, communities, and the broader public. Open to all majors.

 

Who We Are

We are a group of nonpartisan Georgia Tech students who critically analyze, evaluate, and debate various issues on the local, state, and federal level. 

 

What We Do

- Coordinate multi-stakeholder forums, town halls, and student roundtables with faculty and practitioners

- Decode campus, local, state, and national developments into evidence-based takeaways 

- Track elections, policy, and legislative agendas—and evaluate the impact on students and the broader public

- Develop nonpartisan issue guides, fact sheets, and policy explainers 

- Survey students and run input labs to pinpoint priorities for PoliScope's research agenda

 

 

Why It's Important

- In this polarizing political climate we find ourselves in, we believe it's important to bridge perspectives and create space for constructive dialogue across different backgrounds and viewpoints.

- From tuition to housing, we believe that having a deeper understanding of the things that affect us provides clarity, enhances decision-making, and establishes a foundation of truth.

 

How We Work

- Nonpartisan 

- Evidence-first

- Engage different perspectives

- Respectful debate

- Accessible

 

Poliscope provides opportunities for members to engage in open discourse about real-world issues, and develop well-supported conclusions. Through public forums, policy debates, research initiatives, and collaborative dialogue, the organization cultivates a culture of respectful engagement, critical thinking, and informed participation in the policy process.
summary: PoliScope aims to foster a well-informed and engaged student body by providing a platform for students to explore, discuss, and analyze policy issues.

name: Pathways for Research and Innovation in STEM
description: Pathways for Research and Innovation in STEM at Georgia Tech (PRISM@GT) is dedicated to providing high school students with a meaningful and accessible research experience. Through GT research labs, PRISM@GT enables students to engage in real-world research, develop critical thinking skills, and explore innovation in STEM. Whether contributing to ongoing projects or designing their research within a given framework, students gain mentorship, institutional resources, and hands-on experience in a university research setting. PRISM@GT aims to make high-level research more approachable for students who may not otherwise have access, empowering them to develop their skills and build a strong foundation for future academic and professional pursuits.
summary: Pathways for Research and Innovation in STEM at Georgia Tech (PRISM@GT) is dedicated to providing high school students with a meaningful and accessible research experience.

description: Article II. Article III. Goals The goals of this chapter shall include, but are not limited to: Fundraise through ACS-approved fundraisers (e.g., Relay For Life, Making Strides Against Breast Cancer). Conduct regular meetings with all members. Pursue, plan, and execute programs supporting ACS’s four focus areas: advocacy, discovery, patient support, and fundraising. Collaborate with ACS staff, surrounding chapters, and the ACS National Campus & Youth Engagement Team. Support the establishment of ACS chapters at surrounding schools.
summary: The American Cancer Society Chapter at Georgia Tech is a student-led organization dedicated to eliminating cancer by initiating and supporting ACS programs and activities within the Georgia Tech community.

name: The After Dark Project
description: a. Connect future healthcare workers and students across disciplines to the human side of medicine through reflection, empathy, and presence. b. Explore themes of grief, loss, suffering, and emotional depth — particularly in healthcare and service environments where these realities are often minimized. c. Provide spaces for intentional dialogue, narrative, and creative reflection that remind students of the responsibility and privilege of caring for others. d. Encourage students to understand medicine not only as a profession of technical skill but as a calling to compassion, integrity, and presence in times of vulnerability.
summary: We connect future healthcare workers and students across disciplines to the human side of medicine through reflection, empathy, and presence.

description:
The purpose of Rooted Christian Fellowship is to:

• Provide a safe, inclusive, and respectful environment where students from all backgrounds can explore the truths of Christianity and grow in their spiritual journey
