• Enhance the college experience by organizing study groups, shared meals, social gatherings, trips and retreats

Join our GroupMe for updates to stay up to date with our events, or check out our website!
summary: The purpose of Rooted Christian Fellowship is to: - Provide a welcoming space where students can hear from speakers, ask questions and engage in open discussions about Christianity and life.

name: Big Data Big Impact
description:
BDBI empowers students to transform data into meaningful impact. Through hands-on learning, collaborative projects, and a strong community, we help students develop practical machine learning and data science skills while applying them to real-world, socially focused challenges. Members engage with peers, mentors, and industry experts to grow their technical abilities, expand their network, and contribute to projects that drive social good.

We believe in a three-step process:

  • Learn
    Build practical skills through workshops, technical trainings, and educational events covering machine learning, data science, and software development.

  • Apply
    Work on real-world, high-impact projects involving areas such as predictive modeling, NLP, computer vision, and data analysis—creating solutions that benefit communities.

  • Network
    Connect with fellow students, project leads, alumni, and industry professionals to gain mentorship, explore opportunities, and grow your academic and career pathways.

Join here: tinyurl.com/bdbi-slack
summary: Empowering students to learn machine learning and data skills, apply them through impactful, real-world projects, and network with peers and professionals to create meaningful social good

name: Bioconnect @ Georgia Tech
description: The goal of BCGT is to foster and strengthen the biotechnology community within Georgia Tech and the greater Atlanta area by providing educational resources, networking opportunities, and awareness initiatives. In addition, we hope to contribute to the biotechnology landscape regionally and nationally through healthcare consulting initiatives and student-led projects. Lastly, students are also able to cultivate professional development by connecting students with industry leaders, academic researchers, and entrepreneurial opportunities in biotechnology
summary: We foster and strengthen the biotechnology community within Georgia Tech and the greater Atlanta area by providing educational resources, networking opportunities, and awareness initiatives.

name: American Preventative Screening and Education Association (APSEA) at Georgia Tech
description:
The purpose of APSEA is to:

a. To raise awareness about hypertension and metabolic syndrome through preventative workshops, medical tables, and educational programs offered throughout Georgia.

b. To educate Georgia Tech students about blood pressure measurements, blood glucose measurements, and the changing diagnostic criteria and prognostics of hypertension, Type II diabetes, and metabolic syndrome

c. To promote communication and interaction with the community, student organizations, and medical field by providing advice from medical and dental students.

d. To promote leadership and student involvement in the community.

e. To increase knowledge of common preventative and treatment practices for hypertension and metabolic syndrome.

f. Increase awareness of causes and symptoms of stroke (due to hypertension).

g. To understand the latest research and studies concerning Type II diabetes and hypertension protocols and diagnostics.
summary: APSEA raises awareness about hypertension, diabetes, and metabolic syndrome through education, community outreach, and leadership. We promote prevention, early detection, and student involvement in health initiatives across Georgia.

name: Georgia Youth Justice Coalition at Georgia Tech
description: To create a Georgia Tech branch of Georgia Youth Justice Coalition and build on the statewide mission by creating a political home for young people on campus. We aim to cultivate civic engagement through accessible, fun, and educational events, while increasing visibility around local and statewide elections. By empowering students with the tools and knowledge to take action, we seek to build a generation of informed and active young Georgians engaged in advocacy at our local and state levels.
summary: We aim to create a Georgia Tech branch of Georgia Youth Justice Coalition and build on the statewide mission by creating a political home for young people on campus.

name: LibreTech Collective
description: The purpose of LibreTech Collective at Georgia Institute of Technology is to: a) Promote, advocate for, and develop free and open-source: software, hardware, and knowledge through collaboration, education, and public engagement. b) Empower Georgia Tech students to contribute to and benefit from the global free and open technology movement. c) Provide hands-on learning through libre/open-source projects, contributions to upstream repositories, and participation in global events.
summary: We work to promote, advocate for, and develop free and open-source: software, hardware, and knowledge through collaboration, education, and public engagement.

name: 3D Audio Lab at GT
description: Our mission is to create a student community at Georgia Tech dedicated to the exploration and application of 3D printing in audio technology and wearable devices. Unlike general makerspaces, our focus is on hands-on training and specialized projects, such as using resin-based 3D printing techniques to design and manufacture custom-fit earphones, hearing devices, and other innovative audio products. The organization aims to empower students with practical skills, foster interdisciplinary collaboration between engineering, design, and health sciences, and provide opportunities to prototype, test, and share their own creations.
summary: Our mission is to create a student community at Georgia Tech dedicated to the exploration and application of 3D printing in audio technology and wearable devices.

name: Collegians for Christ
description:
Wecome! We are the Collegians for Christ, a Christian Organization on campus. Our mission is to reach students by sharing the Gospel of Jesus Christ, to teach them life-applicable truths from God’s Word, to connect these students to other believers and a local church in the area, and to train students to be leaders who impact their campus and community for its good. We want students to know what they believe, why they believe it, and where to find it in the Bible.

We will have monthly meetings at 7 pm on the last Monday of each month this semester. We will share a meal, enjoy fellowship, and study the Bible together. People from all backgrounds will feel welcome here. We look forward to connecting with you!
summary: Our mission is to reach students by sharing the Gospel of Jesus Christ, to teach them life-applicable truths from God’s Word, to connect these students to other believers and a local church in the area, and to train students to be leaders.

name: BioBridge @GT
description: In the greater Atlanta community, many under-resourced middle and high schools lack hands-on science labs and up-to-date technology, reducing students’ STEM skills. This challenge disproportionately affects low-income, rural, and minority youth, limiting future opportunities. Existing STEM clubs, free online resources, and science classes in school provide valuable glimpses into science but are inconsistent. Providing hands-on kits, developing interactive curricula, and offering mentorship will ensure that every student experiences high-quality science education and a chance to pursue a STEM career. We started an organization called BioBridge and are striving to build a team that can work towards our initiative. Our program delivers interactive science kits with step-by-step hands-on experiments, complemented by open-access, standards-aligned printable worksheets and engaging educational videos. Students can access materials online or via packets, ensuring flexible use in-class or at home. Unlike fragmented initiatives, our integrated platform unites physical labs, digital media, and curricula in one user-friendly hub. By offering free, adaptable resources tailored to local needs and supported by mentorship, we empower all learners to deepen STEM understanding regardless of their school’s funding or infrastructure
summary: In the greater Atlanta community, many under-resourced middle and high schools lack hands-on science labs and up-to-date technology, reducing students’ STEM skills.

name: Racquetball Club at Georgia Tech
description: The purpose of the Racquetball Club is to create a community of players, introduce interested members of the GT community to the sport, coach and train members who seek to improve their game, and foster friendly competition through organized tournaments.
summary: The purpose of the Racquetball Club is to create a community of player, introduce interested members of the GT community to the sport, coach and train members who seek to improve their game, and foster friendly competition through organized tournaments.

description: Develop leadership skills, promote civic awareness, strengthen civilian–military relationships, and foster professional growth by preparing members for future careers in both civilian and military fields. Our mission is a national, co-ed, professional organization dedicated to developing civilians’ leadership skills and giving back to their communities, while working closely with Arnold Air Society to improve civilian–military relations. - National Silver Wings Mission.
summary: We develop leadership skills, promote civic awareness, strengthen civilian–military relationships, and foster professional growth by preparing members for future careers in both civilian and military fields.

name: Health and Business Alliance
description: To bring together students with interests in both pre-health jobs as well as business. This could include those who want to own their own practice or those who want to manage a hospital. Also, we will provide some service opportunities for those who need help getting in more volunteer hours.
summary: To bring together students with interests in both pre-health jobs as well as business. This could include those who want to own their own practice or those who want to manage a hospital.

name: MedVenture
description: Our purpose is to explore the intersection of medicine, business, and innovation by empowering students to confidently pursue ventures that challenge the misconception that risk-taking has no place in healthcare-related fields. Through MedVenture, we aim to redefine what it means to pursue a career in medicine by providing students with the tools and flexibility to explore diverse opportunities while expanding their career options beyond traditional medical paths. Members will be encouraged to develop and manage a sustainable risk-to-reward framework tailored to their individual goals, while strengthening core medical skills such as critical thinking, clinical problem-solving, patient-centered communication, and an understanding of healthcare systems. At the same time, they will unearth essential business and innovation skills such as entrepreneurship, financial literacy, and the ability to navigate healthcare policy while staying within the legal boundaries of medicine. Ultimately, MedVenture serves as a platform that supports and propels students in uncovering new possibilities that may have previously been hidden from view while striving to foster close collaboration between the executive board, experienced mentors, and members to create a long-lasting environment of sustainable learning and growth.
summary: Our purpose is to explore the intersection of medicine, business, and innovation by empowering students to confidently pursue ventures that challenge the misconception that risk-taking has no place in healthcare-related fields.

name: Southeast Analog at Georgia Tech
description: Facilitate regionally based, convenient, low-cost analog astronautics simulation to a diverse field of students from all backgrounds in the Southeast United States for research and education initiatives uniquely suited to analog settings. Providing opportunities for members from all majors and skill sets to contribute to the SEA mission through many of SEA @GT multidisciplinary subteams, as well as working with interdisciplinary clubs at Georgia Tech to provide educational and professional development opportunities for members to enhance their technical and interdisciplinary skills. Will serve as the Georgia Tech chapter to a broader SEA initiative, with outreach to regional partners.
summary: Facilitate regionally based, convenient, low-cost analog astronautics simulation to a diverse field of students from all backgrounds in the Southeast United States for research and education initiatives uniquely suited to analog settings

name: Tamil Sangam at Georgia Tech
description: Tamil Sangam is a cultural and community organization established to preserve, promote, and celebrate the Tamil language, culture, heritage, and traditions. The organization seeks to bring together Tamil students and allies at Georgia Tech to create a strong cultural and social community.
summary: Tamil Sangam is a cultural and community organization established to preserve, promote, and celebrate the Tamil language, culture, heritage, and traditions.

name: Art4Smiles
description: Art4Smiles is a pre-health student organization at Georgia Tech which aims to spread positivity and provide oral health education to underserved communities. By partnering with Atlanta's own reduced cost dental clinics, we are combining our passion for arts and crafts through handmade projects, which will be donated to the clinics. Our members will design handmade arts and crafts projects and write personalized oral-hygiene notecards that are given to the patients after their appointments alongside essential dental hygiene tools to help maintain their oral microbiome (floss, toothbrushes, etc). Our “smile kits” will brighten patients’ days (and their teeth) while providing practical oral health guidance. Through fundraising efforts throughout the year, we aim to help restore smiles and improve oral hygiene.
summary: Art4Smiles is a pre-health student organization at Georgia Tech which aims to spread positivity and provide oral health education to underserved communities.

The organization aims to provide both social and academic support, promote visibility within the Scheller community, and serve as a platform for outreach and student advocacy. summary: The purpose of Scheller Pride is to foster a supportive, inclusive, and connected community for LGBTQIA+ graduate students and graduate student allies at the Scheller College of Business.

In this sense, regenerative work relates to actions that help restore or bring new life to the local environment or community (e.g. trail restoration, home repair/construction volunteering, community gardening, etc.). In collaboration with the Regeneration Field Institute, the Regeneration Club at GT aims to promote study abroad opportunities in Ecuador.
summary: The Regeneration Club at GT aims to engage students and faculty in socially and ecologically regenerative work at Georgia Tech and in the greater Atlanta region through hands-on projects and learning experiences.

The purpose of the ABC is to:

a. Discuss the wasteful and self-destructive nature of scrolling/using one’s phone or other device in an unhealthy length of time or manner.

b. Provide a space to explore other activities that can reduce this behavior, such as team sports, arts and crafts events, and other activities that will help people socialize in person and get active.

c. Create a networking space for students and faculty who want to meet like-minded individuals that care about these issues.

d. Petition for improvements that the university can make to help reduce “brainrot” or other similar things that detract from people’s quality of life.

The organization seeks to strengthen faith, promote fellowship among Orthodox Christian students, and encourage active participation in service, prayer, and educational activities that reflect the values of the Church within the university community.
summary: The purpose of the EOTC-Mahibere Kidusan Campus Ministry at Georgia Tech is to provide students with the opportunity to grow spiritually and intellectually through the teachings and traditions of the Ethiopian Orthodox Tewahedo Church.

summary: The purpose of the Medical Literacy Initiative @ GT is to improve medical literacy by rephrasing information from medical papers at a high-school reading level and to give students an opportunity to practice researching and writing.

name: Scholars of Finance
description: Scholars of Finance is a finance based volunteering and professional development club with a mission to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow. Our vision is a future where all finance leaders steward the world's capital to serve the greater good. In order to accomplish this our org will be hosting volunteering projects and programs focused on helping students define their values and morals and apply them to a career in finance.
summary: Scholars of Finance is a finance based volunteering and professional development club with a mission to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow.

Through academic forums, intercultural and interfaith exchanges, leadership development, and engagement with the UN Sustainable Development Goals, the Society cultivates informed, responsible, and visionary changemakers dedicated to building a more interconnected world.
summary: The Global Future Leaders Society empowers students to become globally minded leaders who champion dialogue, foster understanding, and drive meaningful change.

summary: The purpose of Grad Latin Club is to: 1) Develop, empower, and advance Latin business leaders and share the Latin culture with the Scheller MBA community 2) Offer networking opportunities for club members 3) Host talks and information sessions

name: SPARK Together
description: The SPARK Together student organization seeks to build a vibrant community of students committed to compassionate leadership, personal growth, and meaningful service. Guided by our core values of Service, Purpose, Action, Reflection, and Kindness, we aim to ignite a passion for positive change through hands-on initiatives, open dialogue, and collaborative projects. We believe that true leadership begins with empathy and reflection. Through volunteer activities, community service projects, mentorship programs, and campus engagement, SPARK Together empowers students to connect their academic and personal goals with purposeful action. Our mission is to nurture individuals who not only strive for excellence but also uplift others, creating a culture of belonging, understanding, and shared responsibility both on campus and in the wider community.
summary: The SPARK Together student organization seeks to build a vibrant community of students committed to compassionate leadership, personal growth, and meaningful service.

name: VAW Global Health Alliances at Georgia Tech
description: VAW Global is an international health and development organization dedicated to building sustainable, equitable partnerships with local communities and professionals. We focus on strengthening local capacity and supporting essential, accessible healthcare services. Working closely with community leaders and initiatives, we aim to improve access to medical, dental, and veterinary care. In addition, we support efforts to provide medications, nutritional support, clean water, and health education. Our Medical, Dental, Veterinary, and Global Health teams travel to various countries each year to collaborate directly with communities in need.
summary: VAW Global is an international health and development organization dedicated to building sustainable, equitable partnerships with local communities and professionals.

name: Project Hikmah
description: Project Hikmah is a student-led journalism and media initiative at Georgia Tech. We empower students to research, create, and share stories with clarity and confidence, covering topics from medicine and physics to human rights through an Islamic-informed lens. We combine storytelling, public speaking, debates, and community engagement to amplify voices, correct misconceptions, and represent Islam with dignity.
summary: Project Hikmah is a student-led journalism club at Georgia Tech. We empower students to research, create, and share stories with clarity and confidence, covering topics from medicine and physics to human rights through an Islamic-informed lens.

name: Disabilities in Business
description: Disabilities in Business (DIB) exists to foster awareness, inclusion, and empowerment for people with disabilities and allies within the business community. Our goal is to highlight disability as a dimension of diversity that drives innovation, empathy, and better business outcomes. Through open dialogue, education, and collaboration with corporate partners and campus organizations, DIB aims to create a culture where every individual—regardless of ability—feels valued, supported, and seen as an essential contributor to business success.
summary: Disabilities in Business (DIB) exists to foster awareness, inclusion, and empowerment for people with disabilities and allies within the business community.

name: CEE Graduate Student Advisory Council
description: The purpose of GSAC is to facilitate community building and to serve as a liaison between the School of Civil and Environmental Engineering (CEE) and its graduate students. Members of this Council represent each of the School’s six Academic Groups. The council hosts a variety of events that provide both academic and social support, enhance the graduate student experience and bring CEE graduate students together! ​Additionally, council members are ambassadors to the School’s External Advisory Board, alumni, supporters, and prospective students. Council members are available to answer questions, field suggestions, and offer insight and advice.
summary: The purpose of CEE GSAC is to facilitate community building and to serve as a liaison between the School of Civil and Environmental Engineering (CEE) and its graduate students.

name: Cosmetic Chemists Society
description: The purpose of this organization is to build a supportive community where students interested in cosmetic chemistry can connect, share ideas, and develop skills through collaborative learning and exploration.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to build a supportive community where students interested in cosmetic chemistry can connect, share ideas, and develop skills through collaborative learning and exploration.

name: ENGin at Georgia Tech
description: ENGin at Georgia Tech is a chapter of the ENGin program, a nonprofit organization advancing social and economic growth in Ukraine. The program utilizes the power of conversation to connect English speakers and Ukrainians to build English fluency and foster cultural exchange. Through weekly virtual sessions, our volunteers serve as English conversation partners and mentors for Ukrainian students registered through the nonprofit program ENGin.
summary: ENGin at Georgia Tech is a chapter of the ENGin program, a nonprofit organization advancing social and economic growth in Ukraine. The program pairs English-speaking volunteers with Ukrainians students interested in learning and practicing English.

name: Etch-A-Tech
description: We are a casual sketching club where we host workshops on media and style explorations.
summary: We are a casual sketching club where we host workshops on media and style explorations.

name: Pre-Optometry at Tech
description: The preoptometry club at Georgia tech serves to bring together students interested in pursuing optometry as well as foster more interest in the field. Members of the club will share application advice and OAT advice, learn about what it means to apply to optometry school, and participate in off site activities (such as volunteering or SECO), among other things.
summary: The preoptometry club at Georgia tech serves to bring together students interested in pursuing optometry as well as foster more interest in the field.

name: Christian Medical and Dental Association at Georgia Tech
description: To provide service opportunities, inspiration, and a tight knit community for pre-medical and pre-dental students who find inspiration in their Christian faith.
summary: To provide service opportunities, inspiration, and a tight knit community for pre-medical and pre-dental students who find inspiration in their Christian faith.

name: Dementia Art Therapy Alliance @ GT
description: DATA (Dementia Art Therapy Alliance) is a Georgia Tech student organization that partners with local memory care facilities. Our goal is to bring creativity and compassion to residents living with dementia and other neurodegenerative conditions through art-based activities. During each visit, small groups of students spend time with residents doing fun and engaging arts and crafts projects that encourage self-expression and connection. Through these visits, we aim to promote awareness about dementia while giving students a meaningful way to serve their community.
summary: DATA is a nonprofit organization that works with memory care facilities.

name: Women’s Club Ice Hockey Team at Georgia Tech
description: The Women's Club Ice Hockey Team at Georgia Tech is open to all students who are interested in hockey, whether they are beginners or have played at a competitive level. We aim to build inclusive community through off/on ice training and games with other colleges, while supporting skill deelopment and team growth.
summary: WHockey aims to give women at Georgia Tech a dedicated space to play ice hockey.

name: Stand-Up Club at Georgia Tech
description: We provide a venue for students interested in stand-up comedy to write, share, perform, and review comedic material at Georgia Tech.
summary: We provide a venue for students interested in stand-up comedy to write, share, perform, and review comedic material at Georgia Tech.

name: The Helping Hive @ GT
description: The Helping Hive is a student-led organization at Georgia Tech dedicated to providing free, one-on-one virtual academic support to K–12 students from underserved, immigrant, and refugee backgrounds. Our mission is to bridge educational gaps by connecting Georgia Tech student volunteers with youth in need of consistent, personalized tutoring across all academic subjects. We aim to foster long-term mentorship, promote educational equity, and build meaningful partnerships with local community organizations serving refugee and immigrant families.
summary: The Helping Hive is a student-led organization at Georgia Tech dedicated to providing free, one-on-one virtual academic support to K-12 students from underserved, immigrant, and refugee backgrounds.

name: SEWB Celebration and Community Building Committee
description: The purpose and mission of the Celebration & Community Building Committee is to help advance the Institute's strategic focus on Cultivate Well-Being for faculty and staff, particularly the specific goal of enhancing community and connection.
summary: SEWB Celebration and Community Building Committee

organizationType.name: Division of Student Engagement and Well-being

name: Brook Byers Institute for Sustainable Systems
description: BBISS is one of Georgia Tech's Interdisciplinary Research Institutes (IRIs) that serves as a synthesizer and catalyzer for sustainability research. We also strongly advocate for community-engaged research projects, many of which involve students. We coordinate and cooperate with other campus organizations that focus on sustainability, including those that advance sustainability in the curriculum and campus operations. BBISS also serves as a resource for sustainability-focused student organizations. We hold events and programming that may be of interest to students. BBISS also sponsors the Georgia Tech Permaculture Interest Group in conjunction with Students Organizing for Sustainability.
summary: Brook Byers Institute for Sustainable Systems

name: TechMade
description: TechMade is a transdisciplinary experiential learning initiative dedicated to creating cross-disciplinary problem solvers by powering spaces, strengthening community, and advancing curriculum that fosters human-centered design, digital engineering, and making and manufacturing.
summary: TechMade

name: Voices of Equity
description: We aim to to educate, inspire, and empower communities and future healthcare leaders on health equity issues through impactful content, storytelling, and actionable community-based initiatives.
summary: To educate, inspire, and empower communities and future healthcare leaders on health equity issues through impactful content, storytelling, and actionable community-based initiatives.

name: Yellow Jackets Padel Club
description: The purpose of the Padel Club is to introduce, promote, and grow the sport of padel within the Georgia Tech community by providing students with accessible opportunities to learn, play, and compete. The club aims to build a welcoming, inclusive community around an emerging global sport while fostering athletic development, sportsmanship, and social connection through regular play, events, and partnerships with local padel facilities.
summary: The purpose of the Padel Club is to introduce, promote, and grow the sport of padel within the Georgia Tech community by providing students with accessible opportunities to learn, play, and compete.

name: Master's in Biomedical Innovation and Development Connect
description: The purpose of MBID Connect is to do the following for members of this organization: - Create and support opportunities that enhance the professional growth of students within the organization - Build a strong sense of community by promoting collaboration and active engagement among students - Foster outreach opportunities with other universities to welcome students from a variety of backgrounds and promote inclusivity within the biomedical engineering program
summary: The purpose of MBID Connect is to create and support opportunities that enhance the professional growth of students within the organization.

name: ECE Graduate Student Organization
description: The purpose of the ECE Graduate Student Organization (GSO) is to serve as the primary liaison between ECE faculty and graduate students while building a stronger, more connected graduate community within the School of Electrical and Computer Engineering. We aim to increase research visibility, provide academic and professional support, and create clear pathways to internships and careers through seminars, mentorship, and industry partnerships.`
summary: The purpose of the ECE Graduate Student Organization (GSO) is to serve as the primary liaison between ECE faculty and graduate students while building a stronger, more connected graduate community within the School of Electrical and Computer Engineering.

name: Substance Safety Initiative
description: Educate Georgia Tech students and staff about drug addiction and its effects on the addict and people around them. Allow students the opportunity to connect/volunteer with Atlanta organizations aimed at preventing drug addiction.
summary: We aim to educate Georgia Tech students and staff about drug addiction and its effects on the addict and people around them.
